title: Regulating Genetic Research and Cloning
prompt: Some people think that genetic research and cloning should be strictly regulated or banned. Others believe that science should be free to explore and innovate. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
guideHtml:
Regulating Genetic Research and Cloning

Task type: IELTS Writing Task 2 · discussion

How to approach this question

Present both views fairly before giving your own opinion. Keep one main idea in each body paragraph and compare the consequences.

Suggested outline

  1. Introduction: introduce the debate and give your overall view.
  2. Body 1: explain why the first group supports this approach.
  3. Body 2: explain the opposing view and evaluate it.
  4. Conclusion: weigh both sides and confirm your position.

Useful language

  • On the one hand, ...
  • On the other hand, ...
  • A further consideration is that...
  • On balance, I believe...

Thesis direction

Although both perspectives are understandable, I believe a balanced approach is the most effective solution.

Model answer

The question of regulating genetic research and cloning has attracted considerable public attention. Some people argue that current policies and individual choices should remain unchanged, whereas others believe that a different approach is needed. In my view, the most convincing position is one that recognises the practical benefits of change while controlling its possible costs.

The first important consideration is the effect on individuals and communities. A well-designed approach can improve access, increase awareness and encourage people to make more informed decisions. For example, when institutions provide clear information and affordable alternatives, people are more likely to adopt responsible behaviour rather than simply follow short-term convenience. This can create benefits that extend beyond the original issue.

Nevertheless, the opposing concerns should not be dismissed. Rapid or poorly regulated change may create inequality, financial pressure or unintended consequences for groups with fewer resources. This is why governments, schools and employers should set realistic standards, provide support during the transition and review the results regularly. Such measures can preserve the main benefits while preventing the most serious disadvantages.

Overall, regulating genetic research and cloning should be approached through balanced and evidence-based decisions. Although no single policy will satisfy everyone, practical safeguards and long-term planning can make this development more beneficial for society as a whole.

title: Affordability of Medical Treatments
prompt: Many medical treatments are now available to people that are not affordable for all. Some think this is an inequality that governments should address. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
guideHtml:
Affordability of Medical Treatments

Task type: IELTS Writing Task 2 · opinion

How to approach this question

Take a clear position and support it with two specific reasons. A brief concession to the opposite view makes the argument more balanced.

Suggested outline

  1. Introduction: paraphrase the issue and state a direct opinion.
  2. Body 1: explain the strongest reason with a practical example.
  3. Body 2: develop a second reason and acknowledge one limitation.
  4. Conclusion: restate the position and summarise the two reasons.

Useful language

  • There is a strong case for...
  • The main reason is that...
  • This is particularly important because...
  • Although this view has some merit, ...

Thesis direction

I largely agree with this position because it can produce lasting social benefits and encourage more responsible decisions.

Model answer

title: Popularity of Extreme Sports
prompt: Extreme sports such as skydiving and car racing have become increasingly popular. What are the reasons for this? What can be done to make these sports safer?
guideHtml:
Popularity of Extreme Sports

Task type: IELTS Writing Task 2 · problem_solution

How to approach this question

Explain the main causes or problems first, then propose realistic solutions. Link each solution directly to a problem.

Suggested outline

  1. Introduction: paraphrase the issue and preview causes and solutions.
  2. Body 1: explain two important causes or effects.
  3. Body 2: present two practical solutions and their likely results.
  4. Conclusion: summarise the problem and recommend coordinated action.

Useful language

  • One major contributing factor is...
  • This problem is aggravated by...
  • A practical response would be to...
  • This would enable...

Thesis direction

This issue is caused by several connected factors, but targeted education, effective regulation and community action can reduce its impact.

Model answer

The question of popularity of extreme sports has attracted considerable public attention. Some people argue that current policies and individual choices should remain unchanged, whereas others believe that a different approach is needed. In my view, the most convincing position is one that recognises the practical benefits of change while controlling its possible costs.

The first important consideration is the effect on individuals and communities. A well-designed approach can improve access, increase awareness and encourage people to make more informed decisions. Nevertheless, the opposing concerns should not be dismissed. Rapid or poorly regulated change may create inequality, financial pressure or unintended consequences for groups with fewer resources. This is why governments, schools and employers should set realistic standards, provide support during the transition and review the results regularly. Such measures can preserve the main benefits while preventing the most serious disadvantages.

Overall, popularity of extreme sports should be approached through balanced and evidence-based decisions. Although no single policy will satisfy everyone, practical safeguards and long-term planning can make this development more beneficial for society as a whole.

Cover both benefits and drawbacks, then answer whether the advantages outweigh the disadvantages when the question asks for a judgement.

Suggested outline

  1. Introduction: introduce the trend and state your judgement.
  2. Body 1: explain the most significant advantages.
  3. Body 2: explain the main disadvantages and weigh them against the benefits.
  4. Conclusion: give a clear final evaluation.

Useful language

  • The most obvious advantage is...
  • This can be offset by...
  • A less desirable consequence is...
  • Overall, the benefits outweigh the costs because...

Thesis direction

While this development creates some genuine difficulties, its practical benefits are greater when it is managed responsibly.

Model answer

The question of living away from family at university has attracted considerable public attention. Some people argue that current policies and individual choices should remain unchanged, whereas others believe that a different approach is needed. In my view, the most convincing position is one that recognises the practical benefits of change while controlling its possible costs.

The first important consideration is the effect on individuals and communities. A well-designed approach can improve access, increase awareness and encourage people to make more informed decisions. For example, when institutions provide clear information and affordable alternatives, people are more likely to adopt responsible behaviour rather than simply follow short-term convenience. This can create benefits that extend beyond the original issue.

Nevertheless, the opposing concerns should not be dismissed. Rapid or poorly regulated change may create inequality, financial pressure or unintended consequences for groups with fewer resources. This is why governments, schools and employers should set realistic standards, provide support during the transition and review the results regularly. Such measures can preserve the main benefits while preventing the most serious disadvantages.

Overall, living away from family at university should be approached through balanced and evidence-based decisions. Although no single policy will satisfy everyone, practical safeguards and long-term planning can make this development more beneficial for society as a whole.

Suggested outline

  3. Body 2: develop a second reason and acknowledge one limitation.
  4. Conclusion: restate the position and summarise the two reasons.

Useful language

  • There is a strong case for...
  • The main reason is that...
  • This is particularly important because...
  • Although this view has some merit, ...

Thesis direction

I largely agree with this position because it can produce lasting social benefits and encourage more responsible decisions.

Model answer

The question of future of museums and art galleries has attracted considerable public attention. Some people argue that current policies and individual choices should remain unchanged, whereas others believe that a different approach is needed. In my view, the most convincing position is one that recognises the practical benefits of change while controlling its possible costs.

The first important consideration is the effect on individuals and communities. A well-designed approach can improve access, increase awareness and encourage people to make more informed decisions. For example, when institutions provide clear information and affordable alternatives, people are more likely to adopt responsible behaviour rather than simply follow short-term convenience. This can create benefits that extend beyond the original issue.

Nevertheless, the opposing concerns should not be dismissed. Rapid or poorly regulated change may create inequality, financial pressure or unintended consequences for groups with fewer resources. This is why governments, schools and employers should set realistic standards, provide support during the transition and review the results regularly. Such measures can preserve the main benefits while preventing the most serious disadvantages.

Overall, future of museums and art galleries should be approached through balanced and evidence-based decisions. Although no single policy will satisfy everyone, practical safeguards and long-term planning can make this development more beneficial for society as a whole.

title: Pie Chart: Percentage of volunteers by organisation type (2008 and 2014)
prompt: The charts below show the percentage of volunteers by organizations in 2008 and 2014. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Volunteers by Sector (2008 vs 2014)
1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ người tình nguyện trong sáu lĩnh vực (Environmental, Art, Sport, Health Care, Educational, Others) vào năm 2008 và 2014. → Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: participation / different organisations / two years. Mẫu: The pie charts illustrate the proportions of volunteers working in six different sectors in 2008 and 2014.

3. Overview (ý chính)
  • Environmental và Sport tăng đáng kể, trở thành hai nhóm lớn nhất vào 2014
  • Educational giảm rõ rệt
  • Art và Others giảm nhẹ
  • Health Care tăng nhẹ, gần như ổn định
4. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Nhóm tăng
  • Environmental: 21% → 29% → tăng mạnh nhất, trở thành lớn nhất
  • Sport: 15% → 25% → tăng đáng kể
  • Health Care: 7% → 8% → tăng nhẹ
BODY 2 – Nhóm giảm
  • Educational: 24% → 17% → giảm rõ rệt (lớn nhất 2008 → không còn dẫn đầu)
  • Art: 18% → 12% → giảm
  • Others: 15% → 9% → giảm
5. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • account for / make up / increase significantly / decline noticeably / rise steadily / remain relatively stable / overtake

The two pie charts compare the proportions of volunteers working in six different sectors in 2008 and 2014.

Overall, environmental and sports-related volunteering increased over the period, while participation in educational activities, art, and "other" categories declined. The most notable change was the rise of environmental work, which became the largest sector by 2014.

In 2008, educational volunteering accounted for the largest share at 24%, followed by environmental work at 21% and art at 18%. Sport and "other" activities each represented 15%, while health care was the least common sector at just 7%.

By 2014, environmental volunteering had grown significantly to 29%, overtaking all other categories. Sport also increased markedly to 25%, becoming the second most popular sector. In contrast, art saw a noticeable decline to 12%, and educational activities fell to 17%. Health care rose slightly from 7% to 8%, whereas the proportion of volunteers in other activities dropped considerably to 9%.

title: Mixed Graph: Average hours worked per week and stress-related illness across eight occupations
prompt: The charts show the average number of hours worked per week and the proportions of people suffering from stress-related illness across eight different occupations. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Working Hours & Stress-Related Illness by Occupation
1. Dạng bài

Mixed charts: Bar chart + Pie chart. Nội dung: working hours + stress levels by occupation.

2. Cách viết Overview
  • Giờ làm việc: chênh lệch lớn giữa các nghề — Business men làm nhiều nhất, Lecturers ít nhất
  • Stress: Lawyers cao nhất, Programmers thấp nhất
  • Không phải nghề làm nhiều giờ nhất sẽ có stress cao nhất
3. Cách chia Body
BODY 1 – Bar chart (Working hours) – theo ranking
  • Cao nhất: business men (~70h)
  • Tiếp theo: movie producers, doctors
  • Trung bình: writers, programmers, lawyers
  • Thấp nhất: chefs, lecturers (~25h)
BODY 2 – Pie chart (Stress levels) – theo tỷ lệ
  • Cao nhất: lawyers (25%)
  • Tiếp theo: movie producers (18%), doctors (15%)
  • Trung bình: business men, chefs
  • Thấp nhất: programmers (5%)
  • → stress không hoàn toàn phụ thuộc vào số giờ làm
4. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • rank first / account for / the highest / the lowest / considerably higher than / moderate level / in contrast

The charts compare the average weekly working hours of eight professions and the proportion of stress-related illness among people in these occupations.

Overall, business men and movie producers worked the longest hours, while lecturers and chefs had the shortest working weeks. In terms of stress, lawyers accounted for the largest share, whereas programmers experienced the lowest levels.

Business men worked the most, averaging 70 hours per week, followed by movie producers at 60 hours. Doctors also had relatively long working weeks at 52 hours, while writers worked 45 hours and programmers 40. Lawyers averaged 35 hours, and the figures were lower for chefs and lecturers, at 30 and 25 hours respectively.

Regarding stress-related illness, lawyers represented the highest proportion at 25%, despite having shorter working hours than several other professions. Movie producers followed at 18%, while doctors accounted for 15%. Business men made up 11% of stress cases, and chefs 10%. Writers and lawyers each contributed 8%, whereas programmers had the lowest figure at 5%.

title: Map: Layout changes of a small theatre (2010 vs 2012)
prompt: The plans below show a small theatre in 2010, and the same theatre in 2012. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

A Small Theatre (2010 vs 2012)
1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi trong bố trí của một nhà hát nhỏ giữa năm 2010 và 2012. → Dạng: map – changes over time

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: small theatre – layout – 2010 and 2012. Dùng: shows → illustrates / compares / changes → modifications / redevelopment

3. Overview (ý chính)
  • Nhà hát được tái cấu trúc để phục vụ tốt hơn
  • Khu hậu trường được nâng cấp (thêm showers, coordination room)
  • Khu phía trước chuyển từ cafe → restaurant
  • Stage và auditorium: giữ nguyên
4. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu phía trên & trung tâm
  • Stage + auditorium: không thay đổi
  • Dressing room: thu nhỏ và chuyển sang bên trái
  • Storage (bên trái) → chuyển xuống dưới
  • Thêm: Showers (bên trái) / Coordination room / Admin office (góc trên phải)
BODY 2 – Khu phía dưới
  • Cafe → được thay bằng restaurant lớn hơn
  • Ticket office: chuyển sang bên phải
  • Admin office (bên trái dưới) → bị loại bỏ
  • Storage mới: xuất hiện ở góc dưới bên trái
  • Main entrance: giữ nguyên
5. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be converted into / be relocated / be replaced by / be removed / be introduced / be reorganised

The diagrams compare the layout of a small theatre in 2010 and after redevelopment in 2012.

Overall, the theatre was upgraded with additional facilities and a more organised layout. While the stage and auditorium remained largely unchanged, several supporting areas were relocated or replaced to improve functionality.

In 2010, the theatre had a simple arrangement. A dressing room was located above the stage, with a storage area to the left. The auditorium occupied the central lower section. In the bottom-left corner, there was an admin office above a ticket office, while a café was positioned in the bottom-right area near the main entrance.

By 2012, notable changes had been made. The dressing room was moved to the upper-left side and reduced in size, and showers were added directly below it. The former dressing room area was converted into a coordination space, and a new admin office was relocated to the top-right corner. At the lower level, the café was replaced by a restaurant, and the ticket office was moved to the bottom-right corner. A new storage area was introduced on the lower-left side.

title: Map: Development of Porth Harbour (2000 vs today)
prompt: The plans below show a harbour in 2000 and how it looks today. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Porth Harbour (2000 vs Today)
1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi của một cảng biển từ năm 2000 đến hiện tại. → Dạng: map – changes over time

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: Porth Harbour – 2000 – today. Dùng: shows → illustrates / compares / changes → developments / transformations

3. Overview (ý chính)
  • Cảng được phát triển mạnh về du lịch và dịch vụ
  • Số lượng dock tăng lên
  • Xuất hiện các tiện ích mới như cafés, shops và hotel
  • Một số khu vực được tái bố trí → tổng thể hiện đại và hướng tới khách du lịch hơn
4. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu trung tâm (bến cảng)
  • 2000: Có 2 dock / Passenger ferries / Lifeboat gần bờ
  • Hiện tại: Số dock tăng lên 3 / Passenger ferries và Lifeboat vẫn giữ vị trí tương tự
BODY 2 – Khu bên trái
  • 2000: Marina / Fishing boats / 2 car parks / showers & toilets
  • Hiện tại: Fishing boats và marina đổi vị trí / Thêm showers & toilets mới / Cafés & shops mới
BODY 3 – Khu bên phải
  • 2000: Public beach / Castle (disused) / con đường đơn giản
  • Hiện tại: Castle → hotel / Road mở rộng dẫn tới hotel / Public beach được giữ
5. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be expanded / be replaced by / be relocated / be upgraded / tourist facilities / waterfront

The maps illustrate changes to Porth Harbour between 2000 and today.

Overall, the harbour has been developed for tourism, with more docks, new commercial facilities, and a hotel, while some original features have been relocated or expanded.

In 2000, the western side contained a marina for private yachts, fishing boats, and two car parks with nearby showers and toilets. A single dock extended into the harbour for passenger ferries. To the south, a lifeboat station was located along a curved road, and a disused castle stood on the eastern side near a public beach.

Today, the harbour has been significantly upgraded. The number of docks has increased to three. Cafés and shops have been added near the marina, which has been moved slightly south, while fishing boats are now positioned further north. Additional showers and toilets have been introduced on the western side. The disused castle has been replaced by a hotel, and the road has been extended to provide direct access to it. The lifeboat remains in a similar location, and the public beach is still present.

title: Bar Chart: Number of people in Europe affected by four types of noise (cities vs rural, day vs night, 2007)
prompt: The charts below show the number of people in Europe who were affected by four types of noise by day and by night in cities and rural areas in 2007. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Noise Pollution (City vs Rural, Day vs Night)
1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy số người (triệu người) bị ảnh hưởng bởi 4 nguồn tiếng ồn: Traffic, Train, Aircraft, Industry — ở city và rural, vào ban ngày và ban đêm. Có 3 layer: location (city vs rural) / time (day vs night) / source (4 loại) → chỉ chọn ý nổi bật.

2. Cách viết Overview
  • Traffic là nguồn lớn nhất trong mọi trường hợp
  • City luôn cao hơn rural
  • Day luôn cao hơn night
  • Industry gần như không đáng kể, đặc biệt ở rural
3. Cách chia Body
BODY 1 – City
  • Traffic: 64 → 48 (giảm nhưng vẫn cao nhất)
  • Train: 10 → 8
  • Aircraft: 4 → 1
  • Industry: 1 → 0.25
BODY 2 – Rural
  • Traffic: 34 → 24
  • Train: 8 → 6
  • Aircraft: 2 → 1
  • Industry: 0 → 0 (không đáng kể)
4. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be affected by noise / dominant source / negligible / minimal
  • figures in urban areas were consistently higher than those in rural areas

The charts compare the number of people affected by four types of noise pollution during the day and at night in urban and rural areas, measured in millions.

Overall, traffic was by far the dominant source of noise in both settings, while industrial noise affected the fewest people. In all cases, more individuals were impacted during the day than at night, and urban figures were consistently higher than those in rural areas.

In cities, traffic noise affected 64 million people during the day, decreasing to 48 million at night. Train noise was the second most significant source, impacting 10 million people by day and 8 million at night. Aircraft noise dropped sharply from 4 million to 1 million, while industrial noise was minimal, at just 1 million during the day and 0.25 million at night.

In rural areas, the same pattern is evident but at lower levels. Traffic affected 34 million people during the day and 24 million at night. Train noise declined from 8 to 6 million, and aircraft noise from 2 to 1 million. Industrial noise had no impact in rural areas at either time.

title: Bar Chart: Percentage of households with various goods (2001 and 2008)
prompt: The graph shows the percentage of households in one country that had various goods between 2001 and 2008. Summarize the information by describing the main features of the charts and making comparisons where appropriate.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Household Goods Ownership (2001–2008)
1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ thể hiện tỷ lệ hộ gia đình sở hữu 8 loại đồ dùng trong 2 năm: 2001 và 2008 — so sánh theo thời gian và giữa các nhóm thiết bị.

2. Cách viết Overview
  • Hầu hết các thiết bị đều tăng theo thời gian
  • Telephone là thiết bị duy nhất giảm nhẹ
  • Các thiết bị có tỷ lệ thấp ban đầu (mobile, internet, dishwasher) tăng mạnh nhất
3. Cách chia Body
BODY 1 – Nhóm sở hữu cao
  • 2001: telephone cao nhất (95%) / microwave (80%) / CD player (70%) / clothes dryer (60%)
  • 2008: microwave & CD player cùng ~90% / clothes dryer lên 70% / telephone giảm nhẹ xuống 90%
BODY 2 – Nhóm sở hữu thấp (tăng mạnh nhất)
  • 2001: computer (40%) / mobile (20%) / internet & dishwasher (10%)
  • 2008: mobile & computer tăng mạnh lên ~80% / internet lên 60% / dishwasher lên 40%
4. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • the proportion of households that owned / rose sharply / increased significantly / declined slightly

The bar chart compares the percentage of households owning eight different items in 2001 and 2008.

Overall, ownership increased across most categories, particularly for newer technologies such as mobile phones, computers, and internet access. In contrast, the proportion of households with telephones declined slightly over the period.

In 2001, traditional items were the most common. Telephones had the highest ownership at 95%, followed by microwaves (80%) and CD players (70%). Clothes dryers were owned by 60% of households. By comparison, newer technologies were far less widespread, with only 20% owning a mobile phone, 40% a home computer, and just 10% having internet access or a dishwasher.

By 2008, ownership levels had risen markedly for most items. Mobile phone ownership surged to 80%, matching the figure for home computers, while internet access increased sharply to 60%. Microwaves and CD players both reached 90%. Clothes dryers rose moderately to 70%, and dishwashers increased to 40%. The only decline was seen in telephones, which fell slightly from 95% to 90%.

title: Bar Chart: Money given to developing countries from five organisations (2008–2011)
prompt: The chart below shows the amount of money given to developing countries from five organizations from 2008 to 2011. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Investment by Organisations (2008–2011)
1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy số tiền đầu tư (billion dollars) của 5 tổ chức qua 4 năm: 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011 → so sánh giữa các nhóm + xu hướng theo thời gian.

2. Cách viết Overview
  • Organisation 2 luôn đầu tư nhiều nhất và tăng mạnh
  • Organisation 4 luôn thấp nhất
  • Organisation 5 giảm rõ rệt theo thời gian
3. Cách chia Body
BODY 1 – Organisation 2 (tăng mạnh nhất)
  • 1.3 → 1.5 → 2.3 → 2.6 tỷ USD → tăng liên tục và mạnh nhất
BODY 2 – Các tổ chức còn lại
  • Organisation 1: 0.6 → 0.9 → 0.7 → 0.7 (tăng rồi ổn định)
  • Organisation 3: 1.5 → 1.8 → 1.4 → 1.5 (dao động)
  • Organisation 4: 0.7 → 0.4 → 0.5 → 0.4 (luôn thấp nhất)
  • Organisation 5: 1.2 → 0.8 → 0.5 → 0.5 (giảm rõ rệt)
4. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • increased significantly / fluctuated / declined steadily / remained the lowest / the highest overall value

The bar chart compares the amount of money (in billions of dollars) invested by five organisations between 2008 and 2011.

Overall, Organisation 2 consistently invested the most and showed a strong upward trend. In contrast, Organisation 4 recorded the lowest figures throughout, and Organisation 5 experienced a notable decline over the period.

In 2008, Organisation 3 was the largest investor at 1.5 billion dollars, followed by Organisation 2 at 1.3 billion. Organisation 5 contributed 1.2 billion, while Organisation 4 and Organisation 1 invested 0.7 billion and 0.6 billion respectively.

By 2009, Organisation 2 rose to 1.5 billion and Organisation 3 peaked at 1.8 billion. Organisation 1 increased to 0.9 billion, whereas Organisations 4 and 5 declined. In 2010 and 2011, Organisation 2 grew significantly to 2.3 and then 2.6 billion — the highest overall. Organisation 3 fell slightly to 1.4–1.5 billion, while Organisations 4 and 5 remained low at 0.4–0.5 billion.

title: Table: The table below shows the total population and the proportion of males and femal...
prompt: The table below shows the total population and the proportion of males and females aged 15 and aged 75 in the UK from 1911 to 2011. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Population by Age Groups (1911–2011)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết tổng dân số và tỷ lệ người ở 2 nhóm tuổi (15 và 75), kèm số lượng nam/nữ, qua 3 năm: 1911, 1961, 2011.

Trọng tâm: xu hướng theo thời gian \+ so sánh giữa nhóm tuổi và giới tính.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: dân số – nhóm tuổi – nam/nữ – 3 năm
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → shows / compares
  • population → giữ nguyên
  • age groups → age categories
  • Viết 1 câu, không số liệu

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Tổng dân số tăng đều theo thời gian
  • Nhóm 15 giảm tỷ lệ, trong khi nhóm 75 tăng rõ rệt

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Tổng dân số & nhóm 15
  • Population: tăng (36.1 → 46.1 → 56.1)
  • % age 15: giảm dần (1.86 → 1.48 → 1.23)
  • Nam: tăng nhẹ
  • Nữ: giảm nhẹ

→ Nhấn mạnh: nhóm trẻ giảm tỷ trọng

BODY 2 – Nhóm 75
  • % age 75: tăng mạnh (0.23 → 0.70)
  • Nam: tăng mạnh liên tục
  • Nữ: tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ cuối kỳ

→ Nhấn mạnh: già hóa dân số rõ rệt

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • increase → rise / grow
  • decrease → fall / decline
  • slightly → marginally
  • significantly → considerably
  • percentage → proportion

Ví dụ:

  • tăng mạnh → rose significantly
  • giảm nhẹ → declined slightly

The table compares population figures and the proportions of people aged 15 and 75 in a country in 1911, 1961 and 2011.

Overall, the total population increased steadily over the period, while the proportion of younger people declined and the share of elderly people rose. In addition, females slightly outnumbered males in older age groups.

In detail, the total population grew from 36.1 million in 1911 to 46.1 million in 1961 and reached 56.1 million in 2011. By contrast, the percentage of people aged 15 fell gradually from 1.86% to 1.23%. The number of males in this group increased from 334,200 to 354,400, whereas the number of females decreased slightly from 335,700 to 333,700.

Regarding the elderly population, the proportion of people aged 75 rose markedly from 0.23% in 1911 to 0.70% in 2011. The number of elderly males increased significantly from 34,100 to 181,300, while females remained higher overall, rising from 47,400 to a peak of 254,900 in 1961 before falling to 210,100 in 2011.

title: Table: The table beneath shows the level of people specialists in various kinds of ente...
prompt: The table beneath shows the level of people specialists in various kinds of enterprises in 1990, 2000, and 2010. Sum up the data by choosing and writing about the primary capacities and make correlations where fitting.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Employment by Gender (1990–2010)

1. Hiểu đề (1 câu là đủ)

Bảng cho biết tỷ lệ (%) nam và nữ làm việc trong 4 ngành (Retail, Health, Manufacturing, Tourism) qua 3 năm: 1990, 2000, 2010.

2. Introduction (Paraphrase gọn)

→ The table compares the percentages of male and female workers in four sectors in 1990, 2000 and 2010.

3. Overview (2 ý chính là đủ)

  • Nam: xu hướng không đồng đều, Manufacturing giảm mạnh nhất
  • Nữ: tăng ở hầu hết ngành, đặc biệt là Tourism luôn cao nhất

→ Viết 1–2 câu, không cần số.

4. Body chia theo giới (dễ viết nhất)

BODY 1 – Nam
  • 1990: Manufacturing cao nhất (30%), Health thấp nhất (7%)
  • Manufacturing: giảm mạnh (30 → 15 → 10)
  • Các ngành còn lại:
  • Retail: giữ nguyên (13)
  • Tourism: giảm nhẹ rồi ổn định (13 → 10 → 10)
  • Health: tăng nhẹ (7 → 7 → 9)

Key idea: Manufacturing giảm mạnh, còn lại khá ổn định

BODY 2 – Nữ
  • 1990: Tourism cao nhất (23%), Retail & Manufacturing thấp nhất (7)
  • Tourism: tăng đều (23 → 25 → 27)
  • Health: tăng đều (9 → 11 → 15)
  • Retail: tăng nhẹ (7 → 8 → 9)
  • Manufacturing: tăng rồi giảm mạnh (7 → 8 → 3)

Key idea: đa số ngành tăng, trừ Manufacturing giảm mạnh cuối kỳ

5. Từ vựng thay thế (ăn điểm nhanh)

  • male/female workers → male/female employment
  • increase → rise / grow
  • decrease → decline / fall
  • stay the same → remain stable

The table compares the percentages of men and women working in four sectors—retail, health, manufacturing and tourism—in 1990, 2000 and 2010.

Overall, men were more concentrated in manufacturing, although their participation declined markedly over time, while women were more represented in tourism and health, both of which saw steady growth. Retail remained relatively stable for both genders.

In detail, male employment in manufacturing dropped significantly from 30% in 1990 to 15% in 2000 and further to 10% in 2010. A similar but less dramatic decline was seen in tourism, from 13% to 10%, where it then stabilised. By contrast, the proportion of men working in retail remained unchanged at 13% across all three years, while health rose slightly from 7% to 9%.

For women, tourism was consistently the largest sector, increasing from 23% in 1990 to 27% in 2010. Employment in health also rose steadily from 9% to 15%, making it the second most significant sector. Retail saw a gradual increase from 7% to 9%, whereas manufacturing remained the least common, fluctuating slightly before falling to just 3% in 2010.

title: Table: The table compares data for the world s large-scale and small-scale sea fishing ...
prompt: The table compares data for the world s large-scale and small-scale sea fishing industries. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng so sánh ngành đánh bắt biển toàn cầu giữa large-scale (tàu công nghiệp) và small-scale (tàu địa phương) theo: lao động, sản lượng, chi phí và nhiên liệu

2. Introduction

The table compares large-scale and small-scale marine fishing industries worldwide in terms of employment, catch, costs and fuel consumption.

3. Overview

Small-scale dùng lao động nhiều hơn rất nhiều, trong khi large-scale hiệu quả hơn (sản lượng công nghiệp, chi phí cao, tiêu thụ nhiên liệu lớn); large-scale chi phối đánh bắt cho chế biến công nghiệp

4. Body

Body 1 (Employment \+ Catch):

Small-scale: \~12 million lao động \>\> large-scale (\~0.5 million); food catch khá tương đương (24 vs 29 triệu tấn); tuy nhiên chỉ large-scale có sản lượng lớn cho industrial processing (\~22 triệu tấn), small-scale gần như không có

Body 2 (Cost \+ Fuel):

Chi phí mỗi việc làm: large-scale rất cao ($30k–$300k) vs small-scale ($250–$2,500); nhiên liệu: large-scale tiêu thụ cực lớn (14–19 triệu tấn) so với small-scale (1.5–1.8)

5. Key phrases

labour-intensive; capital-intensive; account for; significantly higher/lower; dominate industrial output

The table compares large-scale industrial fishing with small-scale local fishing in terms of employment, catch, costs and fuel use.

Overall, small-scale fisheries employ far more people, while industrial fishing produces slightly more total catch and consumes significantly more fuel.

In terms of employment, local fishing involves around 12 million workers, compared to only 500,000 in the industrial sector. However, large-scale operations yield a higher total catch, with 29 million tonnes of food fish and an additional 22 million tonnes for industrial processing. By contrast, small-scale fisheries produce 24 million tonnes of food and almost no output for processing.

There is also a clear difference in costs. The capital cost per job in industrial fishing is much higher, ranging from $30,000 to $300,000, whereas in small-scale fishing it is only $250 to $2,500. Similarly, fuel consumption is far greater in large-scale operations, at 14–19 million tonnes annually, compared to just 1.5–1.8 million tonnes for local boats.

title: Table: The table compares the international tourist arrivals in millions in 9 countries...
prompt: The table compares the international tourist arrivals in millions in 9 countries in 2009 and 2010 and the changes (in percentages). Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng so sánh lượng khách du lịch quốc tế (triệu người) ở 9 quốc gia trong 2009 vs 2010, kèm % thay đổi

2. Introduction

The table compares international tourist arrivals in nine countries in 2009 and 2010, along with the percentage changes.

3. Overview

Hầu hết các quốc gia tăng nhẹ, ngoại trừ Hong Kong giảm; France vẫn cao nhất và không đổi, trong khi Germany và China có tăng trưởng mạnh nhất

4. Body

Body 1 (Top \+ stable \+ small growth):

France giữ nguyên (76); US, Spain, UK, Italy tăng nhẹ (khoảng \+1–3%), trong đó UK tăng nhiều nhất trong nhóm nhỏ (+3.6%)

Body 2 (Big changes):

Germany tăng mạnh nhất (11 → 23, \+109%); China (22 → 34, \+55%) và Turkey (33 → 45, \+33%) cũng tăng đáng kể; Hong Kong là nước duy nhất giảm (10 → 9, \-11%)

5. Key phrases

remained unchanged; rose slightly; saw a sharp increase; experienced the fastest growth; the only decline

The table compares the number of international tourist arrivals, in millions, in nine countries in 2009 and 2010, along with the percentage change over the period.

Overall, France and the US received the highest numbers of tourists in both years, while most countries experienced growth, particularly Germany and China. In contrast, Hong Kong was the only country to record a decline.

In detail, France remained the most visited country, with arrivals unchanged at 76 million in both years. The US ranked second, rising slightly from 66 to 67 million. Spain and the UK had similar figures in 2009 (both 55 million), but the UK grew more noticeably to 57 million, compared to Spain’s modest increase to 56 million. Italy also saw a small rise from 44 to 45 million.

More substantial growth was observed in several countries. Turkey increased sharply from 33 to 45 million, while China rose significantly from 22 to 34 million. The most dramatic change occurred in Germany, where arrivals more than doubled from 11 to 23 million. By contrast, Hong Kong was the only exception, with a slight decrease from 10 to 9 million.

title: Table: The table describes the changes of people who went for international travel in 1...
prompt: The table describes the changes of people who went for international travel in 1990, 1995, 2000 and 2005 (in millions). Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Regional Data (1990–2005)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết số liệu theo khu vực (Africa, America, Asia-Pacific, Europe, Middle East) qua 4 năm: 1990, 1995, 2000, 2005, kèm tổng (total).

Trọng tâm: xu hướng theo thời gian \+ so sánh giữa các khu vực.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: số liệu – khu vực – 4 năm – có tổng
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → shows / compares
  • figures → data / figures
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Tổng số liệu tăng đều theo thời gian
  • Europe luôn cao nhất, trong khi Middle East thấp nhất

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Europe, America, Asia-Pacific)
  • Europe: cao nhất và tăng liên tục (280 → 400)
  • America: tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ cuối kỳ
  • Asia-Pacific: tăng mạnh nhất, vượt America vào cuối

→ Nhấn mạnh: Europe dẫn đầu, Asia tăng nhanh

BODY 2 – Nhóm thấp (Africa, Middle East \+ Total)
  • Africa: tăng đều (18 → 28)
  • Middle East: thấp nhất nhưng tăng ổn định (9 → 15)
  • Total: tăng mạnh (448 → 693)

→ Nhấn mạnh: tất cả khu vực đều tăng

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • increase → rise / grow
  • decrease → fall / decline
  • steadily → consistently
  • highest → the highest figure
  • lowest → the lowest figure

Ví dụ:

  • tăng đều → rose steadily
  • cao nhất → remained the highest throughout

The table compares figures for five regions between 1990 and 2005, along with the overall total.

Overall, all regions experienced growth over the period, and Europe consistently recorded by far the highest figures, while the Middle East remained the lowest. Asia and the Pacific showed the most notable increase, eventually overtaking America by 2005.

In 1990, Europe dominated with 280.2, far exceeding America (80.5) and Asia and the Pacific (60.2). Africa and the Middle East were much lower at 18.2 and 9.8 respectively. By 1995, all regions had risen, particularly Europe (390.3) and America (112.5), while Asia also increased significantly to 80.3.

From 2000 to 2005, Europe grew more gradually from 393.2 to 400.2, maintaining its lead. Asia and the Pacific continued to rise sharply, reaching 135.8 and surpassing America, which fell slightly from 118.2 to 113.2. Africa and the Middle East also increased steadily, though they remained comparatively small contributors. Meanwhile, the overall total climbed consistently from 448.9 in 1990 to 693.7 in 2005.

title: Table: The table shows information about department stores and online stores in Austral...
prompt: The table shows information about department stores and online stores in Australia in 2011. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Department vs Online Stores

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng so sánh 2 loại cửa hàng (department vs online) dựa trên 4 chỉ số: số lượng doanh nghiệp, lợi nhuận, doanh thu và tăng trưởng.

Trọng tâm: so sánh trực tiếp giữa 2 nhóm.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: 2 loại cửa hàng – số liệu kinh doanh – 4 chỉ số – không có thời gian
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → compares
  • businesses → companies / firms
  • Viết 1 câu, không số liệu

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Online stores vượt trội ở tất cả các chỉ số
  • Chênh lệch lớn nhất nằm ở số lượng doanh nghiệp

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Quy mô (Number of businesses)
  • Online: 368, cao hơn rất nhiều
  • Department: chỉ 67

→ Nhấn mạnh: online áp đảo về số lượng

BODY 2 – Hiệu quả kinh doanh
  • Profit: online cao hơn (863 vs 807)
  • Revenue: online cao hơn (13,400 vs 12,700)
  • Growth: online cũng cao hơn (0.6% vs 0.4%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: online dẫn đầu toàn diện

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • compare → contrast
  • higher → greater
  • slightly → marginally
  • significantly → considerably

Ví dụ:

  • cao hơn đáng kể → considerably higher
  • cao hơn một chút → slightly higher

The table compares department stores and online stores in terms of business numbers, profit, sales revenue and growth.

Overall, online stores outperform department stores in all categories, particularly in the number of businesses and growth rate.

In detail, there are far more online stores (368) than department stores (67). In terms of profit, online businesses generate slightly higher figures at 863 AUD compared to 807 AUD for department stores. A similar pattern is seen in sales revenue, where online stores reach 13,400 AUD, exceeding the 12,700 AUD recorded for traditional retailers.

Regarding growth, online stores also show a stronger performance at 0.6%, compared to just 0.4% for department stores, indicating a faster expansion of the online sector.

title: Table: The table shows information about medical care in three different European count...
prompt: The table shows information about medical care in three different European countries between 1980 and 2000. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng so sánh 3 chỉ số y tế (physicians, hospital beds, length of stay) tại Austria, France, Sweden qua 1980–2000

2. Introduction

The table compares three healthcare indicators in Austria, France and Sweden between 1980 and 2000.

3. Overview

Sweden luôn cao nhất ở beds và length of stay; France giảm rõ về physicians và thời gian nằm viện; Austria tăng mạnh beds và biến động lớn ở length of stay

4. Body

Body 1 (Physicians \+ Beds):

Physicians: Sweden cao nhất (3.6 → 3.8), France giảm mạnh (2.4 → 1.3), Austria tăng nhẹ (0.8 → 1)

Beds: Sweden ổn định cao (6.4 → 6.9), Austria tăng mạnh (1.4 → 3.7), France cũng tăng (0.2 → 1.6)

Body 2 (Length of stay):

Austria biến động mạnh (18 → 6 → 18); France giảm đều (9 → 5); Sweden giảm rồi tăng nhẹ (23 → 18 → 21)

5. Key phrases

remained the highest; rose sharply; declined steadily; fluctuated significantly; by contrast

The table compares three healthcare indicators, namely physicians and hospital beds per 1,000 people and the average length of hospital stay, in Austria, France and Sweden from 1980 to 2000.

Overall, Sweden consistently recorded the highest figures for hospital beds and length of stay, while France showed clear declines in both the number of physicians and the duration of hospital stays. By contrast, Austria experienced a significant rise in hospital beds and notable fluctuations in length of stay.

In terms of physicians, Sweden remained the highest throughout, increasing slightly from 3.6 in 1980 to 3.8 in 2000 despite a dip in 1990. France, on the other hand, declined steadily from 2.4 to 1.3, whereas Austria rose modestly from 0.8 to 1. Regarding hospital beds, Sweden maintained a consistently high level, rising marginally from 6.4 to 6.9. Austria saw a sharp increase from 1.4 to 3.7, while France also grew from 0.2 to 1.6, although it remained the lowest.

As for the average length of hospital stay, Austria fluctuated significantly, dropping from 18 days to 6 before returning to 18. France declined steadily from 9 to 5, while Sweden fell from 23 to 18 before rising slightly to 21.

title: Table: The table shows the frequency of adults using the internet in one European count...
prompt: The table shows the frequency of adults using the internet in one European country from 2006 to 2010. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Frequency of activity (2006–2010)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy tỷ lệ (%) người tham gia một hoạt động theo tần suất (daily, weekly, monthly, never) từ 2006–2010.

→ Dạng: time series \+ categories

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

  • Giữ: percentage – frequency – over a 5-year period
  • Dùng:
  • shows → illustrates / presents
  • from 2006 to 2010 → over the period from 2006 to 2010

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Daily tăng mạnh và trở thành cao nhất
  • Never giảm cực mạnh
  • Các nhóm còn lại biến động nhẹ / giảm dần

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Xu hướng chính (Daily & Never)
  • Daily: tăng liên tục (36% → 72%)
  • Never: giảm mạnh (34% → 1%)

→ Ý: shift rõ rệt từ không tham gia → tham gia thường xuyên

BODY 2 – Nhóm còn lại
  • Weekly: tăng rồi giảm (15 → 29 → 22)
  • Monthly: giảm đều (15 → 5)

→ Ý: tần suất trung bình giảm hoặc dao động nhẹ

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • rise sharply → tăng mạnh
  • fall dramatically → giảm mạnh
  • fluctuate → dao động
  • steadily → đều đặn
  • overtake → vượt

Ví dụ:

  • Daily participation rose sharply over the period.
  • The proportion of people who never participated fell dramatically.
  • Weekly figures fluctuated before declining.

The table compares the frequency of an activity, measured as percentages, between 2006 and 2010.

Overall, daily participation increased markedly over the period, while the proportion of people who never engaged in the activity declined sharply. Less frequent participation, such as three times per month, also decreased, whereas weekly figures fluctuated slightly.

In 2006, daily participation stood at 36%, rising steadily to 40% in 2007 and 45% in 2008, before increasing sharply to 65% in 2009 and peaking at 72% in 2010. By contrast, the percentage of people who never took part fell dramatically from 34% in 2006 to 20% in 2007 and 16% in 2008, before dropping to just 2% in 2009 and 1% in the final year.

Weekly participation showed a more variable trend, increasing from 15% in 2006 to a peak of 29% in 2008, then declining to 25% in 2009 and 22% in 2010. Meanwhile, participation at a rate of three times per month decreased consistently from 15% in 2006 and 2007 to 10% in 2008, followed by further declines to 8% and 5% in the final two years.

title: Table: The table shows the number of employees and factories producing silk in England ...
prompt: The table shows the number of employees and factories producing silk in England and Wales between 1851 and 1901. Summarise the information by selecting, reporting the main features, and make comparison where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Employees & silk factories (England and Wales, 1851–1901)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy số lượng lao động (nam, nữ, tổng) và số nhà máy sản xuất tơ lụa tại England & Wales từ 1851 đến 1901.

→ Dạng: time series \+ nhiều biến

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

  • Giữ: number of employees – factories – silk production – England & Wales – period
  • Dùng:
  • shows → illustrates / presents
  • between → over the period from … to …

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Tổng lao động giảm mạnh theo thời gian
  • Nữ luôn nhiều hơn nam
  • Factories tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ về cuối

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Employees (main trend)
  • Total: giảm liên tục (130k → 38k)
  • Male: giảm mạnh nhất
  • Female: cũng giảm nhưng luôn cao hơn nam

→ Ý: ngành suy giảm lao động, đặc biệt là nam

BODY 2 – Factories \+ chi tiết đáng chú ý
  • Factories: tăng mạnh (272 → 761) rồi giảm (→ 623)
  • 1891: slight rise ở male (khác trend chung)

→ Ý: số nhà máy không giảm đồng bộ với lao động

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • decline sharply → giảm mạnh
  • remain higher than → luôn cao hơn
  • peak at → đạt đỉnh
  • fluctuate → dao động
  • overall downward trend → xu hướng giảm chung

Ví dụ:

  • The number of employees declined sharply over the period.
  • Female workers consistently outnumbered males.
  • The number of factories peaked in 1861 before falling gradually.

The table compares the number of male and female employees, total workers, and silk factories in England and Wales between 1851 and 1901.

Overall, the number of employees declined substantially over the period, while the number of factories initially increased before falling slightly. Women consistently made up a larger proportion of the workforce than men.

In 1851, there were 130,750 workers in total, including 53,964 men and 76,786 women, working across 272 factories. Over the next two decades, total employment dropped steadily to 91,412 in 1871, with both male and female figures decreasing, although women remained the majority. By 1881, the workforce had fallen further to 67,904, and this downward trend continued to 49,025 in 1891 and just 38,942 by 1901.

In contrast, the number of factories rose sharply from 272 in 1851 to a peak of 761 in 1861, before declining gradually to 623 by 1901. Despite the reduction in factories after 1861, their number remained significantly higher than at the beginning of the period, even as employment levels continued to fall.

title: Table: The tables below provide information about the consumption and production of pot...
prompt: The tables below provide information about the consumption and production of potatoes in five parts of the world in 2006. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Potato Consumption & Production

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết mức tiêu thụ (kg/người) và sản lượng (triệu tấn) khoai tây ở 5 khu vực.

Trọng tâm: so sánh giữa consumption vs production \+ giữa các khu vực.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: tiêu thụ – sản xuất – khoai tây – 5 khu vực
  • Đổi từ:
  • tables → show / compare
  • consumption → intake
  • production → giữ nguyên
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Europe tiêu thụ nhiều nhất, trong khi Africa thấp nhất
  • Asia sản xuất nhiều nhất, theo sau là Europe

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Consumption
  • Europe cao nhất (96.1), vượt xa các khu vực khác
  • North America đứng thứ hai (57.9)
  • Asia và South/Central America trung bình (\~23–26)
  • Africa thấp nhất (14.1)

→ Nhấn mạnh: Europe nổi bật, Africa thấp nhất

BODY 2 – Production
  • Asia cao nhất (131.2), sát với Europe (126.3)
  • North America thấp hơn nhiều (24.7)
  • Africa và South/Central America thấp nhất (\~15–16)

→ Nhấn mạnh: Asia dẫn đầu sản xuất

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • consumption → intake
  • production → output
  • highest → the highest figure
  • lowest → the lowest figure
  • significantly → considerably

Ví dụ:

  • cao nhất → recorded the highest figure
  • thấp nhất → had the lowest level

The tables compare potato consumption per person and total production across five regions.

Overall, Europe records the highest consumption, while Asia produces the most potatoes despite relatively low consumption per capita. By contrast, Africa has the lowest figures for both measures.

In detail, Europe has by far the highest consumption at 96.1 kg per person, followed by North America at 57.9 kg. Asia and South and Central America show similar, moderate levels at 25.8 kg and 23.6 kg respectively, whereas Africa is lowest at 14.1 kg.

In terms of production, Asia leads with 131.2 million tonnes, slightly ahead of Europe at 126.3 million tonnes. The remaining regions produce significantly less, with North America at 24.7 million tonnes, Africa at 16.4 million tonnes, and South and Central America at 15.6 million tonnes.

title: Table: The tables below show the distribution of students at UK universities by mode of...
prompt: The tables below show the distribution of students at UK universities by mode of study and gender. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Postgraduate Students (1996–2010)

1. Hiểu đề (1 câu)

Bảng cho biết số lượng học viên sau đại học nam và nữ theo 2 hình thức học (full-time, part-time) qua 4 giai đoạn.

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

→ The tables compare the number of male and female postgraduate students studying full-time and part-time between 1996 and 2010.

3. Overview (chỉ 2 ý)

  • Full-time: tăng mạnh ở cả nam và nữ
  • Part-time: luôn cao hơn full-time, nhưng biến động nhẹ (tăng rồi giảm)

4. Body chia theo mode (dễ viết hơn chia theo gender)

BODY 1 – Full-time
  • Nam: tăng đều (75k → 87k → 115k → 151k)
  • Nữ: tăng tương tự (62k → 85k → 118k → 146k)

Key: cả hai giới đều tăng mạnh, gần như song song

BODY 2 – Part-time
  • Nam: giảm nhẹ rồi tăng, sau đó giảm (113k → 108k → 138k → 117k)
  • Nữ: tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ (108k → 125k → 172k → 163k)

Key: luôn cao hơn full-time, nữ cao hơn nam ở cuối kỳ

5. So sánh nhanh (chèn vào body nếu muốn ăn điểm)

  • Ban đầu: nam \> nữ (full-time)
  • Cuối kỳ: số liệu gần bằng nhau (full-time)
  • Part-time: nữ vượt nam rõ ở 2005–2010

The tables compare the numbers of male and female postgraduate students studying full-time and part-time between 1996/97 and 2009/10.

Overall, full-time enrolment increased steadily for both genders, while part-time figures fluctuated. Men initially outnumbered women in part-time study, but women became the dominant group by the end of the period.

For full-time study, both genders showed consistent growth. The number of male students rose from 75,370 in 1996/97 to 151,275 in 2009/10, while the female figure increased from 62,344 to 146,980. Although men remained slightly higher overall, the gap narrowed significantly over time.

In contrast, part-time study showed more variation. Male participation fell slightly from 113,961 to 108,770 in 2000/01, then rose to a peak of 138,505 before declining to 117,105 in 2009/10. Meanwhile, the number of female part-time students increased steadily from 108,702 to 172,640 in 2005/06, before dropping slightly to 163,340. By the final year, women clearly outnumbered men in part-time education.

title: Table: The tables show results of 2 surveys done in 1980 and 2010 on various aspects of...
prompt: The tables show results of 2 surveys done in 1980 and 2010 on various aspects of city living in a particular city. Units are measured in per
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Public Opinion (1980 vs 2010)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết tỷ lệ đánh giá (good / neutral / bad) về 6 lĩnh vực trong 2 năm: 1980 và 2010.

Trọng tâm: so sánh theo thời gian \+ thay đổi trong mức độ đánh giá.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: ý kiến công chúng – 6 lĩnh vực – 3 mức đánh giá – 2 năm
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → compares / shows
  • good/bad → positive / negative
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Positive (good) chiếm tỷ lệ cao nhất ở hầu hết lĩnh vực
  • Negative (bad) tăng lên ở nhiều lĩnh vực vào năm 2010, đặc biệt là Transport

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Nhóm tích cực (Good)
  • 1980: Healthcare cao nhất (82), Education & Environment (72)
  • 2010: Education cao nhất (82), Employment tăng mạnh (62 → 72)
  • Transport giảm rõ (52 → 39)

→ Nhấn mạnh: một số lĩnh vực cải thiện, nhưng transport giảm mạnh

BODY 2 – Neutral & Bad
  • Neutral nhìn chung giảm (ví dụ Education: 23 → 11)
  • Bad tăng ở nhiều lĩnh vực:
  • Employment (10 → 23)
  • Environment (6 → 16)
  • Transport cao nhất (32 → 38)

→ Nhấn mạnh: xu hướng tiêu cực tăng lên

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • good → positive
  • bad → negative
  • neither → neutral
  • increase → rise
  • decrease → decline

Ví dụ:

  • tăng đáng kể → rose considerably
  • giảm nhẹ → declined slightly

The tables compare people’s opinions about six aspects of life in 1980 and 2010, classified as good, neutral, or bad.

Overall, most aspects were viewed positively in both years, although opinions became more polarized in 2010, with higher proportions rating some areas as bad. Education remained the most positively rated category, while transport was consistently the least favourable.

In 1980, healthcare received the highest “good” rating at 82%, followed by education and the environment, both at 72%. Shops and employment were also viewed positively by around two-thirds of respondents, whereas transport had the lowest positive rating at 52% and the highest “bad” figure at 32%.

By 2010, education became the top-rated aspect, increasing to 82%, while employment also rose significantly to 72%. In contrast, healthcare and the environment saw slight declines in positive ratings, and the proportion of negative responses increased notably, particularly for employment (23%), environment (16%) and shops (16%). Transport remained the weakest area, with only 39% rating it as good and 38% considering it bad.

title: Table: The graph below shows the average retirement age of males and females in six cou...
prompt: The graph below shows the average retirement age of males and females in six countries in 2003. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Life Expectancy (Men vs Women)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết tuổi thọ trung bình (năm \+ tháng) của nam và nữ ở 6 quốc gia.

Trọng tâm: so sánh giữa nam – nữ và giữa các quốc gia.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: tuổi thọ – nam và nữ – 6 quốc gia – đơn vị năm/tháng
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → shows / compares
  • life expectancy → giữ nguyên
  • Viết 1 câu, không số liệu

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Nữ luôn sống lâu hơn nam ở tất cả quốc gia
  • Korea và Japan cao nhất, trong khi France và Italy thấp hơn

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Men
  • Cao nhất: Korea (68), Japan (67)
  • Trung bình: US (65), Australia (64)
  • Thấp nhất: Italy (58), France (57)

→ Nhấn mạnh: chênh lệch rõ giữa các nước

BODY 2 – Women
  • Cao nhất: Korea (67), Japan (66)
  • Trung bình: US (64), Australia (63)
  • Thấp nhất: Italy (57), France (56)

→ Nhấn mạnh: pattern giống nam nhưng luôn cao hơn

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • life expectancy → average lifespan
  • higher → longer
  • lower → shorter
  • slightly → marginally

Ví dụ:

  • sống lâu hơn → have a longer life expectancy
  • cao hơn một chút → slightly higher

The table compares life expectancy for men and women in six countries, measured in years and months.

Overall, females live slightly longer than males in all countries, while Korea and Japan record the highest life expectancy and France and Italy the lowest.

In detail, Korea has the highest figures, with men living 68 years and 8 months and women 67 years and 2 months. Japan follows closely, at 67 years and 4 months for males and 66 years and 9 months for females. The United States and Australia show similar patterns, with male life expectancy at around 64–65 years and females slightly lower in months.

By contrast, Italy and France have the lowest life expectancy, with men living 58 years and 2 months and 57 years and 4 months respectively, while women reach 57 years and 3 months in Italy and 56 years and 9 months in France.

title: Table: The table below shows the change in number of people engaged in various physical...
prompt: The table below shows the change in number of people engaged in various physical activities between the years 2001-2009 in Australia (in mil
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng cho thấy số người (triệu) tham gia 5 hoạt động thể chất ở Úc (running; aerobics; tennis; cycling; swimming) qua 3 năm: 2001; 2005; 2009 → dạng: change over time \+ so sánh

2. Introduction

The table shows the number of people participating in five physical activities in Australia in 2001, 2005 and 2009.

3. Overview

Running; aerobics; tennis tăng; cycling và swimming giảm tổng thể; cycling luôn phổ biến nhất; running tăng mạnh nhất

4. Body

Body 1 (nhóm tăng):

2001: aerobics cao nhất (1.5) \> running (1.4) \> tennis (1.0); running tăng mạnh lên 2.2 vào 2009; aerobics và tennis tăng nhẹ, lần lượt đạt 1.7 và 1.2

Body 2 (nhóm giảm):

2001: cycling cao nhất (4.5), sau đó là swimming (3.7); cycling giảm xuống 3.1 rồi hồi nhẹ 3.6; swimming tăng nhẹ lên 3.8 rồi giảm còn 3.3

5. Key phrases

increase steadily; rise significantly; decline overall; remain the most popular; show a slight growth

The table shows the number of people (in millions) participating in five physical activities in Australia in 2001, 2005 and 2009, along with the percentage change.

Overall, running experienced the most significant growth, while cycling and swimming declined over the period. Cycling remained the most popular activity despite a decrease.

In 2001, cycling was the leading activity at 4.5 million, followed by swimming (3.7 million). By contrast, running and tennis were the least popular, at 1.4 and 1.0 million, respectively. Over time, participation in running rose steadily to 2.2 million in 2009, representing the largest increase (56%). Aerobics and tennis also grew slightly, reaching 1.7 and 1.2 million.

In contrast, cycling dropped from 4.5 million in 2001 to 3.6 million in 2009, a decline of 20%. Swimming followed a similar pattern, falling from 3.7 to 3.3 million (\-10%), although it remained the second most popular activity.

title: Table: The table below shows the information about the posts held by women in parliamen...
prompt: The table below shows the information about the posts held by women in parliament and in management in 5 countries in 2000. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Women in Government & Management (2000)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết tỷ lệ phụ nữ trong quốc hội và vị trí quản lý ở 5 quốc gia trong năm 2000.

Trọng tâm: so sánh giữa 2 lĩnh vực \+ giữa các quốc gia.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: phụ nữ – vị trí (quốc hội & quản lý) – 5 quốc gia – 1 năm
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → shows / compares
  • positions → roles / representation
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Tỷ lệ phụ nữ trong quản lý luôn cao hơn quốc hội ở tất cả quốc gia
  • Norway và Italy nổi bật cao, trong khi Uruguay thấp nhất (quốc hội)

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Parliament (quốc hội)
  • Norway cao nhất (36%), tiếp theo Canada (28%)
  • Tobago and Trinidad trung bình (19%)
  • Italy (10%) và Uruguay thấp nhất (8%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: chênh lệch lớn giữa các quốc gia

BODY 2 – Management (quản lý)
  • Italy cao nhất (54%), Norway (52%)
  • Tobago and Trinidad (40%)
  • Canada và Uruguay bằng nhau (37%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: mức cao hơn rõ so với quốc hội

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • percentage → proportion
  • women → female representation
  • high → higher / highest
  • low → lower / lowest

Ví dụ:

  • tỷ lệ cao → a higher proportion of women
  • thấp nhất → the lowest figure

The table compares the proportions of women in parliamentary positions and management roles in five countries in 2000.

Overall, women were better represented in management than in parliament in all countries, with Norway and Italy recording the highest figures in each category respectively.

In detail, Norway had the highest proportion of female parliamentarians at 36%, followed by Canada at 28%, while the figures were much lower in Tobago and Trinidad (19%), Italy (10%) and Uruguay (8%). In terms of management roles, Italy ranked first with 54%, closely followed by Norway at 52%. Canada and Uruguay had identical figures of 37%, while Tobago and Trinidad stood at 40%.

Notably, the gap between the two sectors was particularly large in countries such as Italy and Uruguay, where female representation in management far exceeded that in parliament.

title: Table: The table below shows the percentage of employers in various sectors having diff...
prompt: The table below shows the percentage of employers in various sectors having difficulty in finding staff in 2003 and 2004. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Difficulty in finding staff (4 sectors, 2003–2004)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy tỷ lệ (%) doanh nghiệp gặp khó khăn trong tuyển dụng ở 4 ngành, theo 3 nhóm:

  • skilled workers
  • unskilled workers
  • technical/managerial staff

→ So sánh giữa 2003 và 2004

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

  • Giữ: percentage – difficulty in recruiting staff – sectors – years
  • Dùng:
  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • finding → recruiting / hiring

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Skilled workers khó tuyển nhất trong mọi ngành
  • Technical/managerial thấp nhất
  • Hầu hết số liệu tăng nhẹ từ 2003 → 2004

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Skilled workers (main pattern)
  • Manufacturing: cao nhất (45 → 51)
  • Commerce: giảm (47 → 41)
  • Building & Services: tăng nhẹ

→ Ý: nhóm skilled luôn cao nhất nhưng không đồng đều

BODY 2 – Unskilled \+ Technical
  • Unskilled: tăng ở hầu hết ngành (đặc biệt Services: 20 → 29)
  • Technical: rất thấp, đa số giảm
  • Commerce: gần như 0
  • Manufacturing/Building: giảm

→ Ý: unskilled tăng, technical vẫn ít khó khăn

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • recruitment difficulties → khó khăn tuyển dụng
  • experienced staff → lao động có kỹ năng
  • low proportion → tỷ lệ thấp
  • increase slightly → tăng nhẹ
  • remain minimal → duy trì ở mức rất thấp

Ví dụ:

  • Skilled workers posed the greatest recruitment challenges.
  • The proportion for technical staff remained minimal.
  • There was a slight increase in most sectors.

The table compares the percentage of employers reporting difficulty in finding skilled, unskilled, and technical/managerial staff across four sectors in 2003 and 2004.

Overall, difficulties in recruiting skilled workers were consistently the highest across all sectors, while shortages of technical and managerial staff were relatively low and generally declined. In addition, problems in hiring unskilled workers tended to increase in most sectors.

In terms of skilled labour, manufacturing recorded the highest figures, rising from 45% in 2003 to 51% in 2004. Building and services also saw increases, from 42% to 45% and from 41% to 45% respectively. In contrast, commerce was the only sector to experience a decline, falling from 47% to 41%.

For unskilled workers, most sectors reported growing difficulties. Manufacturing rose slightly from 26% to 28%, while building increased from 21% to 23%. The most notable rise occurred in services, which climbed from 20% to 29%. By contrast, commerce saw a decrease from 22% to 19%.

Regarding technical and managerial staff, the percentages were much lower overall and declined in every sector. Manufacturing dropped from 8% to 6%, building from 14% to 10%, services from 5% to 2%, and commerce from 2% to zero, indicating that this category posed the least recruitment challenge.

title: Table: The table below shows the percentage of expenditure per capita in Australia acro...
prompt: The table below shows the percentage of expenditure per capita in Australia across different categories in 1986 and 2009. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Difficulty in finding staff (4 sectors, 2003–2004)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy tỷ lệ (%) doanh nghiệp gặp khó khăn trong tuyển dụng ở 4 ngành, theo 3 nhóm:

  • skilled workers
  • unskilled workers
  • technical/managerial staff

→ So sánh giữa 2003 và 2004

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

  • Giữ: percentage – difficulty in recruiting staff – sectors – years
  • Dùng:

  • finding → recruiting / hiring

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Skilled workers khó tuyển nhất trong mọi ngành
  • Technical/managerial thấp nhất
  • Hầu hết số liệu tăng nhẹ từ 2003 → 2004

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Skilled workers (main pattern)
  • Manufacturing: cao nhất (45 → 51)
  • Commerce: giảm (47 → 41)
  • Building & Services: tăng nhẹ

→ Ý: nhóm skilled luôn cao nhất nhưng không đồng đều

BODY 2 – Unskilled \+ Technical
  • Unskilled: tăng ở hầu hết ngành (đặc biệt Services: 20 → 29)
  • Technical: rất thấp, đa số giảm
  • Commerce: gần như 0
  • Manufacturing/Building: giảm

→ Ý: unskilled tăng, technical vẫn ít khó khăn

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • recruitment difficulties → khó khăn tuyển dụng
  • experienced staff → lao động có kỹ năng
  • low proportion → tỷ lệ thấp
  • increase slightly → tăng nhẹ
  • remain minimal → duy trì ở mức rất thấp

Ví dụ:

  • Skilled workers posed the greatest recruitment challenges.
  • The proportion for technical staff remained minimal.
  • There was a slight increase in most sectors.

The table compares the percentage of employers reporting difficulty in finding skilled, unskilled, and technical/managerial staff across four sectors in 2003 and 2004.

Overall, difficulties in recruiting skilled workers were consistently the highest across all sectors, while shortages of technical and managerial staff were relatively low and generally declined. In addition, problems in hiring unskilled workers tended to increase in most sectors.

In terms of skilled labour, manufacturing recorded the highest figures, rising from 45% in 2003 to 51% in 2004. Building and services also saw increases, from 42% to 45% and from 41% to 45% respectively. In contrast, commerce was the only sector to experience a decline, falling from 47% to 41%.

For unskilled workers, most sectors reported growing difficulties. Manufacturing rose slightly from 26% to 28%, while building increased from 21% to 23%. The most notable rise occurred in services, which climbed from 20% to 29%. By contrast, commerce saw a decrease from 22% to 19%.

Regarding technical and managerial staff, the percentages were much lower overall and declined in every sector. Manufacturing dropped from 8% to 6%, building from 14% to 10%, services from 5% to 2%, and commerce from 2% to zero, indicating that this category posed the least recruitment challenge.

title: Table: The table below shows the percentage of first-year students who gave ‘very good’...
prompt: The table below shows the percentage of first-year students who gave ‘very good’ rating to the resources provided by their college, for three courses. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Student Satisfaction (Courses & Resources)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho biết tỷ lệ sinh viên năm nhất đánh giá “very good” cho 5 loại tài nguyên ở 3 khóa học (Economics, Law, Commerce).

Trọng tâm: so sánh giữa các khóa học \+ giữa các loại tài nguyên.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: mức độ hài lòng – sinh viên năm nhất – 3 khóa học – 5 loại tài nguyên
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → shows / compares
  • rating → evaluation / feedback
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Commerce có mức đánh giá cao nhất ở hầu hết tiêu chí
  • Teaching và Tutor thường được đánh giá cao nhất, trong khi pre-course information thấp hơn

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Commerce (nổi bật nhất)
  • Gần như cao nhất ở tất cả tiêu chí (≈ 93–95%)
  • Đặc biệt: Teaching & Pre-course đều đạt 95%

→ Nhấn mạnh: mức hài lòng rất cao và ổn định

BODY 2 – Economics & Law
  • Economics:
  • Teaching & Tutor rất cao (95%, 90%)
  • Pre-course thấp hơn (59%)
  • Law:
  • Khá đồng đều (≈ 60–80%)
  • Other resources cao nhất (80%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: Economics mạnh về giảng dạy, Law cân bằng hơn

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • percentage → proportion
  • very good → highly rated / positive feedback
  • high → high / strong
  • low → lower / less favourable

Ví dụ:

  • được đánh giá cao → received positive feedback
  • thấp hơn → was comparatively lower

The table shows the percentage of first-year students who rated various college resources as “very good” across three courses.

Overall, Commerce received the highest ratings across almost all categories, while Economics showed mixed results, particularly in pre-course information and other resources. Teaching and tutor support were generally rated highly in all courses.

In detail, Commerce recorded consistently strong figures, with 95% for both pre-course information and teaching, and similarly high ratings for tutors (93%), printed resources (85%) and other resources (81%). Economics also performed well in teaching (95%) and tutor support (90%), but had lower scores for pre-course information (59%) and other resources (60%). Law, by contrast, showed a more balanced pattern, with relatively high ratings for pre-course information (72%), tutors (76%) and other resources (80%), although its teaching score was notably lower at 62%.

title: Table: The table below shows the percentage of government spending on education and tra...
prompt: The table below shows the percentage of government spending on education and training and the participation of 18-24 year olds in five count
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Expenditure vs Participation

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng so sánh tỷ lệ chi tiêu và tỷ lệ tham gia ở 5 quốc gia.

Trọng tâm: so sánh giữa 2 chỉ số và giữa các quốc gia.

2. Định hướng viết Introduction

  • Paraphrase với 4 ý: chi tiêu – tham gia – 5 quốc gia – dạng tỷ lệ
  • Đổi từ:
  • table → compares / shows
  • rate → proportion / percentage
  • Viết 1 câu, không số

3. Định hướng viết Overview

  • Slovenia cao nhất về chi tiêu, trong khi Poland thấp nhất
  • Poland có participation cao nhất, còn Romania thấp nhất

4. Phân chia BODY rõ ràng

BODY 1 – Expenditure rate
  • Slovenia cao nhất (25%)
  • Hungary (13%), Romania (10%), Czech (9%)
  • Poland thấp nhất (5%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: chênh lệch rõ giữa các nước

BODY 2 – Participation rate
  • Poland cao nhất (44%), tiếp theo Slovenia (40%)
  • Hungary trung bình (34%)
  • Czech (27%) và Romania thấp nhất (23%)

→ Nhấn mạnh: không hoàn toàn tỷ lệ thuận với chi tiêu

5. Gợi ý Paraphrasing

  • rate → proportion / percentage
  • high → higher / highest
  • low → lower / lowest
  • similar → comparable

Ví dụ:

  • cao nhất → recorded the highest proportion
  • thấp nhất → had the lowest figure

The table compares five countries in terms of expenditure rates and participation rates.

Overall, higher spending does not consistently lead to higher participation, although Slovenia records both the highest expenditure and a relatively high participation rate. Poland, despite the lowest spending, shows the highest participation.

In detail, Slovenia allocates the largest share at 25%, followed by Hungary at 13%, while Romania, the Czech Republic and Poland spend 10%, 9% and 5% respectively. However, Poland leads in participation at 44%, slightly ahead of Slovenia at 40%. Hungary also records a moderate figure of 34%, whereas the Czech Republic and Romania have lower participation rates at 27% and 23% respectively.

title: Table: The table below shows the percentage use of four different fuel types to generat...
prompt: The table below shows the percentage use of four different fuel types to generate electricity in five Asian countries in 2005. Summarise the information by selecting, reporting the main features, and make comparison where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Fuel types used to generate electricity (5 Asian countries, 2005)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy tỷ lệ (%) sử dụng 4 loại nhiên liệu để sản xuất điện ở 5 quốc gia châu Á năm 2005.

→ Dạng: so sánh giữa các quốc gia \+ nhiều categories

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

  • Giữ đủ: percentage – fuel types – generate electricity – five Asian countries – 2005
  • Dùng từ:
  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • use → consumption / usage

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Không có 1 loại nhiên liệu thống trị tất cả quốc gia
  • Coal chiếm ưu thế ở Malaysia & Singapore
  • Nuclear nổi bật ở Japan & South Korea
  • Thailand phụ thuộc nhiều vào petroleum & other sources

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Nhóm dùng Coal/Nuclear nhiều
  • Malaysia: coal cao nhất (61%)
  • Singapore: coal cao (42%), other cũng cao
  • Japan: nuclear cao nhất (49%)
  • South Korea: hydro \+ nuclear cao

→ Ý: các nước phát triển dùng coal/nuclear nhiều

BODY 2 – Nhóm còn lại / khác biệt
  • Thailand: không dùng nuclear (0%)
  • Petroleum cao nhất ở Thailand (36%)
  • Hydro cao nhất ở South Korea (48%)
  • Other cao ở Thailand & Singapore

→ Ý: Thailand khác biệt nhất (phụ thuộc nhiên liệu khác)

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • account for → chiếm
  • dominate → chiếm ưu thế
  • rely on → phụ thuộc vào
  • make up → tạo thành
  • proportion → tỷ lệ

Ví dụ:

  • Coal accounts for the largest proportion in Malaysia.
  • Thailand relies heavily on petroleum and other sources.

The table compares the proportions of electricity generated from four fuel types in five Asian countries in 2005.

Overall, coal and lignite dominated electricity production in Malaysia and Singapore, while nuclear power was the main source in Japan and South Korea. By contrast, Thailand relied heavily on petroleum products and other sources, with no use of nuclear energy.

In Malaysia, coal and lignite accounted for the majority at 61%, followed by nuclear energy at 19%, while the remaining sources made relatively small contributions. A similar pattern can be seen in Singapore, where coal (42%) was the largest source, although a substantial share also came from other sources (39%). In contrast, Japan depended primarily on nuclear power, which made up 49% of electricity generation, with other sources contributing 39%, while coal, petroleum and hydro played only minor roles.

South Korea also relied heavily on nuclear energy (33%) and hydro and wind (48%), with very limited use of coal and petroleum. Thailand showed a markedly different profile, as it generated most of its electricity from other sources (42%) and petroleum products (36%), alongside a notable proportion from hydro and wind (19%), but no nuclear power at all.

title: Table: The table below shows the population ratio of males per 100 females in six diffe...
