Finally, the clean pulp is passed through a paper-making machine, where it is pressed and dried using heated rollers. Excess water is removed during this stage, and the material is formed into rolls of recycled paper ready for use.

title: Process: The diagram shows a process of making a storage area from a material called conc...
prompt: The diagram shows a process of making a storage area from a material called concrete canvas. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and making comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ mô tả quy trình tạo ra một khu lưu trữ bằng vật liệu gọi là concrete canvas.

→ Dạng: process (man-made, linear, nhiều bước)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The diagram illustrates the process of creating a storage area using concrete canvas.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Quy trình gồm 8 bước chính, từ sản xuất đến sử dụng
  • Có thể chia thành 3 giai đoạn:

→ manufacturing & delivery → installation → hardening & usage

  • Điểm nổi bật:

→ vật liệu ban đầu mềm (dạng cuộn) nhưng trở nên cứng sau khi được làm ướt

4. Body chia logic

BODY 1 – Manufacturing → Delivery → Preparation

  • Ban đầu:

→ concrete canvas được sản xuất với 3 lớp:

  • fibrous surface
  • dry concrete
  • waterproof layer
  • Sau đó:

→ vật liệu được cuộn lại và vận chuyển bằng xe tải

  • Khi đến nơi:

→ được unwrapped (mở ra)

BODY 2 – Installation \+ shaping

  • Tiếp theo:

→ tấm canvas được trải trên mặt đất

  • Sau đó:

→ được cố định bằng pegs

  • Một máy bơm:

→ pumps air into the structure

→ Kết quả:

  • tạo thành hình mái vòm (arched structure)

BODY 3 – Hardening \+ final use

  • Tiếp theo:

→ nước được phun lên bề mặt (hydrating)

  • Sau khoảng:

→ 20–24 hours, vật liệu sets and hardens

  • Cuối cùng:

→ cấu trúc được sử dụng như a storage area

5. Từ vựng ăn điểm

  • concrete canvas → vải bê tông
  • fibrous surface → bề mặt sợi
  • waterproof layer → lớp chống nước
  • unwrap → tháo cuộn
  • spread on the ground → trải trên mặt đất
  • peg → cọc cố định
  • pump air → bơm khí
  • hydrate → làm ướt
  • set / harden → đông cứng
  • storage facility → khu lưu trữ

The diagram illustrates how a storage area is constructed using concrete canvas.

Overall, the process involves eight main stages, beginning with the manufacture of the canvas and ending with a fully formed storage structure. Key steps include delivering and preparing the material, inflating it with air, hydrating it, and allowing it to harden.

Initially, the concrete canvas is produced with three layers: a fibrous top surface, a dry concrete layer, and a waterproof backing. The material is then rolled up and delivered to the construction site. After delivery, it is unwrapped and the protective cover is removed.

Next, the canvas is spread flat on the ground and secured in place using pegs, typically over a length of around 10 metres. An air pump is then used to inflate the canvas, giving it a curved, tunnel-like shape. Once the structure is formed, it is sprayed with water to activate the concrete.

After hydration, the material is left to set for 20 to 24 hours, during which it hardens into a solid structure. Finally, the completed storage area can be used for storing goods such as boxes.

title: Process: The diagram shows how tea leaves are processed into five tea types. Summarize th...
prompt: The diagram shows how tea leaves are processed into five tea types. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

How tea leaves are processed into five tea types

1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ mô tả quá trình chế biến lá trà để tạo ra 5 loại trà khác nhau.

→ Dạng: process (quy trình có phân nhánh)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • tea leaves – processed – five types

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / outlines
  • process → procedure

Ví dụ:

The diagram illustrates how tea leaves are processed to produce five different types of tea.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Tất cả các loại trà đều trải qua các bước chung ban đầu:

leaf growth → plucking → withering

  • Sau đó, quy trình phân nhánh thành các phương pháp khác nhau
  • Sự khác biệt chính nằm ở:

steaming / rolling / crushing / mức độ fermentation

  • Tất cả kết thúc bằng oven drying

→ Ý: khác nhau ở xử lý trung gian → tạo ra các loại trà khác nhau

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Giai đoạn chung & trà ít xử lý

Giai đoạn đầu (giống nhau):

  • Lá trà được:
  • trồng (growth)
  • hái (plucking)
  • làm héo (withering)

White tea:

  • Sau withering → đi thẳng đến drying

→ ít xử lý nhất

Green tea:

  • Sau withering → steaming
  • Sau đó:
  • rolling
  • drying

→ không có fermentation

BODY 2 – Trà lên men & xử lý mạnh hơn

Oolong tea:

  • After rolling → slight fermentation
  • Sau đó → drying

Black tea (2 loại):

  • Large-leaf black tea:
  • rolling → full fermentation → drying
  • Small-leaf black tea:
  • crushing (thay vì rolling)
  • → full fermentation
  • → drying

→ Ý: mức độ fermentation tăng dần từ oolong → black tea

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • undergo a process → trải qua quá trình
  • be subjected to → được xử lý bằng
  • fermentation → quá trình lên men
  • slight / full fermentation → lên men nhẹ / hoàn toàn
  • final stage → giai đoạn cuối
  • be dried in an oven → được sấy khô

The diagram illustrates how tea leaves are processed to produce five different types of tea.

Overall, all tea types follow the same initial stages—leaf growth, plucking, and withering—but differ in later processes such as steaming, rolling, fermentation, and crushing. The key differences lie in the level of fermentation, which determines the final type of tea.

After being harvested, the leaves are first withered to remove moisture. For green tea, the leaves are then steamed to prevent fermentation before being rolled and dried. In contrast, white tea undergoes minimal processing, as it is dried directly after withering without further treatment.

Oolong tea follows a more complex process: after rolling, the leaves undergo slight fermentation before oven drying. For black tea, the leaves are either rolled (for large-leaf black tea) or crushed (for small-leaf black tea), followed by full fermentation and final drying.

title: Process: The diagram shows the process for recycling plastic bottles. Summarise the infor...
prompt: The diagram shows the process for recycling plastic bottles. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ mô tả quy trình tái chế chai nhựa thành các sản phẩm mới.

→ Dạng: process (linear, multi-stage)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The diagram illustrates how plastic bottles are recycled to produce new products.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Quy trình gồm 9 bước, bắt đầu từ thu gom và kết thúc ở sản phẩm mới
  • Có thể chia thành 3 giai đoạn:

→ collection & sorting → processing → manufacturing new products

  • Điểm nổi bật:

→ chai nhựa được biến thành pellets, sau đó tạo thành raw material

4. Body chia logic

BODY 1 – Collection \+ sorting

  • Ban đầu:

→ used plastic bottles are placed in recycling bins

  • Sau đó:

→ được collected by trucks

  • Tại trung tâm tái chế:

→ chai được sorted manually

BODY 2 – Processing (mechanical treatment)

  • Tiếp theo:

→ chai được compressed into blocks

  • Sau đó:

→ được crushed into small pieces

  • Các mảnh này:

→ được washed to remove impurities

BODY 3 – Conversion into new materials \+ products

  • Sau khi làm sạch:

→ nhựa được processed into plastic pellets

  • Các pellets:

→ được heated to form raw material

  • Cuối cùng:

→ nguyên liệu này được used to manufacture new products

(e.g. bottles, T-shirts, bags, pencils)

5. Từ vựng ăn điểm

  • recycling bin → thùng tái chế
  • sorting → phân loại
  • compress → nén
  • crush → nghiền
  • wash → rửa
  • pellet → hạt nhựa
  • raw material → nguyên liệu thô
  • manufacture → sản xuất
  • end products → sản phẩm cuối

The diagram illustrates how plastic bottles are recycled into new products.

Overall, it is a nine-stage process that begins with the collection of used bottles and ends with the production of new items. The procedure involves sorting, compressing, cleaning, and transforming the material into reusable raw plastic.

Initially, discarded plastic bottles are placed in recycling bins and collected by trucks, which transport them to a recycling centre. At the facility, the bottles are sorted to remove unsuitable items. The selected bottles are then compressed into large blocks to facilitate handling and transportation.

Next, these blocks are crushed into small pieces, which are subsequently washed to eliminate impurities. The clean fragments are processed into plastic pellets, which are then heated to form raw plastic material.

In the final stages, this raw material is used to manufacture a variety of end products, such as new bottles, clothing, shopping bags, and stationery items.

title: Process: The diagrams below give information about the manufacture of frozen fish pies. S...
prompt: The diagrams below give information about the manufacture of frozen fish pies. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Fish pie production line

1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy các thành phần của một chiếc fish pie và quy trình sản xuất sản phẩm này trên dây chuyền công nghiệp.

→ Dạng: process (manufacturing process – nhiều nguyên liệu song song)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • fish pie – production

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / outlines
  • how → the process by which

Ví dụ:

The diagram illustrates the process by which fish pie is produced, along with its main ingredients.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Fish pie gồm các thành phần chính:

→ fish, peas, sauce và potato

  • Quy trình sản xuất gồm:

→ xử lý khoai tây và cá hồi riêng biệt

→ sau đó kết hợp tất cả nguyên liệu, đóng gói và đông lạnh

→ Đặc điểm:

  • có 2 dòng nguyên liệu song song
  • kết thúc bằng đóng gói và bảo quản lạnh

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Xử lý khoai tây

  • Khoai tây được vận chuyển đến nhà máy
  • Sau đó:

→ được rửa (clean)

→ gọt vỏ (peel)

→ cắt lát (slice)

  • Tiếp theo:

→ được luộc (boil)

  • Sau đó:

→ làm lạnh (chill)

→ và lưu trữ (store)

BODY 2 – Xử lý cá và lắp ráp sản phẩm

  • Cá hồi tươi:

→ được giao đến và xử lý trong vòng 12 giờ

  • Sau đó:

→ thêm lemon juice và muối

→ được hấp (steam oven)

  • Tiếp theo:

→ loại bỏ da và xương

→ kiểm tra chất lượng (inspect)

  • Sau đó:

→ kết hợp với:

  • đậu Hà Lan (peas)
  • nước sốt (sauce)
  • khoai tây đã chuẩn bị
  • Cuối cùng:

→ sản phẩm được đóng gói (wrap)

→ đông lạnh (freeze)

→ và lưu trữ hoặc phân phối (store/dispatch)

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

  • be delivered → được vận chuyển
  • be processed → được xử lý
  • be peeled / sliced → được gọt / cắt
  • be boiled → được luộc
  • be chilled → được làm lạnh
  • be seasoned → được nêm
  • be steamed → được hấp
  • be combined with → được kết hợp với
  • be packaged → được đóng gói
  • be frozen → được đông lạnh

The diagrams illustrate the ingredients of a fish pie and the industrial process used to produce it.

Overall, the dish consists of fish, peas, sauce and potato layered in a container, while the production line involves two parallel preparation streams—one for potatoes and one for fish—before final assembly, freezing and distribution.

In terms of ingredients, a fish pie is made by combining pieces of fish with peas and sauce, topped with sliced potatoes, all placed in a microwavable container.

Regarding the production process, potatoes are first delivered and can be stored for up to one month. They are then cleaned, peeled and sliced before being boiled, chilled and stored. In parallel, fresh salmon is delivered and processed within 12 hours. It is seasoned with lemon juice and salt, then cooked in a steam oven. After cooking, the skin and bones are removed, and the fish is inspected. Any waste generated during both streams is disposed of appropriately.

Finally, the prepared ingredients—including peas, sauce and potatoes—are assembled into containers. The finished pies are then wrapped, frozen, and stored or dispatched for distribution.

title: Pie Chart: The charts give information about world forests in five different regions. Summa...
prompt: The charts give information about world forests in five different regions. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main fea
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: World forest vs timber distribution by region

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ rừng trên thế giới và tỷ lệ sản lượng gỗ (timber) ở 5 khu vực: South America, Africa, Asia, Europe và North America.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 biểu đồ khác nội dung

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

world forest

timber

different regions

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

percentage → proportion / share

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the proportions of world forest and timber production across five different regions.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Africa và North America chiếm tỷ lệ rừng lớn nhất

North America có tỷ lệ timber cao nhất

Africa có nhiều rừng nhưng tỷ lệ timber thấp

Các khu vực khác có sự phân bố tương đối cân bằng

→ xu hướng: phân bố rừng không tương ứng hoàn toàn với sản lượng gỗ

4. BODY 1 – World forest

Africa: 27% → cao nhất

North America: 25%

Europe: 18%

South America: 16%

Asia: 14% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: rừng tập trung chủ yếu ở Africa và North America

5. BODY 2 – Timber

North America: 30% → cao nhất

South America: 23%

Asia: 18%

Europe: 20%

Africa: 9% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: North America dẫn đầu về timber, trong khi Africa dù nhiều rừng nhưng khai thác thấp

The two pie charts illustrate the distribution of the world’s forest area across five regions and the proportion of global timber production originating from these regions.

Overall, Africa contains the largest share of global forest, whereas North America is the leading producer of timber. In contrast, Africa contributes a disproportionately small share of timber despite its extensive forest coverage.

In terms of forest distribution, Africa accounts for the highest proportion at 27%, followed by North America at 25%. Europe represents 18% of global forest area, while South America and Asia contribute 16% and 14% respectively, making Asia the region with the smallest forest share.

However, the pattern for timber production differs considerably. North America dominates with 30% of global timber output, despite having a slightly smaller forest area than Africa. South America is the second-largest producer at 23%, followed by Europe and Asia at 20% and 18% respectively. Notably, Africa contributes only 9% of timber production, which is significantly lower than its share of forest resources.

title: Pie Chart: The charts show the difference in how three countries learn English in 2010 and ...
prompt: The charts show the difference in how three countries learn English in 2010 and 2015. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting t
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Study modes in three countries (2010 vs 2015)

1. Giải thích đề

Sáu biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ ba hình thức học (Evening class, Online, Abroad) ở ba quốc gia (A, B, C) vào năm 2010 và 2015.

→ Dạng: pie charts – nhiều đối tượng \+ nhiều mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

study modes

three countries

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

proportions → distribution

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of three study modes in three different countries in 2010 and 2015.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Evening classes chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở tất cả các quốc gia nhưng giảm theo thời gian

Online tăng mạnh, đặc biệt ở Country B và C

Abroad nhìn chung giảm hoặc chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

→ xu hướng: chuyển dịch từ học truyền thống sang học online

4. BODY 1 – Country A

Evening class:

70% → 67%

→ giảm nhẹ

Abroad:

25% → 23%

→ giảm nhẹ

Online:

5% → 10%

→ tăng gấp đôi

→ Ý chính: thay đổi nhỏ, online bắt đầu tăng

5. BODY 2 – Country B

Evening class:

72% → 48%

→ giảm mạnh

Online:

8% → 32%

→ tăng đáng kể

Abroad:

20% → 20%

→ không đổi

→ Ý chính: chuyển dịch rõ rệt sang học online

6. BODY 3 – Country C

Evening class:

94% → 82%

→ vẫn chiếm ưu thế nhưng giảm

Online:

6% → 15%

→ tăng rõ

Abroad:

0% → 3%

→ xuất hiện nhưng rất nhỏ

→ Ý chính: vẫn phụ thuộc vào học truyền thống nhưng có xu hướng đa dạng hóa

The six pie charts compare how students in three countries (A, B and C) participated in evening classes, online courses and study abroad in 2010 and 2015.

Overall, evening classes remained the dominant mode in all three countries, although their shares declined over time, particularly in Country B and C. Meanwhile, online learning increased markedly across all countries, whereas studying abroad showed mixed trends.

In Country A, evening classes accounted for the majority in both years, though this figure fell slightly from 70% in 2010 to 67% in 2015. Online learning doubled from 5% to 10%, while participation in studying abroad decreased marginally from 25% to 23%.

A more dramatic shift occurred in Country B. While evening classes dominated in 2010 at 72%, this proportion dropped sharply to 48% by 2015. In contrast, online learning rose significantly from 8% to 32%, becoming a major mode of study. The proportion of students studying abroad remained unchanged at 20%.

Country C showed a similar pattern to Country B, though less pronounced. Evening classes declined from an overwhelming 94% in 2010 to 82% in 2015. Online participation more than doubled from 6% to 15%, whereas the proportion studying abroad fell from a negligible level to just 3% in 2015.

title: Pie Chart: The charts show the sources of the electricity produced in 4 countries between 2...
prompt: The charts show the sources of the electricity produced in 4 countries between 2003 and 2008. Summarise the information by selecting and rep
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Electricity production sources (India, Sweden, Morocco, Vietnam)

1. Giải thích đề

Bốn biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ sản xuất điện từ ba nguồn (fossil fuel, hydro power, nuclear power) ở bốn quốc gia: Ấn Độ, Thụy Điển, Morocco và Việt Nam.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh nhiều đối tượng cùng thời điểm

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

electricity production

different sources

four countries

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

sources → types

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the proportions of electricity production from different sources in four countries.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Fossil fuel chiếm ưu thế ở Ấn Độ và Việt Nam

Hydro power là nguồn chính ở Morocco và Sweden

Nuclear power chỉ đáng kể ở Sweden và rất thấp hoặc không có ở các nước còn lại

→ xu hướng: các quốc gia có cơ cấu năng lượng rất khác nhau

4. BODY 1 – India & Vietnam

India:

Fossil fuel: 82% → cao nhất

Hydro: 14%

Nuclear: 4%

Vietnam:

Fossil fuel: 56% → cao nhất

Hydro: 44%

Nuclear: 0%

→ Ý chính: hai nước phụ thuộc nhiều vào fossil fuel

5. BODY 2 – Sweden & Morocco

Sweden:

Hydro: 52% → cao nhất

Nuclear: 44%

Fossil fuel: 4%

Morocco:

Hydro: 95% → áp đảo

Fossil fuel: 5%

Nuclear: 0%

→ Ý chính: Sweden đa dạng hơn, Morocco phụ thuộc gần như hoàn toàn vào hydro

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

dominate

be the primary source

have a negligible share

be heavily reliant on

The four pie charts illustrate the proportions of electricity generated from fossil fuels, hydro power and nuclear power in India, Sweden, Morocco and Vietnam between 2003 and 2008.

Overall, the dominant energy source varied markedly across countries. Fossil fuels accounted for the vast majority of electricity production in India and Vietnam, whereas hydro power was the primary source in Morocco and Sweden. Nuclear energy was only significant in Sweden and made a negligible or non-existent contribution in the other countries.

In India, electricity generation was heavily dependent on fossil fuels, which made up 82% of total production. Hydro power contributed a relatively small share at 14%, while nuclear energy accounted for just 4%. A similar reliance on fossil fuels can be seen in Vietnam, although to a lesser extent, with 56% of electricity derived from this source. The remaining 44% came entirely from hydro power, with no contribution from nuclear energy.

By contrast, Morocco relied almost exclusively on hydro power, which represented 95% of its electricity generation. Fossil fuels made up only 5%, and nuclear power was not used at all. Sweden displayed the most balanced energy mix among the four countries. Hydro power was the largest contributor at 52%, followed closely by nuclear energy at 44%, while fossil fuels accounted for a minimal 4%.

title: Pie Chart: The first chart below shows how energy is used in an average Australian househol...
prompt: The first chart below shows how energy is used in an average Australian household. The second chart shows the greenhouse gas emissions which
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Australian household energy use vs greenhouse gas emissions

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ sử dụng năng lượng trong hộ gia đình ở Úc và lượng khí thải nhà kính tương ứng theo từng hoạt động.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 biểu đồ khác nội dung

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

energy use

greenhouse gas emissions

households

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

use → consumption

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of energy use and the corresponding greenhouse gas emissions in Australian households.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Heating chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất về energy use nhưng không phải nguồn phát thải lớn nhất

Water heating và other appliances đóng góp nhiều vào emissions

Refrigeration và lighting tạo ra tỷ lệ phát thải cao hơn so với mức tiêu thụ năng lượng

Cooling chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ nhất

→ xu hướng: mức tiêu thụ năng lượng không tương ứng trực tiếp với lượng khí thải

4. BODY 1 – Energy use

Heating: 42% → cao nhất

Water heating: 30%

Other appliances: 15%

Refrigeration: 7%

Lighting: 4%

→ Ý chính: năng lượng chủ yếu dùng cho heating và water heating

5. BODY 2 – Greenhouse gas emissions

Water heating: 32% → cao nhất

Other appliances: 28%

Heating: 15% → giảm mạnh so với mức tiêu thụ

Refrigeration: 14%

Lighting: 8%

Cooling: 3% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: emissions tập trung vào water heating và appliances

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

contribute to

be responsible for

be disproportionately high/low

in contrast

The two pie charts compare the proportions of energy use in Australian households and the corresponding shares of greenhouse gas emissions by end use.

Overall, heating accounts for the largest share of energy consumption, whereas water heating is the biggest contributor to emissions. Notably, most other categories generate a higher proportion of emissions relative to the energy they consume, except for heating.

In terms of energy use, heating dominates at 42%, followed by water heating at 30%. Other appliances account for 15%, while refrigeration and lighting represent relatively small shares at 7% and 4%, respectively.

By contrast, water heating is the leading source of greenhouse gas emissions at 32%, closely followed by other appliances at 28%. Refrigeration contributes 14%, which is double its share of energy use, and lighting also produces a higher proportion of emissions (8%) compared to its energy consumption. Cooling, although not significant in energy use, accounts for 3% of emissions. Meanwhile, heating produces only 15% of emissions despite being the largest consumer of energy, indicating it is comparatively less emission-intensive.

title: Pie Chart: The graphs below show the age of visitors from the UK to Spain in 1983 and 2003....
prompt: The graphs below show the age of visitors from the UK to Spain in 1983 and 2003. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the ma
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Age distribution (1983 vs 2003)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy cơ cấu độ tuổi của dân số vào năm 1983 và 2003, gồm 5 nhóm: 0–15, 16–24, 25–34, 34–54 và 55+.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

age distribution

different age groups

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

distribution → proportion

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of the population across different age groups in 1983 and 2003.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Nhóm 34–54 chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai năm nhưng giảm

Nhóm 55+ tăng đáng kể

Nhóm 16–24 tăng mạnh

Nhóm 25–34 giảm rõ rệt

Nhóm 0–15 tăng nhẹ

→ xu hướng: dân số có xu hướng già hóa, với sự gia tăng ở nhóm lớn tuổi

4. BODY 1 – Nhóm lớn (34–54, 55+)

34–54:

50% → 35%

→ giảm mạnh nhưng vẫn lớn nhất

55+:

16% → 26%

→ tăng đáng kể

→ Ý chính: giảm ở nhóm trung niên, tăng ở nhóm cao tuổi

5. BODY 2 – Các nhóm còn lại

0–15:

10% → 12%

→ tăng nhẹ

16–24:

4% → 15%

→ tăng mạnh

25–34:

20% → 12%

→ giảm rõ rệt

→ Ý chính: nhóm trẻ biến động mạnh, đặc biệt 16–24 tăng nhanh

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

decline significantly

increase sharply

rise steadily

fall noticeably

The two pie charts compare the age distribution of a population in 1983 and 2003.

Overall, the proportion of people aged 34–54 remained the largest group in both years, although it declined notably over time. In contrast, the shares of the two oldest age groups increased, while younger and middle-aged adults showed mixed changes.

In 1983, individuals aged 34–54 accounted for exactly half of the population (50%), making this by far the dominant group. The next largest segment was those aged 25–34 at 20%, followed by the 55+ group at 16%. Younger people represented smaller proportions, with children aged 0–15 comprising 10% and those aged 16–24 making up just 4%.

By 2003, the proportion of 34–54-year-olds had fallen significantly to 35%, though it remained the largest category. Meanwhile, the percentage of people aged 55 and over rose markedly to 26%, becoming the second-largest group. The share of 16–24-year-olds also increased substantially to 15%, while children (0–15) experienced a slight rise to 12%. In contrast, the proportion of those aged 25–34 declined to 12%, indicating a shift in the population structure toward older age groups.

title: Pie Chart: The pie chart below shows the main reasons why students chose to study at a part...
prompt: The pie chart below shows the main reasons why students chose to study at a particular UK university in 1997 and 2007. Summarize the informa
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Reasons for choosing a university in the UK (1997 vs 2007)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy các lý do chính khiến sinh viên chọn một trường đại học ở Anh vào năm 1997 và 2007.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

reasons

students chose a university

UK

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

reasons → factors

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the main reasons why students chose a particular university in the UK in 1997 and 2007.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Suitable degree courses là lý do lớn nhất ở cả hai năm

Proximity to parental home tăng mạnh

Good sports and social activities giảm đáng kể

Các yếu tố khác thay đổi nhẹ

→ xu hướng: ưu tiên thực tế (course \+ location) tăng, yếu tố giải trí giảm

Từ khóa nên dùng:

remained the dominant factor

increased significantly

declined sharply

changed slightly

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Suitable degree & Proximity)

Suitable degree courses:

1997: 35% → 2007: 37%

→ vẫn là lý do lớn nhất, tăng nhẹ

Proximity to parental home:

1997: 10% → 2007: 22%

→ tăng mạnh

Ý chính: sinh viên ngày càng ưu tiên ngành học phù hợp và vị trí gần nhà

BODY 2 – Các yếu tố còn lại

Quality of resources:

21% → 17% (giảm nhẹ)

Quality of teaching:

15% → 18% (tăng nhẹ)

Good sports & social activities:

19% → 6% (giảm mạnh nhất)

Ý chính: các yếu tố học thuật thay đổi nhẹ, trong khi yếu tố giải trí giảm đáng kể

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

represent

increase significantly

decline sharply

remain the largest proportion

Ví dụ:

Suitable degree courses accounted for the largest proportion in both years.

The proportion of students choosing universities for proximity to home increased significantly.

Good sports and social activities declined sharply over the period.

Other factors showed only minor changes.

The two pie charts illustrate the primary reasons why students selected a particular university in the UK in 1997 and 2007.

Overall, suitable degree courses remained the most influential factor in both years, while proximity to home and teaching quality became more important over time. By contrast, good sports and social activities declined sharply, and the importance of resources decreased slightly.

In 1997, suitable degree courses dominated at 35%. This was followed by quality of resources (21%) and good sports and social activities (19%). Quality of teaching accounted for 15%, whereas proximity to parental home was the least significant factor at 10%.

By 2007, suitable degree courses rose marginally to 37%, maintaining its leading position. Proximity to parental home increased substantially to 22%, becoming the second most common reason. Quality of teaching also grew to 18%. In contrast, quality of resources fell slightly to 17%, while good sports and social activities dropped dramatically to just 6%, the lowest figure overall.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts below show the age groups of the users on different social media ...
prompt: The pie charts below show the age groups of the users on different social media platforms in Australia in 2011. Summarize the information by
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Social media age distribution (Facebook, YouTube, Twitter)

1. Giải thích đề

Ba biểu đồ tròn cho thấy phân bố độ tuổi người dùng trên ba nền tảng mạng xã hội: Facebook, YouTube và Twitter.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh nhiều đối tượng cùng thời điểm

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

age distribution

social media platforms

different age groups

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

distribution → proportion

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the age distribution of users on three social media platforms: Facebook, YouTube and Twitter.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Nhóm 18–34 và 35–49 chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất trên cả ba nền tảng

Twitter có tỷ lệ người 35–49 cao nhất

Nhóm 65+ chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ nhất ở tất cả các nền tảng

Cơ cấu độ tuổi tương đối giống nhau nhưng Twitter thiên về nhóm trung niên hơn

4. BODY 1 – Facebook & YouTube

Facebook:

18–34: 29% → cao nhất

35–49: 28%

10–17: 19%

50–64: 14%

65+: 10%

YouTube:

18–34: 30% → cao nhất

35–49: 26%

10–17: 20%

50–64: 15%

65+: 9%

→ Ý chính: hai nền tảng có cơ cấu khá tương đồng, tập trung vào người trẻ và trung niên

5. BODY 2 – Twitter

35–49: 40% → cao nhất

18–34: 29%

10–17: 18%

50–64: 9%

65+: 4% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: Twitter có xu hướng nghiêng về nhóm trung niên nhiều hơn

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

be the largest group

be followed by

have a similar distribution

be skewed towards

The three pie charts illustrate the age distribution of users on Facebook, YouTube and Twitter.

Overall, the 18–34 and 35–49 age groups account for the majority of users across all three platforms, while those aged 65 and over represent the smallest proportion. Notably, Twitter has a particularly high concentration of users aged 35–49.

On Facebook, the largest share of users falls into the 18–34 category at 29%, closely followed by those aged 35–49 at 28%. Teenagers (10–17) make up 19%, while the 50–64 group accounts for 14%. The smallest proportion belongs to users aged 65 and above, at 10%.

A similar pattern is seen on YouTube, where 18–34-year-olds again represent the largest group at 30%, followed by 35–49-year-olds at 26%. The proportions of teenagers and those aged 50–64 are 20% and 15% respectively, while seniors (65+) account for just 9%.

In contrast, Twitter shows a different distribution, with the 35–49 age group dominating at 40%, the highest figure among all platforms. Users aged 18–34 account for 29%, while teenagers represent 18%. The proportion of 50–64-year-olds is relatively low at 9%, and only 4% of users are aged 65 or over.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts below show the comparison of different kinds of energy production...
prompt: The pie charts below show the comparison of different kinds of energy production of France and Germany in 2020. Summarise the information by
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Energy production in France and Germany (2020)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn so sánh tỷ lệ sản xuất năng lượng theo 6 nguồn (Gas, Coal, Petrol, Nuclear, Solar, Other) ở Pháp và Đức vào năm 2020.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 đối tượng cùng thời điểm

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

energy production

different sources

France and Germany

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

kinds → types / sources

Mẫu:

The pie charts compare the proportions of energy production from different sources in France and Germany in 2020.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Gas và Solar chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai nước

Pháp phụ thuộc nhiều vào Nuclear hơn Đức

Đức sử dụng Coal và Petrol nhiều hơn

Other chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ nhất ở cả hai

→ xu hướng: Pháp thiên về năng lượng hạt nhân, trong khi Đức đa dạng hơn nhưng phụ thuộc nhiều hơn vào nhiên liệu hóa thạch

4. BODY 1 – France

Gas: 30% → cao nhất

Nuclear: 25%

Solar: 25%

→ cùng đứng thứ hai

Petrol: 10%

Coal: 7%

Other: 3% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: Pháp phụ thuộc nhiều vào Gas và Nuclear

5. BODY 2 – Germany

Solar: 28% → cao nhất

Gas: 25%

Nuclear: 18% → thấp hơn Pháp

Petrol: 14%

Coal: 10%

Other: 5% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: Đức cân bằng hơn nhưng dùng nhiều nhiên liệu hóa thạch hơn

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

be the largest contributor

have a higher/lower share

be considerably higher than

remain the smallest category

7. Ví dụ câu

Gas accounted for the largest proportion in France, at 30%.

Solar was the biggest energy source in Germany, making up 28%.

France had a significantly higher share of nuclear energy compared to Germany.

By contrast, Germany relied more on coal and petrol.

Other sources made up the smallest percentage in both countries.

The two pie charts compare the proportions of energy produced from six different sources in France and Germany in 2020.

Overall, gas and solar were the dominant sources in both countries, although France relied more heavily on gas, while Germany had a slightly higher proportion of solar energy. In addition, France generated a considerably larger share of its energy from nuclear power, whereas Germany depended more on coal and petrol.

In France, gas accounted for the largest proportion at 30%. Nuclear and solar energy each made up 25%, jointly representing half of total production. Petrol contributed 10%, followed by coal at 7%, while other sources were minimal at 3%.

In Germany, solar energy was the leading source at 28%, closely followed by gas at 25%. Nuclear power was less significant than in France, at 18%. Petrol and coal accounted for 14% and 10% respectively, both higher than the corresponding figures in France. Other sources also made up a slightly larger share in Germany, at 5%.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts below show the different types of courses, which were followed by...
prompt: The pie charts below show the different types of courses, which were followed by the students during the years of 1984, 1994 and 2004. Summa
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Courses followed by students (1984–2004)

1. Giải thích đề

Ba biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ sinh viên theo học bốn loại khóa học (face-to-face, correspondence, mixed media, online) vào các năm 1984, 1994 và 2004.

→ Dạng: pie charts – nhiều mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

courses followed

different types

three years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

followed → taken

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the proportions of students taking different types of courses in 1984, 1994 and 2004.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Face-to-face courses giảm mạnh nhưng vẫn chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất

Mixed media tăng đáng kể

Online xuất hiện từ 1994 và duy trì ở mức thấp

Correspondence giảm nhẹ

→ xu hướng: chuyển dịch từ học trực tiếp sang các hình thức linh hoạt hơn

4. BODY 1 – Face-to-face & Mixed media

Face-to-face:

67% → 54% → 40%

→ giảm liên tục

Mixed media:

13% → 15% → 35%

→ tăng mạnh

→ Ý chính: sự thay đổi rõ rệt từ học truyền thống sang kết hợp

5. BODY 2 – Correspondence & Online

Correspondence:

20% → 20% → 15%

→ ổn định rồi giảm

Online:

0% → 11% → 10%

→ xuất hiện và duy trì

→ Ý chính: online trở thành một lựa chọn mới nhưng chưa chiếm ưu thế

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

decline steadily

increase significantly

emerge

remain relatively low

The three pie charts illustrate the proportions of students taking four types of courses—face-to-face, correspondence, mixed media, and online—in 1984, 1994 and 2004.

Overall, face-to-face courses were the dominant mode at the beginning of the period but declined steadily over time, while mixed media courses rose significantly to become the most popular by 2004. Meanwhile, online learning emerged only in 1994 and remained a relatively small segment, and correspondence courses showed a slight downward trend.

In 1984, traditional face-to-face courses accounted for a substantial majority at 67%. Correspondence courses made up 20%, while mixed media courses represented a smaller share at 13%. There was no participation in online courses at this point.

By 1994, the proportion of face-to-face learning had fallen to 54%, although it still remained the largest category. Mixed media courses increased modestly to 15%, matching the share of correspondence courses, which stayed unchanged at 20%. Notably, online courses appeared for the first time, accounting for 11% of all enrolments.

In 2004, the trend shifted further. Face-to-face courses declined sharply to 40%, while mixed media courses rose markedly to 35%, becoming nearly as popular as traditional learning. Correspondence courses decreased slightly to 15%, and online courses remained relatively limited at 10%, showing only marginal change since 1994.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts below show the percentage of five kinds of books sold by a bookse...
prompt: The pie charts below show the percentage of five kinds of books sold by a bookseller between 1972 and 2012. Summarize the information by sel
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Book sales distribution (1972, 1992, 2012)

Giải thích đề

Ba biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ doanh số sách theo 5 thể loại (Adult Fiction, Children’s Fiction, Biography, Travel, Others) vào các năm 1972, 1992 và 2012.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh nhiều mốc thời gian

Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

book sales distribution

different categories

three years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

distribution → proportion / breakdown

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of book sales across five categories in 1972, 1992, and 2012.

Overview (ý chính)

Adult Fiction chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất và tăng mạnh theo thời gian

Children’s Fiction tăng nhẹ

Biography và Travel giảm đáng kể

Others giảm dần

→ xu hướng: thị trường ngày càng tập trung vào Adult Fiction, trong khi các thể loại khác mất dần thị phần

Từ khóa nên dùng:

accounted for the largest share

increased significantly

declined sharply

experienced a downward trend

BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Adult Fiction, Children’s Fiction)

Adult Fiction:

20% → 25% → 45%

→ tăng mạnh, đặc biệt giai đoạn sau

Children’s Fiction:

20% → 22% → 25%

→ tăng nhẹ, ổn định

Ý chính: Fiction (đặc biệt Adult Fiction) ngày càng chiếm ưu thế trên thị trường

BODY 2 – Các nhóm còn lại (Biography, Travel, Others)

Biography:

20% → 15% → 8%

→ giảm đáng kể

Travel:

15% → 18% → 10%

→ tăng nhẹ rồi giảm mạnh

Others:

25% → 20% → 12%

→ giảm đều theo thời gian

Ý chính: các thể loại ngoài fiction nhìn chung suy giảm, đặc biệt Biography

Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

rise dramatically

decline steadily

drop sharply

experience fluctuations

Ví dụ câu

Adult fiction accounted for the largest proportion and increased dramatically over the period.

Children’s fiction showed a gradual rise.

In contrast, the figure for biography declined significantly.

Travel experienced a slight increase before dropping sharply in 2012.

The proportion of other categories decreased steadily over time.

The three pie charts illustrate the distribution of book sales across five categories—adult fiction, children’s fiction, biography, travel, and others—in 1972, 1992, and 2012.

Overall, adult fiction increased substantially to become the dominant category by 2012, while biography and travel declined over time. In contrast, children’s fiction rose moderately, whereas the proportion of other books decreased steadily.

In 1972, sales were relatively evenly distributed, with “others” accounting for the largest share at 25%. Adult fiction represented 20%, equal to children’s fiction and biography, while travel made up the smallest proportion at 15%. By 1992, adult fiction had risen to 25%, becoming the leading category. Children’s fiction also increased slightly to 22%, whereas biography fell to 15%. Travel edged up to 18%, while the share of other books declined to 20%.

By 2012, adult fiction had grown markedly to 45%, far exceeding all other categories. Children’s fiction continued its upward trend, reaching 25%. In contrast, biography dropped sharply to just 8%, and travel decreased to 10%. The proportion of other books also fell significantly to 12%, indicating a clear shift in consumer preferences toward fiction, particularly adult titles.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts show energy sources in Australia in 2008 and predictions for 2030...
prompt: The pie charts show energy sources in Australia in 2008 and predictions for 2030. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the m
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Energy sources (2008 vs 2030)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ các nguồn năng lượng khác nhau vào năm 2008 và dự đoán cho năm 2030.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh hiện tại và tương lai

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

energy sources

different types

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

use → consumption / distribution

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of different energy sources in 2008 and projections for 2030.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Coal là nguồn lớn nhất năm 2008 nhưng giảm mạnh vào 2030

Oil và Gas tăng và trở thành hai nguồn chính

Năng lượng tái tạo (solar, wind, hydro, geothermal) tăng nhẹ nhưng vẫn chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

→ xu hướng: giảm phụ thuộc vào coal, chuyển sang oil, gas và năng lượng sạch

4. BODY 1 – Nguồn chính (Coal, Oil, Gas)

Coal:

39% → 22.3%

→ giảm mạnh

Oil:

32.5% → 35.5%

→ tăng nhẹ, cao nhất vào 2030

Gas:

22.9% → 32%

→ tăng đáng kể

→ Ý chính: sự chuyển dịch từ coal sang oil và gas

5. BODY 2 – Các nguồn còn lại (Renewables & Others)

Others:

4.3% → 4.3%

→ không đổi

Solar:

0.8% → (gần như không còn đáng kể)

Wind:

0.3% → 0.9%

→ tăng nhẹ

Hydro:

0.2% → 0.7%

→ tăng

Geothermal:

0% → 4.3%

→ xuất hiện đáng kể

→ Ý chính: năng lượng tái tạo tăng nhưng vẫn chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

The two pie charts compare the proportions of energy production by source in 2008 and projected figures for 2030.

Overall, fossil fuels remain dominant in both years, although their combined share is expected to decline slightly. Coal is predicted to decrease substantially, while gas and oil will become more prominent. Renewable sources, though still minor, are projected to grow, particularly geothermal energy.

In 2008, coal was the leading source at 39%, followed by oil at 32.5% and gas at 22.9%. Other sources accounted for 4.3%. Renewable energy made up only a negligible proportion, with solar at 0.8%, wind at 0.3%, and hydro at 0.2%.

By 2030, oil is forecast to become the largest contributor at 35.5%, while gas will increase significantly to 32%. In contrast, coal is expected to fall sharply to 22.3%. The proportion of other sources remains unchanged at 4.3%. Among renewables, geothermal energy will emerge at 4.3%, while wind and hydro are projected to rise modestly to 0.9% and 0.7% respectively.

title: Pie Chart: The pie charts show people’s reasons for immigration to a European country in 20...
prompt: The pie charts show people’s reasons for immigration to a European country in 2009 and reasons for emigration from a European country in 200
guideHtml:
Hướng dẫn viết bài

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Reasons for immigration (pie charts)

TID sẽ chỉ bạn cách xử lý dạng double pie chart này sao cho overview rõ ý và body không bị kể số liệu nha.

1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy:

  • lý do nhập cư vào một quốc gia châu Âu
  • và lý do rời khỏi quốc gia đó

trong năm 2009.

TID nhắc bạn:

Với pie chart, điều quan trọng nhất là:

  • tìm category lớn nhất
  • nhỏ nhất
  • nhóm nào giống nhau
  • điểm khác biệt nổi bật giữa 2 biểu đồ

2. Cách viết Introduction

Paraphrase:

  • reasons for immigration / emigration
  • a European country
  • in 2009

Viết 1 câu ngắn là đủ nha.

3. Cách viết Overview

TID gợi ý overview nên tập trung vào:

  • Job-related reasons chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai charts
  • “No reason” là thấp nhất
  • People leaving the country có xu hướng đi vì “definite job” nhiều hơn
  • Chart “from the country” có sự chênh lệch lớn hơn giữa các categories

TID lưu ý:

Overview chỉ cần:

  • biggest
  • smallest
  • xu hướng chung
  • 1 điểm khác biệt nổi bật

Không cần nhiều số liệu nha.

4. Cách chia Body

BODY 1 – Immigration TO the country

Nhóm:

  • category lớn nhất
  • các nhóm bằng nhau
  • category nhỏ nhất

TID gợi ý:

Chart này khá balanced nên có thể:

  • viết largest category trước
  • gom 3 categories bằng nhau vào cùng 1 câu
  • kết thúc bằng smallest category

BODY 2 – Immigration FROM the country

Tập trung vào:

  • “Definite job” vượt trội rõ rệt
  • các category trung bình
  • smallest category

TID lưu ý:

Body 2 nên nhấn mạnh:

  • sự dominance của “definite job”
  • một vài categories giảm so với chart đầu

5. Những điểm nên so sánh

TID gợi ý vài ý so sánh dễ ăn điểm:

  • Definite job tăng mạnh ở chart “from”
  • Study giảm đáng kể
  • Accompany/family giảm
  • No reason luôn thấp nhất
  • Employment-related purposes chiếm phần lớn ở cả hai charts

6. Gợi ý paraphrase

TID gợi ý một số cách diễn đạt:

  • accounted for
  • represented
  • made up
  • the largest proportion
  • a relatively small percentage
  • employment-related reasons
  • people migrating for work
  • by comparison
  • whereas / while

TID lưu ý:

Pie chart không cần viết quá nhiều số.

Quan trọng là:

  • grouping thông minh
  • overview rõ
  • so sánh có chọn lọc nha.

The two pie charts illustrate the reasons why people immigrated to and emigrated from a European country in 2009.

Overall, employment-related reasons accounted for the largest proportions in both charts, particularly having a definite job. By contrast, migrating without a specific reason represented the smallest percentage in each case. In addition, people leaving the country were more likely to move for a definite job than those entering it.

Regarding immigration to the European country, accompanying family members was the most common reason, accounting for 25% of immigrants. This was followed closely by people who moved for a definite job at 23%. Meanwhile, study, looking for work and other reasons each represented an identical figure of 15%. Only a small minority of immigrants, at 7%, moved without giving a particular reason.

As for emigration from the country, having a definite job became significantly more dominant, making up 38% of all cases. Other reasons accounted for 23%, while accompanying family represented 15%. The proportion of people leaving to look for work stood at 13%, compared to just 7% for study purposes. Similar to the first chart, no reason was the least common category, at only 4%.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below give information about the number of people who use public libr...
prompt: The charts below give information about the number of people who use public libraries and the main reasons to visit in Britain in 1991 and i
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Reasons for library visits (1991 vs 2000)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy lý do người dân đến thư viện vào năm 1991 và 2000, với tổng số lượt là 290 triệu mỗi năm.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

library visits

reasons

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

reasons → purposes

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the reasons why people visited libraries in 1991 and 2000.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Borrowing/returning books chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai năm nhưng giảm

Obtaining information tăng đáng kể

Study giảm rõ rệt

Reading (newspapers/magazines) tăng nhẹ

Xuất hiện thêm borrowing videos vào năm 2000

→ xu hướng: thư viện chuyển từ chức năng truyền thống sang cung cấp thông tin nhiều hơn

4. BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Borrow books, Obtain information)

Borrow or return books:

65% → 55%

→ giảm nhưng vẫn lớn nhất

Obtain information:

10% → 20%

→ tăng mạnh, gấp đôi

→ Ý chính: vai trò của việc tìm kiếm thông tin ngày càng quan trọng

5. BODY 2 – Các hoạt động còn lại

Study:

10% → 5%

→ giảm một nửa

Read newspapers/magazines:

15% → 18%

→ tăng nhẹ

Borrow or return videos:

0% → 2%

→ xuất hiện mới

→ Ý chính: các hoạt động học tập giảm, trong khi giải trí/thông tin tăng

The two pie charts compare how visitors used a library in 1991 and 2000, with the total number of visits remaining unchanged at 290 million in both years.

Overall, borrowing or returning books remained the primary purpose in both years, although its share declined slightly. In contrast, obtaining information became significantly more common, while studying decreased. New activity in the form of borrowing or returning videos appeared in 2000.

In 1991, a substantial 65% of visitors went to the library to borrow or return books, making it by far the dominant activity. Reading newspapers or magazines accounted for 15%, while both studying and obtaining information each represented 10% of visits.

By 2000, the proportion of visitors borrowing or returning books had fallen to 55%, though it still remained the largest category. Obtaining information doubled to 20%, becoming the second most common purpose. Reading newspapers or magazines increased slightly to 18%. In contrast, studying declined sharply to just 5%. Additionally, borrowing or returning videos emerged as a new activity, accounting for a small proportion of 2%.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show changes in the proportion of energy produced from differen...
prompt: The charts below show changes in the proportion of energy produced from different resources in a country in 1985 and 2003. Summarise the inf
guideHtml:
ELTS Writing Task 1 – Energy production by resource (pie charts)

TID sẽ chỉ bạn cách xử lý dạng double pie chart này sao cho overview rõ và không bị liệt kê số liệu nha.

1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy tỷ lệ sản xuất năng lượng từ 6 nguồn khác nhau:

  • Hydrogen
  • Natural gas
  • Oil
  • Other renewable
  • Coal
  • Nuclear

trong hai năm:

  • 1985
  • 2003

TID nhắc bạn:

Với pie chart theo thời gian, bạn cần tập trung vào:

  • nguồn lớn nhất / nhỏ nhất
  • nguồn tăng mạnh
  • nguồn giảm mạnh
  • sự thay đổi tổng thể giữa 2 năm

2. Cách viết Introduction

Paraphrase:

  • energy production
  • by different resources
  • in 1985 and 2003

Viết 1 câu ngắn gọn là đủ nha.

3. Cách viết Overview

TID gợi ý overview như này:

  • Natural gas remained the largest source in both years
  • Oil and coal increased significantly over the period
  • Nuclear energy declined considerably
  • Hydrogen stayed unchanged and other renewable sources remained relatively small

TID lưu ý:

Overview chỉ cần:

  • biggest
  • xu hướng chính
  • nguồn tăng / giảm nổi bật

Không cần nhét quá nhiều số nha.

4. Cách chia Body

BODY 1 – Major energy sources

Tập trung vào:

  • Natural gas
  • Oil
  • Coal

TID gợi ý:

Đây là các nguồn chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất nên viết trước.

Natural gas:

  • giảm từ 52% → 39%
  • nhưng vẫn đứng đầu

Oil:

  • tăng đáng kể từ 13% → 23%

Coal:

  • tăng mạnh từ 8% → 22%

TID lưu ý:

Coal và oil là 2 nguồn tăng nổi bật nhất nha.

BODY 2 – Remaining sources

Hydrogen:

  • giữ nguyên 4%

Other renewable:

  • tăng nhẹ từ 1% → 4%

Nuclear:

  • giảm mạnh từ 22% → 8%

TID gợi ý:

Body này nên nhấn mạnh:

  • nuclear giảm đáng kể
  • renewable tăng nhưng vẫn thấp

5. Những điểm nên highlight

TID gợi ý vài ý dễ ăn điểm:

  • Natural gas remained dominant despite a decline
  • Coal almost tripled
  • Oil nearly doubled
  • Nuclear dropped sharply
  • Renewable sources still accounted for only a small proportion in 2003

The two pie charts compare the proportions of energy production from six different resources in 1985 and 2003.

Overall, natural gas remained the dominant source of energy production in both years, despite a noticeable decline over the period. By contrast, coal and oil experienced significant growth, while nuclear energy saw a substantial decrease. Hydrogen remained unchanged, and other renewable sources continued to account for relatively small percentages.

In 1985, natural gas was by far the largest energy source, representing 52% of total production. Nuclear energy accounted for 22%, making it the second most important resource, while oil contributed 13%. Coal made up a relatively modest proportion at 8%, whereas hydrogen and other renewable sources represented only 4% and 1% respectively.

By 2003, the proportion of energy generated from natural gas had fallen to 39%, although it still remained the leading source. Oil and coal both increased considerably, rising to 23% and 22% respectively. In contrast, nuclear energy declined sharply to just 8%. Hydrogen remained stable at 4%, while the figure for other renewable resources rose slightly to 4%.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show the average percentages in typical meals of three types of...
prompt: The charts below show the average percentages in typical meals of three types of nutrients, all of which may be unhealthy if eaten too much.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Sodium, saturated fat and added sugar in meals (USA)

1. Giải thích đề

Ba biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ trung bình của sodium, saturated fat và added sugar trong bốn bữa ăn (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks) tại Mỹ.

→ Dạng: pie charts – nhiều biểu đồ, so sánh theo danh mục

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

average percentages

nutrients

different meals

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

consumed → intake

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the average percentages of sodium, saturated fat and added sugar consumed in different meals in the USA.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Dinner chiếm tỷ lệ cao nhất về sodium và saturated fat

Snacks chiếm tỷ lệ cao nhất về added sugar

Breakfast luôn thấp nhất hoặc gần thấp nhất ở cả ba loại

→ xu hướng: bữa tối và đồ ăn vặt đóng góp nhiều nhất vào các chất không lành mạnh

4. BODY 1 – Sodium & Saturated fat

Sodium:

Dinner: 43% → cao nhất

Lunch: 29%

Breakfast & Snacks: 14%

Saturated fat:

Dinner: 37% → cao nhất

Lunch: 26%

Snacks: 21%

Breakfast: 16%

→ Ý chính: Dinner là nguồn chính của sodium và saturated fat

5. BODY 2 – Added sugar

Snacks: 42% → cao nhất

Dinner: 23%

Lunch: 19%

Breakfast: 16% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: Added sugar chủ yếu đến từ snacks

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

be the largest contributor

be highest in

constitute

be the smallest proportion

The three pie charts illustrate the average proportions of sodium, saturated fat and added sugar contained in four typical meals—breakfast, lunch, dinner and snacks—in the USA.

Overall, dinner contributes the highest share of sodium and saturated fat, whereas added sugar is predominantly consumed in snacks. By contrast, breakfast consistently accounts for the smallest proportions across all three nutrients.

In terms of sodium, dinner makes up the largest proportion at 43%, followed by lunch at 29%, while both breakfast and snacks contribute equally at 14% each. A similar pattern is observed for saturated fat, with dinner again the highest at 37%, and lunch representing 26%. Snacks account for 21%, whereas breakfast has the lowest share at 16%.

By comparison, added sugar is mainly consumed through snacks, which constitute a substantial 42%. Dinner and lunch contribute more moderate proportions, at 23% and 19% respectively, while breakfast remains the lowest at 16%. This indicates a clear shift in dietary patterns, with sugar intake concentrated outside main meals, unlike sodium and fat, which are primarily associated with dinner.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show the percentage of students at an adult education centre ta...
prompt: The charts below show the percentage of students at an adult education centre taking various courses offered in 1985 and this year. Summaris
guideHtml:
Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Course enrolment comparison (1985 vs Current year)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn so sánh tỷ lệ đăng ký các khóa học vào năm 1985 và hiện tại.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 thời điểm (có thay đổi danh mục)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

course enrolment

comparison

two time periods

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

enrolment → participation

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of course enrolments in 1985 and in the current year.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Fitness and dance giữ nguyên tỷ lệ

IT xuất hiện và trở thành lớn nhất

Modern languages giảm

Cookery tăng

Một số môn biến mất (music appreciation, local history)

Từ khóa nên dùng:

remained unchanged

emerged as the largest category

declined

increased

disappeared

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Các môn giữ hoặc tăng

Fitness and dance:

22% → 22% (không đổi)

Cookery:

14% → 19% (tăng)

Art:

17% → 15% (giảm nhẹ)

IT:

không có năm 1985 → 27% (cao nhất hiện tại)

Ý chính: IT trở thành môn phổ biến nhất, cookery tăng, fitness ổn định

BODY 2 – Các môn giảm hoặc biến mất

Modern languages:

24% → 17% (giảm đáng kể)

Music appreciation & Local history:

8% \+ 15% (1985) → không còn trong năm hiện tại

Ý chính: một số môn truyền thống giảm hoặc biến mất, cho thấy sự thay đổi sở thích học tập

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase moderately

decline significantly

remain stable

be replaced by

emerge as

Ví dụ:

IT emerged as the largest category in the current year.

Fitness and dance remained unchanged over time.

Modern languages declined significantly.

Some subjects were replaced by newer courses such as IT.

The two pie charts compare course enrolment in 1985 and the current year across several subjects.

Overall, while fitness and dance maintained its position as one of the most popular courses, there was a clear shift towards technology-related study, with IT becoming the dominant subject in the current year. In contrast, traditional subjects such as local history and music appreciation disappeared entirely.

In 1985, modern languages accounted for the largest proportion of enrolments at 24%, followed by fitness and dance at 22%. Art represented 17%, while cookery made up 14%. Local history and music appreciation were less popular, at 15% and 8% respectively.

In the current year, IT has emerged as the most popular course, comprising 27% of enrolments. Cookery increased to 19%, while fitness and dance remained unchanged at 22%. Art declined slightly to 15%, and modern languages fell to 17%. Notably, local history and music appreciation are no longer present in the distribution.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show the percentage of volunteers by organizations in 2008 and ...
prompt: The charts below show the percentage of volunteers by organizations in 2008 and 2014. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting t
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Participation in six activities (2008 vs 2014)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ người tham gia sáu loại hoạt động (Environmental, Art, Sport, Health Care, Educational, Others) vào năm 2008 và 2014.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

participation

different activities

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

participation → involvement

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the proportions of participation in six different activities in 2008 and 2014.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Environmental và Sport tăng đáng kể và trở thành hai nhóm lớn nhất

Educational giảm rõ rệt

Art giảm nhẹ

Health Care và Others chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ và thay đổi không đáng kể

→ xu hướng: chuyển dịch sang các hoạt động thể chất và môi trường

4. BODY 1 – Nhóm tăng (Environmental, Sport)

Environmental:

21% → 29%

→ tăng mạnh

Sport:

15% → 25%

→ tăng đáng kể

→ Ý chính: hai hoạt động này trở nên phổ biến hơn

5. BODY 2 – Nhóm còn lại

Art:

18% → 12%

→ giảm

Educational:

24% → 17%

→ giảm rõ

Health Care:

7% → 8%

→ tăng nhẹ

Others:

15% → 9%

→ giảm

→ Ý chính: các hoạt động học thuật và khác giảm, trong khi health care gần như ổn định

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase significantly

decline noticeably

rise steadily

remain relatively stable

The two pie charts compare the proportions of volunteers working in six different sectors in 2008 and 2014.

Overall, environmental and sports-related volunteering increased over the period, while participation in art, educational activities, and “other” categories declined. The most notable change was the rise of environmental work, which became the largest sector by 2014.

In 2008, educational volunteering accounted for the largest share at 24%, followed by environmental work at 21% and art at 18%. Sport and “other” activities each represented 15%, while health care was the least common sector at just 7%.

By 2014, environmental volunteering had grown significantly to 29%, overtaking all other categories. Sport also increased markedly to 25%, becoming the second most popular sector. In contrast, art saw a noticeable decline to 12%, and educational activities fell to 17%. Health care rose slightly from 7% to 8%, whereas the proportion of volunteers involved in other activities dropped considerably to 9%.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show the proportion of holidaymakers using four different types...
prompt: The charts below show the proportion of holidaymakers using four different types of accommodation in three different years. Summarize the in
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Holiday accommodation (1988, 1998, 2008)

1. Giải thích đề

Ba biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ các loại hình lưu trú khi đi nghỉ (staying with family, rented apartment, camping, hotels) vào các năm 1988, 1998 và 2008.

→ Dạng: pie charts – nhiều mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

holiday accommodation

different types

three years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

types → categories

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the distribution of different types of holiday accommodation in 1988, 1998 and 2008.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Staying with family chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả ba năm nhưng giảm vào 2008

Rented apartment tăng mạnh và trở thành lựa chọn phổ biến hơn

Camping giảm dần

Hotels tăng nhẹ rồi ổn định

→ xu hướng: chuyển từ ở với gia đình sang thuê căn hộ

4. BODY 1 – Staying with family & Rented apartment

Staying with family:

52% → 54% → 44%

→ cao nhất nhưng giảm vào giai đoạn cuối

Rented apartment:

7% → 11% → 22%

→ tăng mạnh, đặc biệt đến 2008

→ Ý chính: sự gia tăng rõ rệt của hình thức thuê căn hộ

5. BODY 2 – Camping & Hotels

Camping:

26% → 13% → 12%

→ giảm đáng kể

Hotels:

15% → 22% → 22%

→ tăng rồi giữ nguyên

→ Ý chính: camping mất dần phổ biến, hotels ổn định

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase steadily

decline significantly

remain stable

become more popular

The three pie charts illustrate the proportions of tourists choosing four types of accommodation—staying with family, rented apartments, camping and hotels—in 1988, 1998 and 2008.

Overall, staying with family was consistently the most popular option across the period, although its share declined slightly by 2008. In contrast, rented apartments became increasingly popular, while camping experienced a steady decrease. The proportion of tourists staying in hotels rose initially and then remained stable.

In 1988, over half of tourists (52%) stayed with family, making it by far the dominant choice. Camping was the second most common option at 26%, followed by hotels at 15%. Only a small minority, 7%, chose rented apartments.

By 1998, the proportion of those staying with family increased marginally to 54%, while hotel use rose to 22%. Meanwhile, camping dropped significantly to 13%. Rented apartments saw a modest increase to 11%, though they remained the least preferred option.

In 2008, staying with family declined to 44%, though it still ranked first. Rented apartments, however, doubled from the previous figure to 22%, equalling the proportion for hotels, which remained unchanged at 22%. Camping continued its downward trend, falling slightly further to 12%.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below show the reasons for immigrants to and from a European country ...
prompt: The charts below show the reasons for immigrants to and from a European country in 2009. Summarise the information by selecting and reportin
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Reasons for immigrants to and from a European country (2009)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy lý do người nhập cư đến và rời khỏi một quốc gia châu Âu vào năm 2009.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 biểu đồ khác đối tượng

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

reasons for immigrants

to and from

a European country

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

reasons → purposes

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the reasons why people immigrated to and emigrated from a European country in 2009.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Có việc làm chắc chắn là lý do phổ biến nhất ở cả hai nhóm

Tỷ lệ này cao hơn đáng kể ở người rời đi

Study và accompany/family phổ biến hơn ở người đến

No reason chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ nhất ở cả hai

→ xu hướng: việc làm là động lực chính, nhưng mục đích khác nhau giữa hai nhóm

4. BODY 1 – Immigrants to the country

Definite job: 23%

Accompany/Family: 25% → cao nhất

Look for job: 15%

Study: 15%

Other reasons: 15%

No reason: 7% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: lý do khá phân tán, với family là cao nhất

5. BODY 2 – Immigrants from the country

Definite job: 38% → cao nhất

Other reasons: 23%

Accompany/Family: 15%

Look for job: 13%

Study: 7%

No reason: 4% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: rời đi chủ yếu vì công việc

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

be the main reason

be more/less common

represent

constitute

The two pie charts illustrate the main reasons for immigrants moving to and from a European country in 2009.

Overall, employment-related reasons were the dominant factor in both directions, particularly among those leaving the country. In contrast, study and family-related motives were more significant for incoming migrants.

For immigration to the country, the largest proportion of people moved for a definite job, accounting for 23%. This was followed by those accompanying family members at 25%, which slightly exceeded the figure for employment. Three other categories—looking for a job, studying, and other reasons—each represented an equal share of 15%, while only 7% migrated with no specific reason.

By comparison, for emigration, having a definite job was by far the most common reason, rising markedly to 38%. Other reasons also increased to 23%, making it the second-largest category. The proportion of people accompanying family members stood at 15%, whereas those leaving to look for work accounted for 13%. Study was a relatively minor factor at 7%, and only 4% of individuals left without a clear reason.

title: Pie Chart: The charts below shows the global market share of three computer operating syste...
prompt: The charts below shows the global market share of three computer operating systems (OS) from 2012 to 2015. Summarize the information by sele
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Global market share of computer operating systems (2012–2015)

1. Giải thích đề

Bốn biểu đồ tròn cho thấy thị phần toàn cầu của ba hệ điều hành (OS1, OS2, OS3) và các hệ khác trong giai đoạn từ 2012 đến 2015.

→ Dạng: pie charts – nhiều mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

global market share

operating systems

four years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

market share → proportion

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate the global market share of three operating systems and other platforms between 2012 and 2015.

3. Overview (ý chính)

OS1 tăng mạnh và trở thành hệ điều hành chiếm ưu thế

Others giảm đáng kể qua thời gian

OS2 tương đối ổn định

OS3 giảm rồi tăng nhẹ trở lại

→ xu hướng: thị trường dần tập trung vào một hệ điều hành chính

4. BODY 1 – OS1 và Others

OS1:

20% → 38% → 47% → 52%

→ tăng mạnh liên tục, trở thành lớn nhất

Others:

48% → 35% → 23% → 15%

→ giảm mạnh

→ Ý chính: OS1 thay thế dần các hệ khác

5. BODY 2 – OS2 và OS3

OS2:

17% → 17% → 18% → 18%

→ gần như không đổi

OS3:

15% → 10% → 12% → 15%

→ giảm rồi phục hồi

→ Ý chính: hai hệ này ổn định hơn, ít biến động

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase steadily

decline sharply

remain stable

recover slightly

The four pie charts illustrate the global market shares of three computer operating systems (OS1, OS2 and OS3) as well as other systems from 2012 to 2015.

Overall, OS1 rose sharply to become the dominant system by 2015, while the share of other operating systems declined markedly. In contrast, OS2 and OS3 showed relatively minor fluctuations over the period.

In 2012, other systems accounted for the largest share at 48%, followed by OS1 at 20%. OS2 and OS3 represented 17% and 15% respectively. By 2013, OS1 had increased dramatically to 38%, overtaking others, whose share fell to 35%. OS2 remained unchanged at 17%, while OS3 declined to 10%.

The upward trend of OS1 continued in 2014, reaching 47%, before peaking at 52% in 2015. Meanwhile, the proportion of other systems dropped steadily to 23% in 2014 and then to just 15% in 2015. OS2 rose slightly from 17% to 18% and then stabilized, whereas OS3 fluctuated modestly, decreasing to 12% in 2014 before recovering to 15% in the final year.

title: Mixed Graph: The chart shows export values of one country by category for 2015-2016. The tabl...
prompt: The chart shows export values of one country by category for 2015-2016. The table shows percentage changes between these years. Summarise th
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – BAR CHART \+ TABLE

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Biểu đồ cột: giá trị xuất khẩu (tỷ USD) năm 2015 vs 2016
  • Bảng: % thay đổi

→ Dạng: so sánh \+ xu hướng

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The bar chart compares export earnings from five product categories in 2015 and 2016, while the table shows the percentage change in these values over the same period.

3. Overview (cực quan trọng)

  • Hầu hết các mặt hàng tăng
  • Textiles tăng mạnh nhất
  • Gems and jewellery là mặt hàng duy nhất giảm
  • Petroleum và engineered goods vẫn là nguồn thu lớn nhất

4. BODY 1 – So sánh số liệu chính

Petroleum:

\~60 → \~62 (cao nhất)

Engineered goods:

\~56 → \~60 (tăng đáng kể)

Gems & jewellery:

\~42 → \~40 (giảm nhẹ)

→ Nhóm top: petroleum \+ engineered

5. BODY 2 – Nhóm còn lại \+ % change

Agricultural:

\~30 → \~31 (tăng nhẹ nhất)

Textiles:

\~25 → \~31 (tăng mạnh nhất)

% change:

  • Textiles: \+15.24% (largest rise)
  • Engineered: \+8.5%
  • Petroleum: \+3%
  • Agricultural: \+0.81%
  • Gems: \-5.18% (only decline)

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

increase slightly / significantly

experience a rise

see a decline

remain the largest contributor

record the highest growth

be the only category to fall

The bar chart compares export earnings from five product categories in 2015 and 2016, while the table shows the percentage change over the same period.

Petroleum products generated the highest revenue in both years, rising slightly from about $60 billion in 2015 to roughly $62 billion in 2016. Earnings from engineered goods also increased, from approximately $56 billion to $60 billion. In contrast, gems and jewellery experienced a decline, falling from around $42 billion to $40 billion.

Agricultural products saw a marginal increase, from about $29 billion in 2015 to just over $30 billion in 2016. The most notable growth was in textiles, which rose significantly from roughly $25 billion to about $30 billion.

In terms of percentage change, textiles recorded the largest increase at 15.24%, followed by engineered goods at 8.5%. Petroleum products grew modestly by 3%, while agricultural products saw only a slight rise of 0.81%. Gems and jewellery was the only category to decrease, with a drop of 5.18%.

title: Mixed Graph: The first chart below gives information about the money spent by British parents...
prompt: The first chart below gives information about the money spent by British parents on their children’s sports between 2008 and 2014. The secon
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: 2 line graphs
  • Nội dung: spending \+ participation

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • two line graphs \+ spending \+ sports participation \+ children \+ time

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Spending: tăng đều theo thời gian
  • Football: luôn cao nhất
  • Athletics: tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ
  • Swimming: tăng ổn định

4. Body 1 – Spending

  • tăng liên tục qua các năm
  • không có giảm

→ Key idea:

  • steady upward trend

5. Body 2 – Participation

Viết theo từng môn:

Football

  • cao nhất toàn bộ
  • tăng nhẹ

Athletics

  • tăng mạnh (đặc biệt giai đoạn giữa)
  • sau đó giảm nhẹ

Swimming

  • tăng đều
  • luôn đứng giữa

→ Key idea:

  • football dominate
  • athletics biến động mạnh nhất

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • increase steadily
  • rise gradually
  • peak
  • slight decline
  • remain the highest
  • fluctuate

The line graphs illustrate the average monthly expenditure on children’s sports between 2008 and 2014, along with participation levels in football, athletics and swimming over the same period.

Overall, spending on children’s sports rose steadily throughout the period. Football consistently had the highest participation, while athletics experienced the most dramatic fluctuation. Swimming showed a gradual upward trend.

Monthly spending increased from £20 in 2008 to £24 in 2010, before rising further to £28 in 2012 and reaching £32 in 2014. This indicates a continuous and moderate growth in expenditure over the six-year period.

In terms of participation, football remained the most popular sport, with the number of children rising slightly from 8.2 million in 2008 to 9.0 million in 2014, despite a minor dip in 2010. Swimming participation also grew steadily, increasing from 2.0 million to 3.8 million.

By contrast, athletics started at a relatively low level of 0.8 million in 2008 and increased marginally to 1.2 million in 2010, before surging to 5.0 million in 2012. However, this figure declined slightly to 4.5 million by 2014.

title: Mixed Graph: The two bar charts below show the proportion of 14- to 16-year-old students stud...
prompt: The two bar charts below show the proportion of 14- to 16-year-old students studying a foreign language in an English speaking country, and
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – 2 BAR CHART (LANGUAGE STUDY)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Chart 1: % học sinh học ngoại ngữ (girls vs boys, 1984–2007)
  • Chart 2: top 3 ngôn ngữ (French, German, Spanish)

→ Dạng: comparison \+ change over time

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The bar charts compare the percentages of boys and girls studying a foreign language in 1984 and 2007, and illustrate the three most popular languages learned by students over the same period.

3. Overview

  • Girls luôn học ngoại ngữ nhiều hơn boys
  • Tỷ lệ giảm nhẹ theo thời gian
  • French vẫn phổ biến nhất nhưng giảm mạnh
  • Spanish tăng rõ rệt

4. BODY 1 – Gender comparison

1984:

  • Girls: 48%
  • Boys: 30%

2007:

  • Girls: 40%
  • Boys: 25%

→ Cả hai đều giảm

→ Gap vẫn lớn (\~15%)

5. BODY 2 – Languages

French:

  • 50% → 25% (giảm mạnh, nhưng vẫn top 1)

German:

  • 20% → 15% (giảm nhẹ)

Spanish:

  • 5% → 10% (tăng gấp đôi)

→ Xu hướng: shift from traditional → emerging language

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

decline slightly / significantly

remain the most popular

experience a sharp fall

double in proportion

narrow/wide gap

by contrast

The charts compare the proportions of boys and girls studying a foreign language in 1984 and 2007, and show the three most popular languages studied in those years.

Overall, girls were consistently more likely than boys to study a foreign language, although participation declined for both groups over time. Meanwhile, French remained the most popular language despite a decrease, whereas Spanish gained popularity.

In 1984, 48% of girls studied a foreign language, compared to 30% of boys. By 2007, these figures had fallen to 40% and 25% respectively. Although both genders saw declines, the gap between girls and boys remained significant.

Regarding language preferences, French was the dominant choice in both years, accounting for 50% in 1984 before dropping sharply to 25% in 2007. German also declined slightly, from 20% to 15%. In contrast, Spanish became more popular, doubling from 5% in 1984 to 10% in 2007.

title: Mixed Graph: The bar chart below shows how tourism to Australia from three different countrie...
prompt: The bar chart below shows how tourism to Australia from three different countries changed between 1995 and 2005. The line chart illustrates
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Bar chart \+ Line graph: Tourists & Types of stay (1995–2005)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ cột: số lượng khách du lịch đến Australia từ UK, USA, Japan (1995 vs 2005).

Biểu đồ đường: số người chọn các loại hình lưu trú (hotel vs backpacking) từ 1995–2005.

→ Dạng: mixed (bar \+ line, change over time \+ comparison)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

number of tourists

types of stay

UK / USA / Japan

staying at hotels / backpacking

Mẫu:

The bar chart compares the number of tourists from the UK, the USA and Japan visiting Australia in 1995 and 2005, while the line graph illustrates changes in types of accommodation over the same period.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Lượng khách từ UK và USA tăng mạnh, Japan giảm

UK và USA đạt mức cao tương đương vào 2005 (\~1.5 million)

Japan là nước duy nhất giảm

Khách ở khách sạn tăng rồi giảm, trong khi backpacking tăng liên tục

Đến 2005, hai loại hình gần như ngang nhau

4. BODY 1 – Bar chart (tourists)

UK:

1995: \~800,000
