2005: \~1,500,000 (tăng mạnh)

USA:

1995: \~500,000

2005: \~1,500,000 (tăng mạnh nhất)

Japan:

1995: \~1,450,000

2005: \~1,100,000 (giảm)

→ Ý chính:

  • USA có mức tăng lớn nhất
  • Japan chuyển từ cao nhất xuống thấp hơn

5. BODY 2 – Line graph (types of stay)

Staying at hotels:

1995: \~1.7 million

2000: \~2.4 million (đỉnh)

2005: \~1.2 million (giảm mạnh)

Backpacking:

1995: \~200,000

2000: \~700,000

2005: \~1.2 million (tăng đều)

→ Ý chính:

  • Xu hướng trái ngược
  • Backpacking bắt kịp hotel vào 2005

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

increase dramatically

remain stable / reach a peak

decline significantly

show an upward trend

overtake / catch up with

be comparable

The bar chart compares the number of tourists from the UK, the USA and Japan who visited Australia in 1995 and 2005, while the line graph shows changes in accommodation types over the same period.

Overall, visitor numbers from the UK and the USA increased significantly over the decade, whereas the figure for Japan declined. In terms of accommodation, staying in hotels was more popular initially, but backpacking grew steadily and matched hotel stays by 2005.

Looking first at tourist numbers, arrivals from the UK rose markedly from about 800,000 in 1995 to around 1.5 million in 2005. A similar but more dramatic increase was seen for visitors from the USA, climbing from roughly 500,000 to approximately 1.5 million. In contrast, Japanese tourism decreased from about 1.45 million in 1995 to just over 1.1 million in 2005.

Turning to accommodation, hotel stays increased from approximately 1.7 million in 1995 to a peak of around 2.4 million in 2000, before dropping sharply to about 1.2 million in 2005. By comparison, backpacking rose consistently throughout the period, starting at around 200,000 in 1995, increasing to roughly 700,000 in 2000, and reaching about 1.2 million in 2005, equal to hotel stays at the end of the period.

title: Mixed Graph: The bar chart below shows the proportions of English men and women of different ...
prompt: The bar chart below shows the proportions of English men and women of different ages who were living alone in 2011. The pie chart compares t
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Bar chart \+ Pie chart
  • Nội dung: living alone (age \+ gender) \+ housing size

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • bar chart \+ pie chart \+ living alone \+ bedrooms \+ England \+ 2011

→ dùng: illustrate / compare / show

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Nhóm trẻ: nam cao hơn nữ
  • Nhóm lớn tuổi: nữ cao hơn nam
  • Trung niên: gần như cân bằng
  • Nhà nhỏ (1–2 phòng): chiếm đa số
  • Nhà lớn (4+ phòng): chiếm tỷ lệ rất nhỏ

4. Body 1 – Bar chart (Age \+ Gender)

Chia theo nhóm tuổi:

16–34

  • nam \> nữ

35–64

  • gần bằng nhau
  • có điểm cân bằng

65+

  • nữ \> nam
  • khoảng cách tăng theo tuổi

→ Key idea:

  • xu hướng đảo chiều theo độ tuổi

5. Body 2 – Pie chart (Bedrooms)

Theo thứ tự:

  • 2 bedrooms: lớn nhất
  • 1 và 3 bedrooms: chiếm phần lớn
  • 4 bedrooms: nhỏ
  • 5+ bedrooms: nhỏ nhất

→ Key idea:

  • nhà ít phòng chiếm ưu thế

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • account for
  • the majority / the minority
  • outnumber
  • be evenly distributed
  • dominate
  • make up a small proportion

The charts illustrate the proportions of males and females living alone in England in 2011 across different age groups, along with the distribution of bedroom numbers in one-person households.

Overall, younger age groups were dominated by men living alone, whereas women accounted for the majority in older age brackets. In addition, most one-person households occupied properties with either two or three bedrooms.

Among younger individuals, men were more likely to live alone. Specifically, males represented 53% of those aged 16–24 and 63% in the 25–34 group, with a peak of 65% among people aged 35–49. The gender gap then narrowed in the 50–64 group, where both sexes accounted for an equal share of 50%. Beyond this age, the pattern reversed, with women becoming increasingly dominant. Females comprised 63% of those aged 65–74, rising to 72% for the 75–84 group and peaking at 75% among individuals aged 85 and over. Overall, women made up a larger proportion of solo dwellers (56%) compared to men (44%).

Regarding housing size, two-bedroom homes were the most common among single occupants, accounting for 35.4% of cases. This was followed by three-bedroom properties at 29.8% and one-bedroom dwellings at 28.0%. Larger homes were relatively rare, with only 5.3% of individuals living in four-bedroom properties and a minimal 1.4% occupying homes with five or more bedrooms.

title: Mixed Graph: The chart below shows what Anthropology graduates from one university did after ...
prompt: The chart below shows what Anthropology graduates from one university did after finishing their undergraduate degree courses. Summarise the
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie chart \+ Table: Anthropology graduates

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ tròn: tình trạng việc làm của sinh viên ngành Nhân học sau khi tốt nghiệp.

Bảng: mức lương sau 5 năm theo loại công việc.

→ Dạng: mixed (outcomes \+ comparison)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

destination of graduates

salaries after five years

Mẫu:

The pie chart illustrates the destinations of anthropology graduates from one university, while the table shows their salary distribution after five years of work across different employment sectors.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Phần lớn sinh viên có việc làm full-time

Unemployment và postgraduate chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

Sau 5 năm, mức lương cao ($75k+) chiếm phần lớn

Government sector có tỷ lệ lương cao nhất

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (destinations)

Full-time work: 52% (lớn nhất)

Part-time work: 15%

Unemployed: 12%

Full-time postgraduate study: 8%

Not known: 8%

Part-time work \+ study: 5%

→ Ý chính:

  • Hơn một nửa có việc full-time
  • Các lựa chọn khác chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ hơn nhiều

5. BODY 2 – Table (salaries after 5 years)

Freelance consultants:

$75–99k: 40%

$100k+: 40% (cao)

Government sector:

$100k+: 50% (cao nhất overall)

$75–99k: 30%

Private companies:

$50–74k: 35% (cao nhất nhóm này)

$100k+: 30%

→ Ý chính:

  • Freelance & government có nhiều người lương cao
  • Private tập trung ở mức trung bình

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

enter full-time employment

account for the largest proportion

a relatively small percentage

earn a higher salary

be concentrated in

be more likely to

The charts show the destinations of anthropology graduates from one university and their salaries after five years of work.

Just over half of graduates (52%) entered full-time employment, making it the most common outcome. Part-time work accounted for 15%, while 5% combined part-time work with postgraduate study. A further 8% pursued full-time postgraduate study. Unemployment stood at 12%, and outcomes for 8% of graduates were not known.

Regarding salaries after five years, earnings varied by employment type. In the government sector, the largest proportion of graduates (50%) earned over $100,000, followed by 30% in the $75,000–99,999 range. Freelance consultants also performed strongly, with 80% earning at least $75,000, evenly split between the top two bands (40% each). In contrast, those working in private companies were more concentrated in the mid-range, with 35% earning $50,000–74,999 and 30% exceeding $100,000. Lower salary bands ($25,000–49,999) were relatively uncommon across all sectors, accounting for only 5–10% of graduates.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information about a public library in a town called Little...
prompt: The charts below give information about a public library in a town called Little Chalfont. Summarise the information by selecting and report
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Mixed charts: Library (pie \+ table \+ bar)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ tròn: độ tuổi thành viên thư viện (2016).

Bảng: tỷ lệ sách/mượn theo thể loại (2016).

Biểu đồ cột: tổng số lượt mượn từ 2007–2016.

→ Dạng: mixed nhiều biểu đồ (overview tổng \+ chi tiết)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

age of members

total loans by category

total number of loans

Mẫu:

The charts provide information about the age distribution of library members, the proportion of loans by category in 2016, and the total number of loans between 2007 and 2016.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Người ≥65 tuổi chiếm phần lớn

Fiction (trẻ em & người lớn) là thể loại mượn nhiều nhất

Tổng số lượt mượn nhìn chung tăng theo thời gian, dù có dao động nhẹ

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (age)

Adults 65+: 51% (lớn nhất)

Young adults (13–17): 22%

Adults (18–64): 15%

Children: 12%

→ Ý chính:

  • Người cao tuổi chiếm hơn một nửa
  • Trẻ em là nhóm nhỏ nhất

5. BODY 2 – Table (loan categories)

Children’s fiction: 38%

Adult fiction: 38% (đồng cao nhất)

Adult non-fiction: 13%

Children’s non-fiction: 6%

Young adults: 2%

Adult audiobooks: 2%

Children’s DVDs: 1% (thấp nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Fiction dominate
  • Các loại còn lại chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

6. BODY 3 – Bar chart (total loans)

2007: \~15,500

2008–2011: tăng dần (\~19k → \~21k)

2012–2013: giảm nhẹ (\~20k → \~19.5k)

2014–2016: tăng lại, đạt \~22,000 (cao nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Xu hướng chung là tăng
  • Có một giai đoạn giảm nhẹ giữa kỳ

The charts provide information about the age distribution of members at Little Chalfont Library in 2016, the breakdown of loans by category in the same year, and the total number of loans from 2007 to 2016.

People aged 65 and over made up the largest proportion of members, accounting for 51%. Young adults (13–17) represented 22%, while adults aged 18–64 comprised 15%. Children formed the smallest group at 12%.

In terms of borrowing, fiction dominated. Both children’s fiction and adult fiction each accounted for 38% of total loans, making them the most popular categories. Adult non-fiction contributed 13%, whereas children’s non-fiction made up 6%. The remaining categories were relatively minor, with young adults at 2%, adult audiobooks at 2%, and children’s DVDs at just 1%.

Looking at trends over time, the total number of loans generally increased despite some fluctuations. Loans rose from around 15,000 in 2007 to approximately 21,000 in 2011, before dipping slightly to just under 20,000 in 2013. After that, the figure recovered and continued to climb, reaching a peak of about 22,000 in 2016.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information about the high-speed continuous internet conne...
prompt: The charts below give information about the high-speed continuous internet connection of households in five countries in 2001 and 2002. Summ
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Table \+ Bar chart: Internet connections (2001–2002)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng: phần trăm tăng trưởng kết nối Internet theo quốc gia.

Biểu đồ cột: số lượng kết nối trên mỗi 1000 người (2001 vs 2002).

→ Dạng: mixed (comparison \+ change)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

percentage change

internet connections per 1000 people

Mẫu:

The table shows the percentage change in internet connections across five countries, while the bar chart compares the number of connections per 1,000 people in 2001 and 2002.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Tất cả các nước đều tăng

USA và South Korea có số lượng kết nối cao nhất

Các nước châu Âu thấp hơn nhiều

Britain có mức tăng lớn nhất (+230%), South Korea thấp nhất (+20%)

4. BODY 1 – Bar chart (actual numbers)

USA:

2001: 93 → 2002: 251.1 (tăng mạnh, cao nhất)

South Korea:

2001: 122 → 2002: 134.4 (cao nhưng tăng nhẹ)

Germany:

2001: 3.4 → 2002: 5.6

Switzerland:

2001: 3.6 → 2002: 7.92

Britain:

2001: 3.3 → 2002: 10.89 (tăng rõ)

→ Ý chính:

  • USA vượt trội về số lượng
  • Europe vẫn ở mức thấp dù có tăng

5. BODY 2 – Table (percentage change)

Britain: \+230% (cao nhất)

USA: \+170%

Switzerland: \+120%

Germany: \+80%

South Korea: \+20% (thấp nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Tăng trưởng không đồng đều
  • Nước có base thấp (Britain) tăng mạnh nhất

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

increase significantly

record the highest growth

remain relatively low

a sharp rise

a modest increase

in contrast

The table and bar chart compare the percentage increase and the number of mobile phone connections per 1,000 people in five countries between 2001 and 2002.

All countries experienced growth, although the scale differed considerably. The USA and South Korea recorded much higher connection levels than the other nations, while Britain showed the most significant relative increase.

In terms of percentage change, Britain rose most sharply, with an increase of 230%. The USA also saw substantial growth at 170%, followed by Switzerland at 120%. Germany experienced a more moderate rise of 80%, whereas South Korea had the smallest increase, at just 20%.

Looking at the actual figures, the USA had the highest number of connections, climbing from 93 per 1,000 people in 2001 to about 251 in 2002. South Korea also maintained a high level, increasing from 122 to roughly 134. By contrast, the remaining countries had relatively low figures. Switzerland grew from approximately 3.6 to nearly 8, while Germany rose from about 3.4 to 5.6. Britain, despite its rapid growth, only increased from around 3.3 to just under 11 connections per 1,000 people.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information on the location and types of dance classes you...
prompt: The charts below give information on the location and types of dance classes young people in a town in Australia are currently attending. Su
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – PIE \+ BAR (DANCE CLASSES)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Pie chart: địa điểm lớp học
  • Bar chart: loại lớp theo độ tuổi
  • Đơn vị: % \+ number of students

→ Dạng mixed chart (composition \+ comparison)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The pie chart illustrates the locations where dance classes are held, while the bar chart compares the number of students in different types of dance classes by age group.

3. Overview (quan trọng)

  • Private studios là địa điểm phổ biến nhất
  • College-based studios là ít nhất
  • Under 11 thích ballet và tap
  • 11–16 tuổi thích modern dance

→ Sự khác biệt rõ ràng theo độ tuổi

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (location)

Private studios: 48% (largest)

School halls: 24%

Community halls: 18%

College-based: 10% (smallest)

→ Insight: phần lớn lớp học diễn ra ở private venues

5. BODY 2 – Bar chart (types by age)

Under 11:

  • Ballet: 600 (highest)
  • Tap: 500
  • Modern: 300 (lowest)

11–16:

  • Modern: 550 (highest)
  • Tap: 450
  • Ballet: 300 (lowest)

→ Insight: preference shifts from ballet → modern

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

account for

make up the largest proportion

be the most/least popular

there is a clear preference for

in contrast

by comparison

The pie chart shows the locations where dance classes are held, while the bar chart compares the number of students in three types of dance classes by age group.

Overall, private studios are the most common location for dance classes, and preferences for dance types differ notably between younger children and teenagers.

In terms of location, nearly half of all classes take place in private studios (48%). School halls (after-school) account for 24%, while community halls and other venues make up 18%. College-based studios are the least common, representing just 10% of the total.

Regarding types of classes, ballet is the most popular among children under 11, with 600 students, compared to only 300 in the 11–16 age group. Tap dancing also attracts a high number of younger students (500), slightly more than older participants (450). In contrast, modern dance is far more popular among teenagers, with 550 students, almost double the figure for younger children (300).

Overall, younger children tend to prefer more traditional styles such as ballet and tap, whereas older students show a stronger preference for modern dance.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts below show UK workers’ average time spent and methods to work in 2009...
prompt: The charts below show UK workers’ average time spent and methods to work in 2009. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the m
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie chart \+ Bar chart: Commuting time & transport modes (UK, 2009)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ tròn cho thấy thời gian đi làm trung bình của người lao động ở Anh năm 2009.

Biểu đồ cột so sánh phương tiện đi làm ở London và phần còn lại của UK.

→ Dạng: mixed charts (pie \+ bar)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

commute time

transport modes

London and the rest of the UK

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

Mẫu:

The pie chart illustrates the average commuting time in the UK, while the bar chart compares modes of transport used in London and the rest of the country in 2009.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Phần lớn người lao động đi làm dưới 30 phút

Car là phương tiện phổ biến nhất, đặc biệt ở ngoài London

London sử dụng nhiều phương tiện công cộng hơn (train, underground)

Underground gần như chỉ phổ biến ở London

→ xu hướng: ngoài London phụ thuộc vào ô tô, trong khi London ưu tiên giao thông công cộng

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (Commuting time)

1–15 mins: 42% → cao nhất

16–30 mins: 33%

31–45 mins: 12%

45–60 mins: 8%

60+ mins: 5% → thấp nhất

→ Ý chính: đa số người đi làm trong vòng 30 phút

5. BODY 2 – Bar chart (Transport modes)

Car:

London: \~37%

Rest of UK: \~70% → cao nhất

Walk:

London: \~18%

Rest: \~20%

Bus:

London: \~23%

Rest: \~17%

Bicycle:

London: \~4%

Rest: \~3%

Train:

London: \~29%

Rest: \~2%

Underground:

London: \~27%

Rest: \~1%

→ Ý chính: London sử dụng train và underground nhiều hơn, trong khi vùng khác phụ thuộc vào car

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

be the most common mode

be heavily reliant on

be significantly higher/lower

in contrast

The pie chart and bar chart illustrate average commuting times in the UK and the modes of transport used by workers in London and the rest of the UK in 2009.

Overall, most workers had relatively short commutes, with journeys under 30 minutes accounting for the majority. In terms of transport, cars were by far the most common mode outside London, whereas London commuters relied more heavily on public transport, particularly trains and the underground.

Regarding commuting time, the largest proportion of workers (42%) travelled for just 1–15 minutes. A further 33% had journeys lasting 16–30 minutes, meaning that three-quarters of workers commuted for under half an hour. Longer journeys were far less common, with 12% travelling for 31–45 minutes, 8% for 45–60 minutes, and only 5% commuting for over an hour.

In terms of transport modes, car use dominated in the rest of the UK, accounting for just over 70% of commuters, compared to around 35% in London. Walking was relatively similar in both areas, at approximately 18% in London and 20% elsewhere. Bus usage was slightly higher in London (about 23%) than in the rest of the country (around 17%). By contrast, rail-based transport showed the greatest difference: trains were used by roughly 29% of London workers but only about 2% outside the capital, while the underground was used by around 27% in London and was negligible elsewhere. Cycling remained the least common mode in both regions, at roughly 3–4%.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts below show the comparison of time spent, in minutes per day, by UK’s ...
prompt: The charts below show the comparison of time spent, in minutes per day, by UK’s males and females on household and leisure activities in 200
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – TABLE \+ BAR CHART (MEN vs WOMEN)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • 2 phần:
  • Table: leisure activities
  • Bar chart: household activities
  • Đơn vị: minutes per day
  • So sánh giới tính

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The table and bar chart compare the average time spent per day by men and women in the UK on leisure and household activities in 2008.

3. Overview (rất quan trọng)

  • Men dành nhiều thời gian hơn cho leisure
  • Women dành nhiều thời gian hơn cho household
  • Biggest gap: cooking & washing
  • Smallest difference: reading

4. BODY 1 – Leisure activities

Men spend more time on entertainment:

  • TV: 137 vs 118 (largest activity overall)

Reading:

  • Nearly equal (18 vs 19)

Sport:

  • Men higher (15 vs 11)

→ Insight: men slightly dominate leisure, especially passive activities

5. BODY 2 – Household activities

Cooking & washing:

  • Women 75 vs men 30 (huge gap)

Shopping:

  • Women higher (35 vs 25)

Clothes washing:

  • Women 30 vs men 10

Repair:

  • Only category men lead (20 vs 10)

→ Insight: women do most domestic work except technical tasks

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

spend more/less time on

account for

be significantly higher than

show a similar pattern

be the only category where…

a considerable gap

The table and bar chart compare the average daily time (in minutes) spent by men and women in the UK on leisure and household activities in 2008.

Overall, men spent more time on leisure activities, particularly watching TV, while women devoted significantly more time to household tasks.

In terms of leisure, watching TV, videos and listening to the radio was the most time-consuming activity for both genders, with men spending 137 minutes per day compared to 118 minutes for women. Reading accounted for relatively little time, at 18 minutes for men and slightly higher at 19 minutes for women. Men also spent more time on sport, averaging 15 minutes daily, compared to 11 minutes for women.

Regarding household activities, women consistently spent more time than men on most tasks. Cooking and washing took up the largest share, with women spending 75 minutes per day, more than double the 30 minutes recorded for men. Similarly, women spent 35 minutes on shopping, compared to 25 minutes for men. Clothes washing and ironing also showed a notable gap, at 30 minutes for women versus only 10 minutes for men. In contrast, men spent more time on repair work, averaging 20 minutes daily, while women spent just 10 minutes on this activity.

title: Mixed Graph: The charts show the average number of hours worked per week and the proportions ...
prompt: The charts show the average number of hours worked per week and the proportions of people suffering from stress-related illness across eight
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Bar chart \+ Pie chart
  • Nội dung: working hours \+ stress levels by occupation

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • bar chart \+ pie chart \+ working hours \+ stress \+ occupations

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Giờ làm việc: chênh lệch lớn giữa các nghề
  • Business men: làm nhiều nhất
  • Lecturers: làm ít nhất
  • Stress: lawyers cao nhất
  • Programmers: thấp nhất
  • Không phải nghề làm nhiều giờ nhất sẽ stress cao nhất

4. Body 1 – Bar chart (Working hours)

Viết theo ranking

  • cao nhất: business men
  • tiếp theo: movie producers, doctors
  • trung bình: writers, programmers, lawyers
  • thấp: chefs
  • thấp nhất: lecturers

→ Key idea:

  • sự khác biệt rõ rệt về số giờ làm

5. Body 2 – Pie chart (Stress levels)

Viết theo tỷ lệ

  • cao nhất: lawyers
  • tiếp theo: movie producers
  • trung bình: doctors, business men, chefs
  • thấp: writers, lecturers
  • thấp nhất: programmers

→ Key idea:

  • stress không hoàn toàn phụ thuộc vào giờ làm

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • rank first / second
  • account for
  • the highest / the lowest
  • considerably higher than
  • moderate level
  • in contrast

The charts compare the average weekly working hours of eight professions and the proportion of stress-related illness among people in these occupations.

Overall, business men and movie producers worked the longest hours, while lecturers and chefs had the shortest working weeks. In terms of stress, lecturers accounted for the largest share, whereas programmers experienced the lowest levels.

Business men worked the most, averaging 70 hours per week, followed by movie producers at 60 hours. Doctors also had relatively long working weeks at 52 hours, while writers worked 45 hours and programmers 40. Lawyers averaged 35 hours, and the figures were lower for chefs and lecturers, at 30 and 25 hours respectively.

Regarding stress-related illness, lecturers represented the highest proportion at 25%, despite having the shortest working hours. Movie producers followed at 18%, while doctors accounted for 15%. Business men made up 11% of stress cases, and chefs 10%. Writers and lawyers each contributed 8%, whereas programmers had the lowest figure at 5%.

title: Mixed Graph: The diagrams show the sales of cars of three different brands between 2015 and 2...
prompt: The diagrams show the sales of cars of three different brands between 2015 and 2019. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting th
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Table \+ Line graph
  • Task: describe trends \+ compare groups

2. Introduction (chỉ cần công thức)

  • table \+ line graph \+ chủ đề \+ thời gian

illustrate / compare / show

3. Overview (chỉ nêu ý chính, KHÔNG số)

  • Attendance: tăng → ổn định \~70%
  • Frequency: đạt đỉnh → giảm → ổn định
  • Nhóm tuổi:
  • 14–24 cao nhất
  • 50+ thấp nhất
  • Tất cả nhóm: cùng pattern (rise → fall → rise → slight fall)

4. Body 1 – Table

Viết theo 2 dòng riêng biệt

Attendance

  • tăng mạnh ban đầu
  • sau đó ổn định

Frequency

  • peak năm 1997
  • giảm mạnh
  • rồi ổn định

Key idea:

  • nhiều người đi nhưng đi ít lần hơn

5. Body 2 – Line graph

Cách viết:

  • So sánh theo rank (cao → thấp)

14–24

  • cao nhất toàn bộ

25–35

  • đứng thứ 2

36–49

  • thấp hơn

50+

  • thấp nhất

Trend chung:

  • tất cả nhóm biến động giống nhau

6. Từ khóa bắt buộc học sinh dùng

  • peak at
  • remain stable
  • decline
  • follow a similar pattern
  • consistently higher / lower

The line graph illustrates car sales of three brands (Xbrand, Ybrand and Zbrand) from 2015 to 2019, while the pie chart shows the proportion of sales by car type.

Overall, Xbrand experienced a steady increase and became the best-selling brand by the end of the period, whereas Zbrand showed a general decline before a slight recovery. Ybrand rose initially but then fell sharply. In terms of car types, MPVs accounted for the largest share, while sedans were the least popular.

In 2015, Zbrand had the highest sales at around 3.4 million units, followed by Ybrand at approximately 1.8 million, while Xbrand recorded the lowest figure at just under 1 million. Over the next two years, Xbrand grew consistently, reaching about 1.9 million in 2017. During the same period, Ybrand increased to a peak of roughly 2.6 million, overtaking Zbrand, whose sales declined to just above 2 million.

From 2017 onwards, Xbrand continued to rise, surpassing both competitors in 2018 and reaching nearly 3 million units in 2019. In contrast, Ybrand dropped significantly after 2017, falling to around 1.6 million by the end of the period. Zbrand decreased to a low of about 1.9 million in 2018 before recovering slightly to approximately 2.3 million in 2019.

Regarding car types, MPVs made up the largest proportion at 35%, followed by SUVs at 27% and sportscars at 25%. Hatchback city cars accounted for 10%, while sedans represented only 3% of total sales.

title: Mixed Graph: The graph and table below give information about water use worldwide and water c...
prompt: The graph and table below give information about water use worldwide and water consumption in two different countries.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Line graph \+ Table
  • Nội dung: global water use \+ country comparison

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • line graph \+ table \+ water use \+ sectors \+ countries \+ time

→ dùng: illustrate / compare / show

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Water use: tăng liên tục ở cả 3 lĩnh vực
  • Agriculture: cao nhất toàn bộ
  • Domestic: thấp nhất
  • Brazil: cao hơn Congo ở tất cả chỉ số

4. Body 1 – Line graph

Agriculture

  • luôn cao nhất
  • tăng mạnh, đặc biệt sau 1950

Industrial

  • tăng đều
  • đứng thứ 2

Domestic

  • thấp nhất
  • tăng chậm

→ Key idea:

  • tất cả đều tăng
  • agriculture chiếm ưu thế

5. Body 2 – Table

So sánh Brazil vs Congo:

Population

  • Brazil lớn hơn nhiều

Irrigated land

  • Brazil vượt trội

Water per person

  • Brazil cao hơn đáng kể

→ Key idea:

  • Brazil cao hơn ở mọi khía cạnh

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • dominate
  • increase dramatically
  • rise steadily
  • remain the lowest
  • significantly higher than
  • in all categories

The line graph shows global water use in agriculture, industry and domestic sectors between 1900 and 2000, while the table compares water consumption in Brazil and the Democratic Republic of Congo in 2000.

Overall, water use increased significantly in all sectors over the century, with agriculture consistently accounting for the largest share. Additionally, Brazil recorded far higher water consumption and agricultural capacity than Congo.

Agricultural water use rose dramatically from about 500 km³ in 1900 to approximately 3,000 km³ in 2000. Industrial use also increased steadily, from around 100 km³ to about 1,200 km³ by the end of the period. Domestic consumption showed the smallest growth, rising gradually from roughly 50 km³ to just over 300 km³.

In 2000, Brazil had a population of 176 million, compared to only 5.2 million in Congo. The area of irrigated land in Brazil was vastly larger, at 26,500 km², whereas Congo had just 100 km². Consequently, water consumption per person in Brazil was significantly higher, at 359 m³, compared to only 8 m³ in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

title: Mixed Graph: The graph and the table show the average monthly temperatures and annual hours o...
prompt: The graph and the table show the average monthly temperatures and annual hours of sunshine received by three major cities. Summarise the inf
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – LINE GRAPH \+ TABLE (SUNSHINE)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Line graph: số giờ nắng theo tháng (3 thành phố)
  • Table: tổng giờ nắng/năm

→ Dạng: trend \+ comparison

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The line graph illustrates the average monthly hours of sunshine in London, New York and Sydney, while the table presents their total annual sunshine hours.

3. Overview

  • New York có biến động mạnh nhất (mùa rõ rệt)
  • Sydney có xu hướng ngược lại (Nam bán cầu)
  • London ổn định hơn, luôn thấp hơn New York
  • Tổng giờ nắng: New York cao nhất, London thấp nhất

4. BODY 1 – London & New York

London:

  • Tăng từ \~8 (Jan) → \~23 (Jul–Aug peak)
  • Sau đó giảm về \~9 (Dec)

New York:

  • Tăng mạnh từ \~4 → \~29 (Jul peak – highest overall)
  • Giảm nhanh về \~5 (Dec)

→ Insight: New York có seasonal variation rõ nhất

5. BODY 2 – Sydney \+ Table

Sydney:

  • Cao đầu năm (\~26 Jan–Feb)
  • Giảm xuống đáy \~16 (Jul)
  • Tăng lại cuối năm (\~25 Dec)

→ Opposite pattern với Northern Hemisphere

Total sunshine:

  • New York: 2,535 (highest)
  • Sydney: 2,473 (slightly lower)
  • London: 1,180 (much lower)

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

peak at

reach a high/low

fluctuate significantly

show an opposite trend

remain relatively stable

record the highest/lowest figure

The line graph illustrates the average monthly temperatures in London, New York and Sydney, while the table compares their total annual hours of sunshine.

Overall, New York experiences the greatest seasonal variation in temperature, whereas Sydney shows an opposite pattern to the other two cities due to its location in the southern hemisphere. In terms of sunshine, New York and Sydney receive substantially more hours than London.

In London, temperatures rise steadily from about 8°C in January to a peak of around 23°C in July and August, before falling back to roughly 9°C in December. New York follows a similar pattern but with more extreme fluctuations, starting at approximately 4°C in January and climbing sharply to nearly 29°C in July, then dropping again to about 5°C by the end of the year.

By contrast, Sydney begins the year with high temperatures of around 26°C in January and February, after which figures gradually decline to a low of about 16°C in July. Temperatures then increase again towards December, reaching roughly 25°C.

Regarding sunshine, New York records the highest total at 2,535 hours per year, closely followed by Sydney with 2,473 hours. London, however, receives significantly less sunshine, at just 1,180 hours annually.

title: Mixed Graph: The graphs below show the cinema attendants in Australia and the average cinema ...
prompt: The graphs below show the cinema attendants in Australia and the average cinema visits by different age groups from 1996 to 2000. Summarise
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Table \+ Line graph: Cinema visits (1996–2000)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng cho thấy tỷ lệ người đi xem phim (attendance) và số lần trung bình (frequency) từ 1996 đến 2000.

Biểu đồ đường minh họa số lần đi xem phim theo 4 nhóm tuổi (14–24, 25–35, 36–49, 50+).

→ Dạng: mixed charts (table \+ line graph)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

cinema visits

attendance

frequency

age groups

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

Mẫu:

The table and line graph illustrate cinema attendance, average visit frequency, and viewing habits across different age groups between 1996 and 2000.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Attendance tăng từ 1996 rồi duy trì ổn định quanh mức 70%

Frequency đạt đỉnh năm 1997 rồi giảm và ổn định

Nhóm 14–24 đi xem nhiều nhất, 50+ ít nhất

Tất cả các nhóm tuổi có xu hướng giống nhau: tăng → giảm → tăng → giảm nhẹ

→ xu hướng: người trẻ đi xem phim thường xuyên hơn, xu hướng chung khá ổn định

4. BODY 1 – Table (Attendance & Frequency)

Attendance:

62% → 72% → 72% → 70% → 72%

→ tăng rồi ổn định

Frequency:

10.3 → 11.1 → 8.3 → 8.2 → 8.3

→ đạt đỉnh 1997 rồi giảm

→ Ý chính: lượng người đi xem cao nhưng tần suất giảm sau 1997

5. BODY 2 – Line graph (Age groups)

14–24: cao nhất (khoảng 14 → 22 → 16 → 23 → 17)

25–35: đứng thứ hai (13 → 20 → 14 → 21 → 16)

36–49: thấp hơn (11 → 17 → 12 → 19 → 14)

50+: thấp nhất (9 → 15 → 10 → 16 → 12)

→ Ý chính: mọi nhóm tuổi biến động giống nhau, nhưng người trẻ luôn đi nhiều hơn

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

peak at

decline sharply

remain stable

follow a similar pattern

be consistently higher/lower

The table and line graph illustrate cinema attendance rates and the average number of visits per person between 1996 and 2000, along with visit frequencies across four age groups.

Overall, cinema attendance increased sharply after 1996 and then remained relatively stable, while the average number of visits peaked in 1997 before declining. Across all years, younger people consistently visited cinemas more frequently than older groups.

Attendance rose from 62% in 1996 to 72% in 1997 and remained at this level in 1998, before dipping slightly to 70% in 1999 and recovering to 72% in 2000. In contrast, the average number of visits per person increased from 10.3 in 1996 to a peak of 11.1 in 1997, then fell to around 8.2–8.3 between 1998 and 2000.

In terms of age groups, individuals aged 14–24 recorded the highest visit frequencies throughout the period, rising from about 14 visits in 1996 to a peak of approximately 23 in 1999, before declining to around 17 in 2000. A similar pattern can be seen among those aged 25–35, whose visits peaked at just over 21 in 1999. Meanwhile, the 36–49 group followed the same trend at lower levels, and people aged 50+ consistently visited cinemas the least, with figures fluctuating between roughly 10 and 16 visits over the five years.

title: Mixed Graph: The graphs below show the percentage of math graduates and other graduates who g...
prompt: The graphs below show the percentage of math graduates and other graduates who got full-time job after graduating from a university in Austr
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Line graph \+ Table: Graduates (2004–2012)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ đường cho thấy tỷ lệ người lao động toàn thời gian có bằng đại học (Maths vs Other graduates).

Bảng số liệu thể hiện mức lương trung bình của hai nhóm trong cùng giai đoạn.

→ Dạng: mixed (line \+ table, change over time)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

percentage of full-time workers

average salary

Maths graduates / Other graduates

Mẫu:

The line graph illustrates the percentage of full-time workers holding a university degree, while the table shows the average salaries of maths graduates and other graduates between 2004 and 2012.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Maths graduates luôn có tỷ lệ cao hơn other graduates

Cả hai nhóm tăng đến khoảng 2008 rồi giảm nhẹ

Lương tăng đều theo thời gian ở cả hai nhóm

Maths graduates luôn kiếm nhiều hơn

4. BODY 1 – Line graph (percentage)

Maths graduates:

2004: \~78%

2006: \~90% (đỉnh)

2008: \~88%

2010: \~82%

2012: \~80%

Other graduates:

2004: \~65%

2006: \~82%

2008: \~86% (đỉnh)

2010: \~76%

2012: \~65%

→ Ý chính:

  • Khoảng cách giữa hai nhóm tồn tại suốt
  • Cả hai đạt peak vào khoảng 2006–2008 rồi giảm

5. BODY 2 – Table (salary)

Maths graduates:

2004: $41,000

2006: $43,000

2008: $50,000

2010: $51,000

2012: $56,000

Other graduates:

2004: $41,000

2006: $43,000

2008: $45,000

2010: $47,000

2012: $51,000

→ Ý chính:

  • Lương tăng ổn định
  • Khoảng cách thu nhập mở rộng theo thời gian (đặc biệt sau 2008)

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

peak at

remain higher than

experience a slight decline

increase steadily

a widening gap

earn more than

The line graph and table compare the proportion of full-time workers holding a university degree and the average salaries of maths graduates and other graduates between 2004 and 2012.

Overall, maths graduates consistently had higher employment rates than other graduates throughout the period, although both groups followed a similar trend of rising and then falling. In terms of income, salaries increased steadily for both groups, with maths graduates earning more, particularly after 2008.

Looking first at employment, around 78% of maths graduates were in full-time work in 2004, compared to approximately 63% of other graduates. Both figures rose to a peak in 2006, reaching about 90% and just over 80% respectively. In 2008, the proportions remained high, at roughly 88% for maths graduates and 86% for others. However, both groups then experienced a decline, falling to around 82% and 75% in 2010, before dropping further to approximately 80% and 63% by 2012.

Turning to salaries, both groups earned the same average income in 2004 and 2006, at $41,000 and $43,000 respectively. From 2008 onwards, a clear gap emerged. Maths graduates saw a sharp increase to $50,000 in 2008, rising further to $51,000 in 2010 and $56,000 in 2012. In contrast, other graduates experienced more moderate growth, with salaries increasing from $45,000 in 2008 to $47,000 in 2010 and reaching $51,000 by the end of the period.

title: Mixed Graph: The table and charts below give information on the police budget for 2017 and 20...
prompt: The table and charts below give information on the police budget for 2017 and 2018 in one area of Britain. The table shows where the money c
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Table \+ Pie charts: Police budget (2017–2018)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng: nguồn ngân sách cảnh sát (2017 vs 2018).

Biểu đồ tròn: cách phân bổ chi tiêu trong 2 năm.

→ Dạng: mixed (table \+ pie, comparison \+ change)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

police budget

sources of funding

how the money was spent

Mẫu:

The table shows the sources of a police budget in 2017 and 2018, while the pie charts illustrate how the money was allocated in these two years.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Tổng ngân sách tăng nhẹ

Chính phủ luôn là nguồn chính

Thuế địa phương tăng đáng kể

Chi cho buildings & transport chiếm phần lớn nhưng giảm nhẹ

Technology tăng rõ rệt, salary giữ nguyên

4. BODY 1 – Table (sources)

Total:

2017: £304.7m → 2018: £318.6m (tăng)

National government:

175.5 → 177.8 (tăng nhẹ, lớn nhất)

Local taxes:

91.2 → 102.3 (tăng mạnh nhất)

Other sources:

38 → 38.5 (gần như không đổi)

→ Ý chính:

  • Government luôn chiếm tỷ trọng lớn nhất
  • Local taxes là nguồn tăng đáng kể

5. BODY 2 – Pie charts (spending)

2017:

Buildings & transport: 75% (lớn nhất)

Salary: 17%

Technology: 8%

2018:

Buildings & transport: 69% (giảm)

Salary: 17% (không đổi)

Technology: 14% (tăng mạnh)

→ Ý chính:

  • Technology tăng gần gấp đôi
  • Buildings vẫn dominant nhưng giảm

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

remain the largest source

increase slightly / significantly

a marginal rise

a noticeable increase

decline moderately

The table and pie charts illustrate the sources of a police budget in 2017 and 2018 and how this money was allocated.

The total budget increased from £304.7 million in 2017 to £318.6 million in 2018. In both years, the national government was the primary source of funding, rising slightly from £175.5m to £177.8m. Revenue from local taxes showed a more noticeable increase, from £91.2m to £102.3m, while other sources such as grants contributed relatively small amounts, growing marginally from £38m to £38.5m.

Regarding expenditure, the largest proportion of the budget in both years was spent on buildings and transport, although this share declined from 75% in 2017 to 69% in 2018. Spending on salaries remained unchanged at 17% in both years. By contrast, the proportion allocated to technology rose significantly, from 8% to 14%, indicating a shift towards increased investment in this area.

title: Mixed Graph: The table and pie chart give information about the population in Australia accor...
prompt: The table and pie chart give information about the population in Australia according to different nationalities and areas. Summarize the key
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – PIE \+ TABLE (NATIONALITY & LOCATION)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Pie chart: tỷ lệ quốc tịch cư dân
  • Table: % sống ở city vs countryside

→ Dạng: composition \+ comparison

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The pie chart shows the distribution of residents by nationality, while the table compares the percentages of each group living in cities and the countryside.

3. Overview

  • Australians chiếm đa số áp đảo
  • Các nhóm còn lại rất nhỏ
  • Tất cả quốc tịch đều chủ yếu sống ở thành phố
  • Chinese có tỷ lệ urban cao nhất

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart

Australian: 73% (dominant)

Others:

  • British: 7%
  • New Zealander: 3%
  • Chinese: 2%
  • Dutch: 1%
  • Other: 14%

→ Insight: population heavily concentrated in one group

5. BODY 2 – Table

City living (cao nhất → thấp hơn):

  • Chinese: 99% (highest)
  • New Zealander: 90%
  • British: 89%
  • Dutch: 83%
  • Australian: 80%

Countryside:

  • Australian: 20% (highest rural share)
  • Dutch: 17%
  • British: 11%
  • New Zealander: 10%
  • Chinese: 1% (lowest)

→ Insight: foreigners even more urbanized than locals

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

account for the majority

make up a small proportion

be overwhelmingly dominant

be concentrated in urban areas

have the highest/lowest proportion

by contrast

The pie chart illustrates the distribution of nationalities in Australia, while the table shows the proportions of each group living in cities and the countryside.

Overall, Australians make up by far the largest proportion of the population, and the vast majority of all nationalities live in urban areas rather than rural ones.

In terms of nationality, Australians account for 73% of the population. The next largest group is people from “other” countries at 14%. British people represent 7%, followed by New Zealanders at 3%. Chinese and Dutch populations are relatively small, at 2% and 1% respectively.

Regarding where people live, all groups are predominantly based in cities. Chinese residents are the most urbanised, with 99% living in cities and only 1% in the countryside. New Zealanders and British people also show high urban figures, at 90% and 89% respectively. Dutch residents have a slightly lower urban proportion at 83%, while Australians have the lowest city share among the groups listed, at 80%, meaning they have the highest proportion living in rural areas (20%).

title: Pie Chart: The chart below shows the percentage of the day working adults spent doing diffe...
prompt: The chart below shows the percentage of the day working adults spent doing different activities in a particular country in 1958 and in 2008.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Percentage of day spent on different activities (1958 vs 2008)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ thời gian trong ngày được dành cho các hoạt động khác nhau vào năm 1958 và 2008.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

percentage of day

different activities

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

time spent → time allocated

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate how people allocated their time to different activities in 1958 and 2008.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Working chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai năm và tăng đáng kể

Sleeping giảm

Travelling to work tăng rõ

Các hoạt động thư giãn tại nhà giảm

→ xu hướng: cuộc sống hiện đại dành nhiều thời gian cho công việc và di chuyển hơn

Từ khóa nên dùng:

accounted for the largest share

increased significantly

declined

showed notable changes

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Working, Sleeping, Travelling)

Working:

33% → 42%

→ tăng mạnh, lớn nhất

Sleeping:

32% → 25%

→ giảm đáng kể

Travelling to work:

19% → 13% (thực tế giảm nhẹ, cần chú ý)

Ý chính: thời gian làm việc tăng trong khi ngủ giảm

BODY 2 – Các hoạt động còn lại

Relaxing at home:

8% → 6% (giảm nhẹ)

Going out:

6% → 6% (không đổi)

Other (hobbies/sport):

2% → 8% (tăng rõ)

Ý chính: các hoạt động giải trí thay đổi nhẹ, nhưng hobbies tăng lên đáng kể

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase significantly

decline moderately

remain unchanged

show a slight change

Ví dụ:

Working accounted for the largest proportion in both years and increased significantly.

The percentage of time spent sleeping declined noticeably.

Travelling to work showed a slight decrease.

Time spent on hobbies and sports increased considerably.

The two pie charts compare how people allocated their daily time across six activities in 1958 and 2008.

Overall, working and sleeping accounted for the largest shares in both years, although the proportion of time spent working increased markedly, while sleeping and commuting declined. Meanwhile, leisure-related activities showed relatively minor changes.

In 1958, working occupied the largest proportion of the day at 33%, closely followed by sleeping at 32%. Travelling to work made up 19%, while relaxing at home accounted for 8%. Smaller proportions were devoted to going out (6%) and other activities such as hobbies or sport (2%).

By 2008, the proportion of time spent working had risen significantly to 42%, making it by far the dominant activity. In contrast, sleeping decreased to 25%, and commuting fell to 13%. Relaxing at home declined slightly to 6%, while the figure for going out remained unchanged at 6%. Notably, time spent on other activities increased fourfold to 8%.

title: Map: The layout of part of a building, indicating the position of the fire exits
prompt: The diagram shows the layout of part of a building, indicating the position of the fire exits. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Student accommodation layout (no time)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ mô tả bố cục của một khu nhà ở sinh viên, bao gồm phòng ngủ, lối đi và các lối thoát hiểm.

→ Dạng: map – layout description (không có thay đổi thời gian)

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: layout – student accommodation

Dùng:

  • show → illustrate / depict
  • map → layout / plan
3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Tổng cộng có 7 bedrooms được bố trí xung quanh hành lang trung tâm
  • Có nhiều lối thoát hiểm (fire exits) và meeting points
  • Khu trung tâm gồm lift và stairs giúp di chuyển thuận tiện
  • Bố cục được thiết kế theo hướng an toàn và dễ tiếp cận
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu phòng ngủ
  • Phía trên:
  • Bedroom 1 và 2 nằm bên trái và trung tâm
  • Bedroom 5 và 6 nằm bên phải, gần cầu thang
  • Phía dưới:
  • Bedroom 3 ở trung tâm
  • Bedroom 4 và 7 nằm phía bên phải

→ Ý: các phòng được phân bố đều quanh hành lang

BODY 2 – Khu giao thông & an toàn
  • Trung tâm:
  • Có lift và một corridor kết nối các phòng
  • Có 2 khu cầu thang (stairs):
  • Một ở bên trái
  • Một ở phía trên bên phải
  • Lối thoát hiểm:
  • Fire exit A (bên trái)
  • Fire exit B (phía trên bên phải)
  • Điểm tập trung:
  • Meeting point 1 (bên trái)
  • Meeting point 2 (phía trên)

→ Ý: hệ thống được thiết kế đảm bảo an toàn cháy nổ

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be located → nằm ở
  • be positioned → được bố trí
  • be connected by → được kết nối bởi
  • provide access to → cung cấp lối đi tới
  • safety features → yếu tố an toàn
  • emergency exit → lối thoát hiểm

The diagram shows the layout of a floor plan with bedrooms, emergency exits, and meeting points.

Overall, the design provides clear evacuation routes, with two fire exits and two designated meeting points located at opposite sides of the building.

There are seven bedrooms arranged around a central corridor. Bedrooms 1 and 2 are positioned in the upper-left and upper-middle areas, while Bedrooms 5 and 6 are located on the right-hand side near a staircase and Fire Exit B. Bedrooms 3, 4, and 7 are situated along the lower section of the plan.

In terms of safety features, Fire Exit A is on the left side near Meeting Point 1, while Fire Exit B is located at the top right, close to the stairs and Meeting Point 2\. A lift is placed near the centre-right of the building, and two staircases are available—one on the left side and another near the right—ensuring multiple evacuation routes from different parts of the floor.

title: Map: The plans below show how the ground of a particular building has changed over time
prompt: The plans below show how the ground of a particular building has changed over time. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

– MAP (3 giai đoạn)

1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi chức năng của một tòa nhà qua 3 giai đoạn:

  • 1958–1984: office
  • 1985–2000: apartment
  • 2001–present: flower shop

→ Dạng: map – multiple time periods (3 stages)

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Dùng cấu trúc:

The diagrams illustrate how a building was used for different purposes between 1958 and the present day.*
3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Tòa nhà trải qua 3 lần chuyển đổi chức năng lớn
  • Từ văn phòng → căn hộ → cửa hàng hoa
  • Bố cục bên trong thay đổi đáng kể theo từng mục đích sử dụng
  • Một số khu vực (như kitchen / entrance) vẫn giữ vị trí tương đối ổn định
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Giai đoạn 1958–1984 (Office)
  • Bên trái:
  • Meeting room
  • Assistant’s office
  • Secretary’s office
  • Toilet
  • Bên phải:
  • Kitchen
  • Manager office
  • Reception area (gần entrance)

→ Ý chính: bố cục phục vụ làm việc hành chính, nhiều phòng chức năng riêng biệt

BODY 2 – Giai đoạn 1985–2000 (Apartment)
  • Bên trái:
  • Living room
  • Bathroom and shower
  • Bên phải:
  • Kitchen
  • Bedroom 1
  • Bedroom 2

→ Ý chính: chuyển thành không gian sinh hoạt, ít phòng hơn nhưng diện tích lớn hơn

BODY 3 – 2001–Present (Flower shop)
  • Bên trái:
  • Gift cards
  • Play area
  • Trung tâm:
  • Flower display area
  • Bên phải:
  • Kitchen (giữ nguyên vị trí tương đối)
  • Office
  • Flower section

→ Ý chính: chuyển sang mục đích thương mại, không gian mở và phục vụ khách hàng

5\. Từ vựng nên dùng
  • be converted into → được chuyển đổi thành
  • undergo significant changes → trải qua thay đổi lớn
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • remain unchanged → giữ nguyên
  • be repurposed → được tái sử dụng
  • layout → bố cục

The diagrams show how a building was used for different purposes between 1958 and the present.

Overall, the building changed from an office to a residential apartment and is now a flower shop, with each stage involving a complete reorganisation of internal space.

Between 1958 and 1984, the building functioned as an office. It contained several work-related rooms, including a meeting room, manager’s office, assistant’s office, and secretary’s office. Supporting facilities such as a kitchen, toilet, and reception area were also provided, all arranged around a central layout.

From 1985 to 2000, the building was converted into an apartment. The internal structure was simplified to suit residential use, with a living room occupying a large central area. Two bedrooms were added on the right, while the kitchen was positioned in the upper section. A bathroom and shower replaced the office facilities on the left.

Since 2001, the building has been used as a flower shop. The space is now divided into functional retail areas, including a flower display zone in the centre, a play area, and a section for gift cards. A kitchen and office are located on the right-hand side, reflecting both commercial and operational needs.

title: Map: The maps below show part of a village in 1910, and the same part of that village i...
prompt: The maps below show part of a village in 1910, and the same part of that village in 2010. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Shalton village (1910 vs 2010\)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi của làng Shalton từ năm 1910 đến 2010\.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • Shalton village
  • 1910 – 2010

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / transformations

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate how Shalton village changed between 1910 and 2010\.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Ngôi làng trở nên phát triển và đô thị hóa hơn
  • Nhiều cơ sở mới được xây dựng (supermarket, bank, parking, café)
  • Nhà ở tăng đáng kể, đặc biệt dọc các tuyến đường chính
  • Một số công trình cũ bị thay thế (butcher, grocer, baker, large house)
  • Post office và school vẫn giữ nguyên
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu bên trái (School Road)

1910:

  • School
  • Butcher

2010:

  • School vẫn giữ
  • Butcher bị thay bằng:
  • Supermarket
  • Bank
  • Parking

Ý chính: khu này được phát triển thành khu thương mại

BODY 2 – Khu trung tâm

1910:

  • Large house ở trung tâm
  • Grocer và baker
  • Nhiều cây xanh

2010:

  • Tất cả bị loại bỏ
  • Thay bằng:
  • Café
  • Nhiều nhà ở

Ý chính: chuyển từ khu ít xây dựng sang khu dân cư

BODY 3 – Khu dọc London Road và High Street

1910:

  • Ít nhà

2010:

  • Nhà xuất hiện dày đặc dọc hai con đường
  • Post office giữ nguyên vị trí

Ý chính: mật độ dân cư tăng mạnh

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be converted into → được chuyển thành
  • be developed into → được phát triển thành
  • remain unchanged → giữ nguyên
  • residential area → khu dân cư
  • commercial facilities → cơ sở thương mại

The maps compare the layout of Shalton village in 1910 and 2010\.

Overall, the village became more residential and commercial over time, with a significant increase in housing and the replacement of small local shops by larger facilities.

In 1910, the village had a relatively simple structure centred around a triangular area formed by London Road, School Road, and High Street. This area contained a large house surrounded by trees, along with a grocer and a baker. To the west, along School Road, there was a school and a butcher. The post office was located near London Road, while only a small number of houses were found, mainly along High Street and in the northeast.

By 2010, the central triangular area had been redeveloped into a residential zone with numerous houses, and the original large house, grocer, and baker had disappeared. A café was introduced in this area. On School Road, the butcher was replaced by a supermarket, and additional facilities such as a bank and a car park were added nearby, while the school remained. The number of houses increased significantly, particularly along London Road and High Street, whereas the post office stayed in the same location.

title: Map: The diagrams show a small museum and its surroundings in 1990 and 2010
prompt: The diagrams show a small museum and its surroundings in 1990 and 2010. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Ground floor of museum (1990 vs 2010\)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi ở tầng trệt của một bảo tàng giữa năm 1990 và 2010\.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • ground floor
  • museum
  • 1990 – 2010

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / alterations

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate how the ground floor of a museum changed between 1990 and 2010\.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Bảo tàng được hiện đại hóa và cải thiện tiện ích
  • Khu vực chức năng được mở rộng và bố trí lại
  • Thêm dịch vụ cho khách tham quan (café, shop lớn hơn)
  • Không gian bên ngoài cũng được thay đổi đáng kể
  • Bãi đỗ xe được mở rộng, trong khi một số khu cũ bị loại bỏ
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Bên trong bảo tàng
1990:
  • Entrance nằm ở trung tâm
  • Store room ở góc trên trái
  • Shop nhỏ ở góc dưới phải
  • Các phòng triển lãm chiếm phần lớn diện tích
2010:
  • Entrance vẫn ở vị trí trung tâm
  • Shop được mở rộng đáng kể (chiếm phần lớn phía dưới)
  • Store room vẫn giữ nhưng thu nhỏ
  • Bố cục exhibition rooms được điều chỉnh lại

→ Ý: không gian bên trong được tối ưu và thương mại hóa hơn

BODY 2 – Khu bên ngoài
1990:
  • Garden nằm ở giữa khu đất
  • Một đường path uốn cong đi qua khu vườn
  • Car park ở phía bên phải
  • Có thêm cottage ở góc dưới bên trái
2010:
  • Garden bị thu hẹp hoặc loại bỏ
  • Path được làm thẳng
  • Car park được mở rộng (chiếm toàn bộ bên phải)
  • Cottage bị loại bỏ
  • Café được xây thêm gần lối vào

→ Ý: khu ngoài chuyển từ không gian xanh → tiện ích phục vụ khách

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be expanded → được mở rộng
  • be removed → bị loại bỏ
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be redesigned → được thiết kế lại
  • be modernised → được hiện đại hóa
  • visitor facilities → tiện ích cho khách

The maps illustrate changes to a museum and its surroundings between 1990 and 2010\.

Overall, the site was modernised, with expanded visitor facilities such as a café and larger car parks, while the original cottage was removed and the internal layout of the museum was reorganised.

In 1990, the museum building was located on the left side of the site, containing an entrance in the centre, a store room in the northwest, and a shop in the southeast. A cottage stood to the south of the building near the main road. To the east, there was a garden with a curved path leading towards a car park.

By 2010, the museum had been redesigned internally, with the entrance moved slightly and the shop enlarged to occupy the entire southern section of the building. The store room remained in the northwest corner. The former cottage was replaced by a café positioned along the main road. The garden to the east was removed and converted into additional parking, resulting in two car park areas, while the path remained alongside them.

title: Map: The maps below show Hunderstone town at present and a proposed plan for it
prompt: The maps below show Hunderstone town at present and a proposed plan for it. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Road system (now vs proposed plan)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy hiện trạng hệ thống giao thông và kế hoạch phát triển trong tương lai của một khu vực.

→ Dạng: map – present vs future (proposed changes)

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • road system / layout
  • now / proposed plan

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • plan → proposed development / future layout

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate the current road layout and the proposed future development of the area.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Hệ thống đường sẽ được mở rộng đáng kể
  • Thêm nhiều tuyến đường mới và vòng xuyến (roundabouts)
  • Airfield sẽ bị thay thế bởi industrial estate
  • Kết nối giao thông được cải thiện, đặc biệt quanh town centre
  • Khu vực trở nên hiện đại và thuận tiện hơn cho lưu thông
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Hiện trạng (NOW)
  • A2 là tuyến đường chính chạy dọc phía bên trái
  • Có một roundabout kết nối với một con đường dẫn đến town centre
  • Railway chạy theo hướng Bắc–Nam, chia khu vực thành hai phần
  • Gas station nằm bên trái đường sắt
  • Airfield nằm ở phía trên bên trái
  • Town centre nằm bên phải, có ring road xung quanh

→ Ý: hệ thống đường còn đơn giản, kết nối hạn chế

BODY 2 – Kế hoạch tương lai (PROPOSED PLAN)
  • A2 vẫn giữ nhưng được kết nối với tuyến mới A4
  • A4 tạo thành một vòng lớn bao quanh khu vực
  • Thêm nhiều roundabouts, bao gồm một ở gần railway
  • Airfield bị thay thế bởi industrial estate
  • Xây thêm đường nối trực tiếp tới town centre
  • Ring road vẫn giữ nhưng được tích hợp tốt hơn

→ Ý: hệ thống trở nên phức tạp hơn và kết nối hiệu quả hơn

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be expanded → được mở rộng
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be constructed → được xây dựng
  • be connected to → được kết nối với
  • transport network → hệ thống giao thông
  • improve connectivity → cải thiện kết nối

The maps compare the current road system with a proposed redevelopment of the area.

Overall, the plan involves a major expansion of the road network, including new routes, roundabouts, and improved connections to the town centre, while the airfield will be replaced by an industrial estate.

At present, a single main road (A2) runs north–south, linking a roundabout near the airfield in the northwest to the town centre in the southeast. A railway line runs parallel to this road, with a gas station located nearby, and the town centre is surrounded by a small ring road.

In the proposed plan, the airfield will be redeveloped into an industrial estate connected to a new roundabout. Additional roundabouts will be introduced along the main route, and a new road (A4) will form a large loop around the area, improving access. The town centre will be linked to this expanded network and remain encircled by a ring road, while the railway and gas station will remain unchanged.

title: Map: The plans below show the site of a farm in 1950 and the same site today
prompt: The plans below show the site of a farm in 1950 and the same site today. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Beechwood Farm (1950 vs Today)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi của một trang trại (Beechwood Farm) từ năm 1950 đến hiện tại.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • Beechwood Farm
  • 1950 – today

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / transformations

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate how Beechwood Farm has changed from 1950 to the present day.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Trang trại chuyển từ nông nghiệp thuần túy → kết hợp du lịch và thương mại
  • Khu trồng trọt (fruit trees, soft fruits, vegetables) phần lớn được giữ lại
  • Nhiều tiện ích mới được thêm vào (farm shop, parking, camping field, solar panels)
  • Khu chăn nuôi và nhà ở được tái bố trí
  • Tổng thể trở nên đa chức năng và hiện đại hơn
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu bên trái (trồng trọt)
1950:
  • Fruit trees (trên trái)
  • Soft fruits (giữa)
  • Vegetables (dưới, gần sông)
Today:
  • Các khu này gần như giữ nguyên
  • Thêm farm shop trong khu fruit trees

→ Ý: hoạt động nông nghiệp vẫn duy trì nhưng có thêm yếu tố thương mại

BODY 2 – Khu bên phải (chăn nuôi & công trình)
1950:
  • Sheep ở phía trên bên phải
  • Barn, farmhouse và chickens ở phía dưới bên phải
Today:
  • Sheep bị loại bỏ
  • Khu này được chuyển thành:
  • Camping field
  • Solar panels
  • Barn được di dời xuống gần khu vegetables
  • Thêm holiday cottages
  • Farmhouse và chickens vẫn giữ

→ Ý: chuyển từ chăn nuôi → du lịch và năng lượng

BODY 3 – Cơ sở hạ tầng
1950:
  • Ít đường và không có bãi đỗ xe
Today:
  • Thêm parking ở nhiều vị trí
  • Hệ thống đường được mở rộng
  • Kết nối giữa các khu rõ ràng hơn

→ Ý: cơ sở hạ tầng được nâng cấp để phục vụ khách

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be transformed into → được chuyển đổi thành
  • be introduced → được thêm vào
  • be relocated → được di dời
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • agricultural land → đất nông nghiệp
  • tourism facilities → cơ sở du lịch

The maps illustrate how Beechwood Farm has changed from 1950 to the present day.

Overall, the farm has been partly modernised and diversified, with new facilities such as parking areas, a camping field, and solar panels, while the original agricultural zones have largely been retained.

In 1950, the western side of the farm was used for crops, including fruit trees, soft fruits, and vegetables, arranged from north to south. To the east, there was land for sheep, along with a barn, farmhouse, and an area for chickens near the river. The entire site was bordered by rivers to the south and connected by internal roads.

Today, the crop areas remain in the same locations, although a farm shop has been added within the fruit tree section. The former sheep field has been replaced by a camping area, and solar panels have been installed further to the northeast. Two parking areas have also been introduced, one near the main road and another on the eastern side. Additionally, holiday cottages have been built near the river, while the farmhouse and chicken area remain, and the barn has been relocated slightly southwards.

title: Map: The plans below show the ground floor of a library in 2001 and how it was redevelo...
prompt: The plans below show the ground floor of a library in 2001 and how it was redeveloped in 2009. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Ground floor (2001 vs 2009\)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi ở tầng trệt của một thư viện từ năm 2001 đến 2009\.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • ground floor – library – 2001 – 2009

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / alterations

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate how the ground floor of a library changed between 2001 and 2009\.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Thư viện được tái cấu trúc và hiện đại hóa đáng kể
  • Không gian chuyển từ đọc truyền thống → đa chức năng
  • Thêm khu cho trẻ em, máy tính và nhiều chuyên mục mới
  • Bố cục trở nên phân khu rõ ràng và phục vụ nhiều đối tượng hơn
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu trung tâm & bên phải
2001:
  • Khu trung tâm có nhiều tables để đọc
  • Bên phải là Newspaper & Periodicals
  • Không có khu công nghệ hay khu trẻ em
2009:
  • Tables được giữ nhưng bố trí lại
  • Newspaper & Periodicals được thay bằng Films & DVDs
  • Thêm computers ở góc dưới bên phải
  • Xuất hiện children’s area ở trung tâm

→ Ý: chuyển từ đọc báo truyền thống → giải trí và công nghệ

BODY 2 – Khu bên trái
2001:
  • History (trên trái)
  • Self-help (dưới trái)
  • Fiction chiếm toàn bộ phía trên
2009:
  • Khu bên trái được chia nhỏ thành nhiều mục:
  • Law
  • Economics
  • Kitchen
  • Self-help (giữ lại nhưng thu nhỏ)
  • Fiction (di chuyển xuống dưới)
  • History (dịch xuống gần entrance)

→ Ý: phân loại sách chi tiết hơn thay vì gom chung

BODY 3 – Khu phía trước (gần entrance)
2001:
  • Librarian’s desk ở gần entrance
  • Stairs ở góc phải dưới
2009:
  • Librarian’s desk vẫn giữ vị trí
  • Stairs không thay đổi
  • Thêm children’s books ở phía trên
  • Khu trung tâm trở nên mở và linh hoạt hơn

→ Ý: giữ các yếu tố cố định nhưng nâng cấp trải nghiệm

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be reorganized → được tái tổ chức
  • be divided into → được chia thành
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be introduced → được thêm vào
  • be relocated → được di chuyển
  • multi-functional space → không gian đa chức năng

The maps show how the ground floor of a library changed between 2001 and 2009\.

Overall, the layout was reorganised to include more specialised sections, particularly for children and digital media, while the central reading area remained.

In 2001, the library had a relatively simple layout. Fiction occupied the top area, while history and self-help were located along the left wall. Newspapers and periodicals were placed on the right side. In the centre, several tables were arranged for reading, and the librarian’s desk was positioned near the entrance, with stairs in the southeast corner.

By 2009, the space had been divided into more categories. A children’s books section was introduced at the top, along with a designated children’s area in the centre. New sections such as law, economics, and kitchen appeared along the left side, while fiction and history were moved to smaller areas. The right side was converted into a films and DVDs section, and computers were added near the bottom right. The librarian’s desk, entrance, and stairs remained in the same positions, although the number and arrangement of tables changed slightly.

title: Map: The maps below show the changes in the art gallery ground floor in 2015 and presen...
prompt: The maps below show the changes in the art gallery ground floor in 2015 and present day. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Art Gallery (2005 vs present day)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi trong bố cục của một phòng trưng bày nghệ thuật từ năm 2005 đến hiện tại.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • art gallery – 2005 – present day

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / alterations

Ví dụ:

The maps illustrate how the layout of an art gallery has changed from 2005 to the present day.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Phòng trưng bày được hiện đại hóa và tối ưu trải nghiệm khách tham quan
  • Thêm tiện ích mới: lift, children’s area, temporary exhibition, vending machine, wheelchair ramp
  • Một số khu cũ bị loại bỏ (café, exhibition room 4\)
  • Không gian trở nên linh hoạt và thân thiện hơn
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu bên phải (exhibition area)
2005:
  • Có 3 phòng trưng bày:
  • Exhibition room 1 (dưới phải)
  • Exhibition room 2 (giữa phải)
  • Exhibition room 3 (trên phải)
Hiện tại:
  • 3 phòng này vẫn được giữ nguyên vị trí
  • Tuy nhiên, bố cục trở nên rõ ràng và hiện đại hơn
  • Thêm lift ở phía trên bên phải gần cầu thang

→ Ý: khu trưng bày chính được giữ ổn định, chỉ nâng cấp tiện ích

BODY 2 – Khu bên trái (major changes)
2005:
  • Bao gồm:
  • Café (trên trái)
  • Gallery office (giữa trái)
  • Exhibition room 4 (dưới trái)
Hiện tại:
  • Café và exhibition room 4 bị loại bỏ
  • Thay bằng:
  • Temporary exhibition
  • Children’s area
  • Gallery office vẫn giữ nhưng thu nhỏ và dời lên phía trên gần cầu thang
  • Thêm vending machine gần khu này

→ Ý: chuyển từ không gian phụ trợ → không gian trải nghiệm

BODY 3 – Khu trung tâm & lối vào
2005:
  • Receptionist ở trung tâm
  • Entrance hall phía dưới
  • Chỉ có một lối vào đơn giản
Hiện tại:
  • Receptionist gần như giữ nguyên vị trí
  • Entrance hall vẫn giữ
  • Thêm:
  • Ramp for wheelchair ở lối vào

→ tăng khả năng tiếp cận

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be removed → bị loại bỏ
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be introduced → được thêm vào
  • be relocated → được di dời
  • be upgraded → được nâng cấp
  • accessibility → khả năng tiếp cận

The maps compare the layout of an art gallery in 2005 and at present.

Overall, the gallery has been modernised with improved visitor facilities, including a lift, children’s area, and temporary exhibition space, while the number of exhibition rooms has been reduced and reorganised.

In 2005, the gallery had four exhibition rooms, with rooms 1–3 located on the right and room 4 on the left. A café occupied the northwest corner, and the gallery office was situated below it. The centre contained a reception area and entrance hall, with stairs positioned at the top.

At present, the café has been removed and replaced by a gallery office and a vending machine. A lift has been added next to the stairs, and exhibition rooms have been consolidated into three larger spaces on the right. On the left, a temporary exhibition area and a children’s zone have been introduced. The entrance hall and reception remain in the centre, and a ramp has been added to improve accessibility.

title: Map: The diagrams give information about changes in a student accommodation
prompt: The diagrams give information about changes in a student accommodation. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Student accommodation (2010 vs now)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi trong khu nhà ở sinh viên từ năm 2010 đến hiện tại.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • student accommodation – 2010 – now

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • changes → developments / alterations

Ví dụ:

The diagrams illustrate how a student accommodation has changed from 2010 to the present day.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Khu nhà được tái cấu trúc để tăng sức chứa
  • Số lượng phòng ngủ tăng đáng kể
  • Không gian chung (living room) bị loại bỏ
  • Thêm tiện ích mới như en-suite room và bãi đỗ xe

→ tổng thể trở nên thực dụng và tối ưu hóa diện tích hơn

4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Số lượng phòng & khu trung tâm
2010:
  • Có:
  • 3 student bedrooms
  • 1 living room (trung tâm phía trên)
  • 1 bathroom (giữa)
Hiện tại:
  • Living room bị loại bỏ
  • Thay bằng 2 student bedrooms mới

→ tổng số phòng ngủ tăng lên

  • Bathroom vẫn giữ nguyên vị trí

→ Ý: ưu tiên chỗ ở hơn không gian sinh hoạt chung

BODY 2 – Khu bên phải
2010:
  • Có:
  • Kitchen (giữa phải)
  • 1 student bedroom (dưới phải)
Hiện tại:
  • Kitchen giữ nguyên vị trí
  • Thêm en-suite room ở phía dưới gần hành lang
  • Student bedroom vẫn tồn tại

→ Ý: nâng cấp tiện nghi cá nhân

BODY 3 – Khu bên trái & bên ngoài
2010:
  • 1 student bedroom (trái dưới)
  • Gardens xung quanh
  • Không có bãi đỗ xe
Hiện tại:
  • Bedroom bên trái vẫn giữ
  • Gardens phía dưới bị thay thế bởi car parking
  • Garden phía trên vẫn giữ

→ Ý: giảm không gian xanh để tăng tiện ích

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be converted into → được chuyển thành
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be removed → bị loại bỏ
  • be added / introduced → được thêm vào
  • be retained → được giữ nguyên
  • capacity → sức chứa

The diagrams illustrate how a student accommodation building has changed between 2010 and the present.

Overall, the layout has been modified to accommodate more students, with additional bedrooms and new facilities such as an en-suite room and car parking, while the living room has been removed.

In 2010, the accommodation consisted of three student bedrooms, a living room, a kitchen, and a bathroom arranged around a central hallway. The living room was located in the northwest, while the kitchen was on the eastern side. Gardens surrounded the building.

At present, the number of student bedrooms has increased to five, mainly by converting the former living room and reconfiguring internal space. The kitchen remains in the same position, and the bathroom is still centrally located, although an en-suite room has been added in the southeast corner. In addition, a car park has replaced part of the garden area to the south, while the main entrance and hallway remain unchanged.

title: Map: The maps below show the future plan about the Biology School in a particular unive...
prompt: The maps below show the future plan about the Biology School in a particular university. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

University campus (now vs future)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy những thay đổi được đề xuất cho một khuôn viên trường đại học trong tương lai so với hiện tại.

→ Dạng: map – future plan

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

  • university campus – now – future

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / outlines
  • changes → developments / proposed changes

Ví dụ:

The diagrams illustrate the current layout of a university campus and the proposed changes in the future.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Khuôn viên sẽ được mở rộng và hiện đại hóa đáng kể
  • Thêm nhiều cơ sở mới như lab, office, music centre
  • Số lượng teaching buildings và lecture rooms tăng lên
  • Xuất hiện bãi đỗ xe lớn cho nhân viên

→ tổng thể trở nên đa chức năng và phục vụ học thuật tốt hơn

4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu trung tâm & bên phải
Hiện tại:
  • Central library nằm ở trung tâm
  • Bên phải có:
  • Drama centre
  • Lecture room
  • IT centre
  • Café
Tương lai:
  • Central library giữ nguyên vị trí
  • Drama centre được chuyển thành music centre
  • Lecture rooms tăng số lượng (từ 1 → 2\)
  • IT centre vẫn giữ nhưng dịch chuyển nhẹ xuống dưới

→ Ý: nâng cấp và đa dạng hóa chức năng học tập

BODY 2 – Khu phía trên
Hiện tại:
  • Chủ yếu là cây xanh
  • Indoor sports hall ở góc trên bên phải
Tương lai:
  • Một phần cây xanh bị thay thế bởi:
  • Lab
  • Office
  • Indoor sports hall giữ nguyên

→ Ý: chuyển đổi không gian tự nhiên → cơ sở học thuật

BODY 3 – Khu phía dưới & bên trái
Hiện tại:
  • Có:
  • Teaching building (1)
  • IT centre
  • Một số cây xanh
Tương lai:
  • Teaching buildings tăng lên (2 tòa)
  • Xuất hiện car park for staff lớn ở bên trái
  • Một số cây xanh bị loại bỏ để nhường chỗ

→ Ý: mở rộng cơ sở vật chất và hạ tầng

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be converted into → được chuyển thành
  • be replaced by → được thay thế bởi
  • be expanded → được mở rộng
  • be constructed → được xây dựng
  • be relocated → được di chuyển
  • facility → cơ sở vật chất

The maps compare the current layout of a university campus with its proposed future development.

Overall, the site will become more academic-focused, with new facilities such as a lab and additional lecture rooms, while recreational and green areas will be reduced and a staff car park will be added.

At present, the central library is located in the middle of the campus, surrounded by trees. To the east is an indoor sports hall, while the drama centre is situated just southeast of the library. In the southern area, there are a teaching building, a café, a lecture room, and an IT centre.

In the future plan, several changes are proposed. The drama centre will be replaced by a music centre, and a new laboratory and office will be constructed in the northern area, reducing the number of trees. Two lecture rooms will replace the single existing one, and an additional teaching building will be added. A staff car park will be introduced in the southwest, replacing green space, while the café and IT centre will remain in similar positions.

title: Map: The plans below show a harbour in 2000 and how it looks today
prompt: The plans below show a harbour in 2000 and how it looks today. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guideHtml:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Porth Harbour (2000 vs today)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi của một cảng biển từ năm 2000 đến hiện tại.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:
